displayed in the instrument panel accompanied by the value of the programmed speed ef speed limiter xo cruise control eco driving familiarisation eco driving eco driving is range of everyday practice that allow the motorist to optimise their fuel consumption and co emission optimise the use of your gearbox with manual gearbox move off gently and change up without waiting during acceleration change up early the gear shift indicator invite you engage the most suitable gear as soon as the indication is displayed in the instrument panel follow it straight away with an electronic gearbox do not press the accelerator pedal heavily or suddenly drive smoothly maintain safe distance between vehicle use engine braking rather than the brake pedal and press the accelerator progressively these practice contribute toward reduction in fuel consumption and co emission and also help reduce the background traffic noise if your vehicle has cruise control make use of the system at speed above mph km when the traffic is flowing well control the use of your electrical equipment before moving off if the passenger compartment is too warm ventilate it by opening the window and air vent before using the air conditioning above mph km close the window and leave the air vent open remember to make use of equipment that can help keep the temperature in the passenger compartment down sunroof and window blind to save energy do not set the air conditioning at too low temperature as soon as the desired temperature has been reached limit the use of the air conditioning except if regulation is automatic switch off the demisting and defrosting control as soon as you consider possible switch off the heated seat as soon as possible switch off the headlamp and foglamp when the level of light doe not require their use avoid running the engine before moving off particularly in winter your vehicle will warm up much faster while driving as passenger if you avoid connecting your multimedia device film music video game you will contribute toward limiting the consumption of electrical energy and so of fuel disconnect your portable device before leaving the vehicle limit the cause of excess consumption spread load throughout the vehicle place the heaviest item in the bottom of the boot as close as possible to the rear seat limit the load carried in the vehicle and reduce wind resistance roof bar roof rack bicycle carrier trailer use roof box in preference remove roof bar and roof rack after use at the end of winter remove snow tyre and refit your summer tyre familiarisation observe the recommendation on maintenance check the tyre pressure regularly when cold referring to the label in the door aperture driver side carry out this check in particular before long journey at each change of season after along period out of use don forget the spare wheel and the tyre on any trailer or caravan have your vehicle serviced regularly engine oil oil filter air filter passenger compartment filter and observe the schedule of operation recommended in the maintenance and warranty guide when refuelling do not continue after the third cut off of the nozzle to avoid any overflow engagement recommendation must not be considered compulsory in fact the configuration of the road the amount of traffic and safety remain determining factor when choosing the best gear therefore the driver remain responsible for deciding whether or not to follow the advice given by the system this system cannot be deactivated the system adapt its gear change recommendation according to the driving condition slope load and the demand of the driver power acceleration braking the system never suggest engaging first gear engaging reverse gear engaging lower gear example you are in third gear you press the accelerator pedal the system may suggest that you engage higher gear the information appear in the instrument panel in the form of an arrow accompanied by the recommended gear gear audible assistance the proximity information is given by an intermittent audible signal the frequency of which increase as the vehicle approach the obstacle the sound emitted by the speaker right or left indicate the side on which the obstacle is located when the distance between the vehicle and the obstacle become less than approximately thirty centimetre the audible signal become continuous driving visual assistance this supplement the audible signal by displaying bar in the screen or the instrument panel which move progressively nearer to the vehicle when the obstacle is near the danger symbol is displayed driving front parking sensor in addition to the rear parking sensor the front parking sensor are triggered when an obstacle is detected in front and the speed of the vehicle is still below mph km the front parking sensor are interrupted if the vehicle stop for more than three second in forward gear if no further obstacle are detected or when the speed of the vehicle exceed mph km the sound emitted by the speaker front or rear indicate whether the obstacle is in front or behind deactivation activation of the front and rear parking sensor the parking sensor can be approaching location lacking visibility tyre under inflation detection system which automatically check the pressure of the tyre while driving the system monitor the pressure on the four tyre once the vehicle is moving it compare the information given by the four wheel speed sensor with reference value which must be reinitialised every time the tyre pressure are adjusted or wheel changed the system trigger an alert as soon as it detect drop in the inflation pressure of one or more tyre the under inflation detection system doe not replace the vigilance or the responsibility of the driver this system doe not avoid the need to check the tyre pressure including the spare wheel every month as well as before long journey driving with under inflated tyre impair road holding extend braking distance and cause premature tyre wear particularly under arduous condition high loading high speed long journey driving with under inflated tyre increase fuel consumption safety the inflation pressure defined for your vehicle can be found on the tyre pressure label see the identification marking section tyre pressure should be checked when the tyre are cold vehicle stopped for hour or after journey of less than mile km at moderate speed otherwise when hot add bar to the pressure shown on the label safety under inflation alert this is given by fixed illumination of ifyou have compressor such as the one this warning lamp accompanied by in the temporary puncture repair kit check an audible signal and the display of the pressure of the four tyre when cold message if it is not possible to carry out this check straight away drive carefully at reduced ze reduce speed immediately avoid speed excessive steering movement and sudden or braking inthe event of puncture use the stop the vehicle as soon as it is safe to do temporary puncture repair kit or the spare so wheel depending on equipment the loss of pressure detected doe not alway cause visible bulging of the tyre do not satisfy yourself with just visual the alert is maintained until the system check is reinitialised reinitialisation it is necessary to the system every time one or more tyre pressure is adjusted and after changing one or more wheel label attached to the middle pillar driver side give reminder of this before reinitialising the system ensure that the pressure of the four tyre are correct for the use of the vehicle and in line with the recommendation on the tyre pressure label the under inflation detection system doe not give warning if pressure is incorrect at the time of reinitialisation reinitialisation of the system is done in the the touch screen tablet with the ignition on and the vehicle stationary inthe secondary page press under inflation initialisation select the driving assistance menu confirm the instruction by pressing yes an audible signal and message confirm the reinitialisation the new pressure setting registered are considered to be reference value by the system snow chain the system doe not have to be reinitialised after fitting or removing snow chain the under inflation alert can only be considered reliable if reinitialisation of the system is done with the the four tyre pressure correctly set safety operating fault service the flashing and then fixed illumination of the under inflation warning lamp accompanied by illumination of the service warning lamp indicate fault with the system in this case under inflation monitoring of the tyre is no longer assured have the system checked by citroen dealer or qualified workshop before doing anything to the system the pressure of the four tyre must be checked and the system reinitialised safety electronic stability control esc electronic stability control esc incorporating the following system the anti lock braking system abs and the electronic brake force distribution ebfd the emergency braking assistance the anti slip regulation asr or traction control the dynamic stability control dsc definition anti lock braking system abs and electronic brake force distribution ebfd this system improve the stability and manoeuvrability of your vehicle when braking and provide improved control in corner in particular on poor or slippery road surface the abs prevent wheel lock in the event of emergency braking the electronic brake force distribution system manage the braking pressure wheel by wheel emergency braking assistance in an emergency this system enable you to reach the optimum braking pressure more quickly and therefore reduce the stopping distance it is triggered according to the speed at which the brake pedal is pressed this is felt by reduction in the resistance of the pedal and an increase in the effectiveness of the braking anti slip regulation asr the asr system also known as traction control optimise traction in order to limit wheel slip by acting on the brake of the driving wheel and on the engine it also improve the directional stability of the vehicle on acceleration dynamic stability control dsc if there is difference between the path followed by the vehicle and that required by the driver the dsc monitor each wheel and automatically act on the brake of one or more wheel and on the engine to return the vehicle to the required path within the limit of the law of physic operation anti lock braking system abs and electronic brake force distribution ebfd when this warning lamp come on accompanied by an audible signal and message it indicate that there is fault with the abs which could cause loss of control of the vehicle when braking when this warning lamp come on coupled with the stop warning lamp accompanied by an audible signal and message it indicate that there is fault with the electronic brake force distribution ebfd which could cause loss of control of the vehicle when braking you must stop as soon as it is safe to do so in both case contact citroen dealer or qualified workshop be when changing wheel tyre and rim make sure that these are approved for your vehicle normal operation of the abs may make itself felt by slight vibration of the brake pedal in emergency braking press very firmly without releasing the pressure safety safety md ay dynamic stability control dsc and anti slip regulation asr activation these system are activated automatically each time the vehicle is started as soon as they detect problem of grip or trajectory these system act on the operation if the engine and brake ee this is indicated by flashing of this warning lamp in the instrument panel deactivation in exceptional condition starting vehicle which is bogged down stuck in snow on soft ground it may be advisable to deactivate the dsc system so that the wheel can move freely and regain grip but it is recommended that the system be reactivated as soon as possible press the button ee off this warning lamp and the indicator ch lamp in the button come on the ee dsc system no longer act on the operation of the engine reactivation the system is reactivated automatically each time the ignition is switched back on or from mph km press the button again to reactivate it manually operating fault if this warning lamp come on ch accompanied by an audible signal ce anda message this indicate fault with the system contact citroen dealer or qualified workshop to have the system checked the dsc system offer exceptional safety in normal driving but this should not encourage the driver to take extra risk or drive at high speed the correct operation of the system depend on observation of the manufacturer recommendation regarding the wheel tyre and rim the braking component the electronic component and the assembly and repair procedure used by citroen dealer after an impact have the system checked by citroen dealer or qualified workshop seat belt front seat belt the front seat belt are fitted with pretensioning and force limiting system this system improve safety in the front seat in the event of front or side impact depending on the severity of the impact the pretensioning system instantly tighten the seat belt against the body of the occupant the pretensioning seat belt are active when the ignition is on the force limiter reduce the pressure of the seat belt on the chest of the occupant so improving their protection rear seat belt lia pan wa if st at fe se the rear seat are each fitted with three point seat belt safety fastening pull the strap then insert the tongue in the buckle check that the seat belt is fastened correctly by pulling the strap unfastening press the red button on the buckle guide the seat belt as it reel in safety seat belt not fastened unfastened warning lamp dawn front seat belt not fastened unfastened warning lamp in the instrument panel front left seat belt warning lamp front right seat belt warning lamp rear right seat belt warning lamp rear centre seat belt warning lamp rear left seat belt warning lamp front seat belt warning lamp on switching on the ignition warning lamp come on in the instrument pitis ce panel and the corresponding warning lamp and come on in red in the passenger seat belt and front airbag warning lamp display if the driver gei seat belt or front passenger seat belt ge en is not fastened or is unfastened rear seat belt warning lamp from approximately mph the corresponding warning lamp km the warning lamp to come on in red in the flash for two minute accompanied seat belt and front airbag warning by an audible signal once these lamp display if one or more rear two minute have elapsed the passenger unfasten their seat belt warning lamp remain on until the driver or front passenger fasten their seat belt the driver must ensure that passenger use the seat belt correctly and that they are all restrained securely before setting off wherever you are seated in the vehicle alway fasten your seat belt even for short journey do not interchange the seat belt buckle as they will not fulfil their role fully the seat belt are fitted with an inertia reel permitting automatic adjustment of the length of the strap to your size the seat belt is stowed automatically when not in use before and after use ensure that the seat belt is reeled in correctly the lower part of the strap must be positioned as low as possible on the pelvis the upper part must be positioned in the hollow of the shoulder the inertia reel are fitted with an automatic locking device which come into operation in the event of collision emergency braking or if the vehicle roll over you can release the device by pulling firmly on the strap and releasing it so that it reel in in order to be effective seat belt must be tightened as close to the body as possible be pulled in front of you with smooth movement checking that it doe not twist be used to restrain only one person not bear any trace of cut or fraying not be converted or modified to avoid affecting its performance in accordance with current safety regulation for all repair on your vehicle go to qualified workshop with the skill and equipment needed which citroen dealer is able to provide have your seat belt checked regularly by citroen dealer or qualified workshop particularly if the strap show sign of damage clean the seat belt strap with soapy water or textile cleaning product sold by citroen dealer after folding or moving seat or rear bench seat ensure that the seat belt is positioned and reeled in correctly safety recommendation for child use suitable child seat if the passenger is less than year old or shorter than one and half metre never use the same seat belt to secure more than one person never allow child to travel on your lap in the event of an impact depending on the nature and seriousness of the impact the pretensioning device may be deployed before and independently of the airbag deployment of the pretensioner is accompanied by slight discharge of harmless smoke and noise due to the activation of the pyrotechnic cartridge incorporated in the system in all case the airbag warning lamp come on following an impact have the seat belt system checked and if necessary replaced by citroen dealer or qualified workshop safety ee airbag system designed to maximise the safety of the occupant with the exception of the rear centre passenger in the event of violent collision see the airbag supplement the action of the force limiting seat belt with the exception of the centre rear passenger if collision occur the electronic detector record and analyse the front and side impact sustained in the impact detection zone inthe case of serious impact the airbag are deployed instantly and contribute toward better protection of the occupant of the vehicle with the exception of the impact detection zone rear centre passenger immediately after peployment sc ee ce the impact the airbag deflate rapidly so front impact zone accompanied by as ight emission side impact zone of smoke and noise due to the that they do not hinder visibility or the exit of the occupant inthe case of minor or rear impact or in certain roll over condition the airbag will not be deployed the seat belt alone contribute toward ensuring your protection in these situation activation of the pyrotechnic cartridge incorporated in the system this smoke is not harmful but sensitive individual may experience slight irritation the noise of the detonation may result in slight loss of hearing for short time the airbag do not operate when the ignition is switched off this equipment will only deploy once if second impact occur during the same or subsequent accident the airbag will not be deployed again front airbag system which protect the driver and front passenger in the event of serious front impact in order to limit the risk of injury to the head and thorax the driver airbag is fitted in the centre of the steering wheel the front passenger airbag is fitted in the roof to allow more space in the glove box deployment the airbag are deployed except the passenger front airbag if it is deactivated in the event of serious front impact to all or part of the front impact zone in the longitudinal centreline of the vehicle on horizontal plane and directed from the front to the rear of the vehicle the front airbag inflate between the thorax and head of the front occupant of the vehicle and the steering wheel driver side and the dashboard passenger side to cushion their forward movement deactivating the passenger front airbag only the passenger front airbag can be deactivated with the ignition off insert the key in the passenger airbag deactivation switch turn it to the off position remove the key keeping the switch in the new position when the ignition is on this warning lamp come in the seat belt display screen it stay on while the airbag is deactivated gs el safety to assure the safety of your child the passenger front airbag must be deactivated when you install rearward facing child seat on the front passenger seat otherwise the child would risk being seriously injured or killed if the airbag were deployed reactivating the passenger front airbag when you remove rearward facing child seat with the ignition off turn the switch to the on position to reactivate the airbag and so assure the safety of your front passenger in the event of an impact when the ignition is switched on this warning lamp come on in the seat belt display screen passenger in the event of serious side impact in order to limit the risk of injury to the chest between the hip and the shoulder each lateral airbag is fitted in the seat backrest frame door side front impact zone side impact zone deployment the lateral airbag are deployed unilaterally in the event of serious side impact applied to all or part of the side impact zone perpendicular to the longitudinal centreline of the vehicle on horizontal plane and directed from the outside toward the inside of the vehicle the lateral airbag inflate between the hip and shoulder of the front occupant of the vehicle and the corresponding door trim panel safety safety curtain airbag system which protect the driver and passenger with the exception of the rear centre passenger in the event of serious side impact in order to limit the risk of injury to the side of the head each curtain airbag is built into the pillar and the upper passenger compartment area deployment the curtain airbag is deployed at the same time as the corresponding lateral airbag in the event of serious side impact applied to all or part of the side impact zone perpendicular to the longitudinal centreline of the vehicle on horizontal plane and directed from the outside toward the inside of the vehicle the curtain airbag inflate between the front or rear occupant of the vehicle and the window operating fault if this warning lamp come on in the instrument panel accompanied by an audible signal and message contact citroen dealer or qualified workshop to have the system checked the airbag may no longer be deployed in the event of serious impact in the event of minor impact or bump on the side of the vehicle or if the vehicle roll over the airbag may not be deployed in the event of rear or front collision none of the lateral airbag is deployed safety for the airbag to be fully effective observe the following safety rule sit in normal upright position wear correctly adjusted seat belt do not leave anything between the occupant and the airbag child pet object this could hamper the operation of the airbag or injure the occupant after an accident or if the vehicle has been stolen or broken into have the airbag system checked all work on the airbag system must be carried out by citroen dealer ora qualified workshop even if all of the precaution mentioned are observed risk of injury or of minor burn to the head chest or arm when an airbag is deployed cannot be ruled out the bag inflate almost instantly within few millisecond then deflate within the same time discharging the hot gas via opening provided for this purpose front airbag do not drive holding the steering wheel by its spoke or resting your hand on the centre part of the wheel passenger must not place their foot on the dashboard do not smoke as deployment of the airbag can cause burn or the risk of injury from cigarette or pipe never remove or pierce the steering wheel or hit it violently do not fit or attach anything to the steering wheel or dashboard this could cause injury with deployment of the airbag lateral airbag use only approved cover on the seat compatible with the deployment the lateral airbag for information on the range of seat cover suitable for your vehicle you can contact citroen dealer refer to the accessory section do not fix or attach anything to the seat back clothing this could cause injury to the chest or arm if the lateral airbag is deployed do not sit with the upper part of the body any nearer to the door than necessary curtain airbag do not fix or attach anything to the roof this could cause injury to the head if the curtain airbag is deployed if fitted on your vehicle do not remove the grab handle installed on the roof they play part in securing the curtain airbag child safety recommendation in accordance with european regulation all child under the age of or less than one and half metre tall must travel in approved child seat suited to their weight on seat fitted with seat belt or isofix mounting statistically the safest seat in your vehicle for carrying child are the rear seat achild weighing less than kg must travel in the rearward facing position both in the front and in the rear citroen recommend that child should travel on the rear seat of your vehicle rearward facing up to the age of forward facing over the age of the rule for carrying child are specific to each country refer to the legislation in force in your country child seat at the rear rearward facing when rearward facing child seat is installed on rear passenger seat move the vehicle front seat hand rear door to the right on the right hand rear door fuel tank capacity of the tank petrol approximately litre diesel approximately or litre depending on version low fuel level wm when the low fuel level is reached this warning lamp in the instrument panel come on accompanied by an audible warning message and the last bar of the fuel gauge flash in red when it first come on about litre of fuel remain in the tank until sufficient fuel has been added this warning lamp appear every time the ignition is switched on accompanied by an audible warning and message this audible warning and message are repeated with increasing frequency as the fuel level drop toward refuel as soon as possible to avoid running out if you run out of fuel diesel refer also to the section running out of fuel diesel refuelling label on the inside of the fuel filler flap remind you of the type of fuel to use depending on your engine type addition of fuel must be of at least litre to be registered by the fuel gauge opening the filler cap may result in an inrush of air this is entirely normal and result from the sealing of the fuel system with stop start never refuel with the system in stop mode you must switch off the ignition with the key practical information to refuel in complete safety ls es you must switch off the engine open the filler flap take care to select the pump that deliver the correct type of fuel for your vehicle introduce the key into the cap and turn it to the left practical information remove the cap and place it on the support on the inner face of the flap introduce the filler nozzle and push it in as far as possible before starting to refuel risk of blowback maintain this position throughout the refuelling operation once you have finished refuelling ze refit the filler cap turn the key to the right then remove it from the cap ze push the fuel flap to close it if you are filling your tank to the top do not persist after the cut off this could cause malfunction the petrol or diesel engine of your vehicle is fitted with catalytic converter device which help to reduce the level of harmful emission in the exhaust gase for petrol engine you must use unleaded fuel the filler neck is narrower allowing only unleaded petrol nozzle to be introduced if you have put in the wrong fuel for your vehicle you must have the fuel tank drained and filled with the correct fuel before starting the engine fuel cut off your vehicle is equipped with safety device which cut off the fuel supply in the event of collision mistuel prevention diesel mechanical device which prevent filling the tank of diesel vehicle with petrol it avoid the risk of engine damage that can result from filling with the wrong fuel operation when petrol filler nozzle is introduced into the fuel filler neck of your diesel vehicle it come into contact with the flap the system remain closed and prevent filling do not persist but introduce diesel type filler nozzle depending on country of sale it remain possible to use fuel can to fill the tank in order to ensure good flow of fuel do not place the nozzle of the fuel can in direct contact with the flap of the misfuel prevention device and pour slowly practical information travelling abroad as diesel fuel pump nozzle may be different in other country the presence of the misfuel prevention device may make refuelling impossible before travelling abroad we recommend that you check with the citro dealer network whether your vehicle is suitable for the fuel pump in the country in which you want to travel practical information quality of the fuel used for petrol engine the petrol engine are perfectly compatible with type petrol biofuel containing ethanol conforming to european standard en and en type fuel containing up to ethanol are reserved exclusively for vehicle marketed for the use of this type of fuel bioflex vehicle the quality of the ethanol must comply with european standard en for brazil only special vehicle are marketed to run on fuel containing up to ethanol type quality of the fuel used for diesel engine the diesel engine are perfectly compatible with biofuel which conform to current and future european standard diesel fuel which comply with standard en mixed with biofuel which comply with standard en available at the pump containing up to fatty acid methyl ester the biofuel can be used in certain diesel engine however this use is subject to strict application of the special servicing condition contact citroen dealer or qualified workshop the use of any other type of bio fuel vegetable or animal oil pure or diluted domestic fuel is strictly prohibited risk of damage to the engine and fuel system running out of fuel diesel on vehicle fitted with hdi engine the fuel system must be primed if you run out of fuel if the engine doe not start first time don keep trying but start the procedure again from the beginning ne engine fill the fuel tank with at least five litre of diesel open the bonnet if necessary unclip the styling cover for access to the priming pump squeeze and release the priming pump repeatedly until resistance is felt there may be resistance at the first press operate the starter until the engine start if the engine doe not start at the first attempt wait around second before trying again if the engine doe not start after few attempt operate the priming pump again then start the engine clip the styling cover back in place close the bonnet practical information blue hdi engine fill the fuel tank with at least five litre of diesel switch on the ignition without starting the engine wait around second and switch off the ignition ze repeat the operation time operate the starter to run the engine practical information adblue additive and scr system for blue hdi diesel engine to assure respect for the environment and conformity with the new euro emission standard without adversely affecting the performance or fuel consumption of diesel engine citroen has taken the decision to equip its vehicle with an effective system that associate scr selective catalytic reduction with particle emission filter pef for the treatment of exhaust gase presentation of the scr system using an additive called adblue containing urea catalytic converter turn up to of nitrous oxide nox into nitrogen and water which are harmless to health and the environment the adblue additive is held in special tank located under the boot at the rear of the vehicle it has capacity of litre this provide driving range of about mile km after which an alert is triggered warning you when the reserve remaining is enough for just mile km during each scheduled service of your vehicle by citroen dealer or qualified workshop the adblue additive tank is refilled in order to allow normal operation of the scr system if the estimated mileage between two service is greater than mile km we recommend that you go to citroen dealer or qualified workshop to have the necessary top up carried out once the adblue tank is empty system required by regulation prevent starting of the engine if the scr is faulty the level of emission from your vehicle will no longer meet the euro standard you vehicle become polluting in the event of confirmed fault with the scr system you must go to citroen dealer or qualified workshop as soon as possible after running distance of mile km system will be triggered automatically to prevent engine starting range indicator when switching on the ignition an indicator give an estimate of the distance that can be travelled with your vehicle before engine starting is automatically prevented once the vehicle start using the reserve of adblue or after fault is detected with the scr system in the event of simultaneous system fault and low adblue level the shortest range is the one displayed practical information in the event of the risk of non starting related to lack of adblue the engine start prevention system required by regulation it will occur after several mile of running topping up the adblue additive filling the adblue tank is an operation included in every routine service on your vehicle by citroen dealer or qualified workshop nevertheless given the capacity of the tank it may be necessary to top up the additive between service more particularly if an alert warning lamp and message signal the requirement you can got to citroen dealer or qualified workshop if you envisage topping up yourself please read the following warning carefully precaution in use the adblue additive is an urea based solution this liquid is non flammable colourless and odourless kept in cool area in the event of contact with the skin wash the affected area with soap and water in the event of contact with the eye wash irrigate the eye with large amount of water or with an eye wash solution for at least minute if burning sensation or irritation persist get medical attention if swallowed immediately wash out the mouth with clean water and then drink plenty of water in certain condition high ambient temperature for example the risk of release of ammonia cannot be excluded do not inhale the product ammonia vapour has en irritant effect on mucous membrane eye nose and throat keep adblue out of the reach of child in its original bottle never transfer adblue to another container it would loose its purity practical information use only adblue additive that meet the iso standard never dilute the additive with water never pour the additive into the diesel fuel tank adblue the supply in non drip bottle simplify toping up you can obtain litre half us gallon bottle from citro dealer or qualified workshop never top up from an adblue dispenser reserved for heavy good vehicle practical information recommendation doing any work access to the battery immobilise the vehicle apply the parking brake put the gearbox in see neutral then switch off the ignition check that all electrical equipment is switched off the presence of this label in particular with the stop start system indicate the use of lead acid battery with special technology and specification sf the involvement of citro dealer ps or qualified workshop is essential when replacing or disconnecting the the battery is located under the bonnet battery for access to the terminal release the bonnet using the interior lever then the exterior safety catch ze raise the bonnet and secure it with its stay after refitting the battery the stop lift the plastic cover for access to the start system will only be active after terminal continuous period of immobilisation of the vehicle period which depend on the climatic condition and the state of charge of the battery up to about hour practical information disconnecting the cable reconnecting the cable ze raise the locking tab fully to release the position the open clamp of the cable on cable terminal clamp the positive post of the battery ze press vertically on the clamp to position it correctly on the battery ze lock the clamp by spreading the positioning lug and then lowering the tab do not apply excessive force on the tab as locking will not be possible if the clamp is not positioned correctly start the procedure again starting using another battery when your vehicle battery is discharged the engine can be started using slave battery external or on another vehicle and jump lead cable first check that the slave battery has nominal voltage of and capacity at least equal to that of the discharged battery do not try to start the engine by connecting battery charger do not disconnect the terminal when the engine is running remove the plastic cover from the terminal if your vehicle has one ze connect the red cable to the positive terminal of the flat battery then to the positive terminal of the slave battery connect one end of the green or black cable to the negative terminal of the slave battery or earth point on the other vehicle connect the other end of the green or black cable to the earth point on the broken down vehicle or on the engine mounting start the engine of the vehicle with the good battery and leave it running for few minute operate the starter on the broken down vehicle and let the engine run if the engine doe not start straight away switch off the ignition and wait few moment before trying again wait until the engine return to idle then disconnect the jump lead cable in the reverse order refit the plastic cover to the terminal if your vehicle has one practical information before disconnecting the battery wait minute after switching off the ignition before disconnecting the battery close the window and the door before disconnecting the battery following reconnection of the battery following reconnection of the battery switch on the ignition and wait minute before starting to permit initialisation of the electronic system however if problem remain following this operation contact citroen dealer or qualified workshop referring to the corresponding section you must yourself depending on version the remote control key practical information charging the battery using battery charger with stop start the battery doe not have to be disconnected for charging disconnect the battery from the vehicle follow the instruction for use provided by the manufacturer of the charger connect the battery starting with the negative terminal check that the terminal and connector are clean if they are covered with sulphate whitish or greenish deposit remove them and clean them the battery contain harmful substance such as sulphuric acid and lead they must be disposed of in accordance with regulation and must not in any circumstance be discarded with household waste take used remote control battery and vehicle battery to special collection point it is advisable to disconnect the battery if the vehicle is to be left unused for more than one month protect your eye and face before handling the battery all operation on the battery must be carried out in well ventilated area and away from naked flame and source of spark so as to avoid the risk of explosion or fire do not try to charge frozen battery the battery must first be thawed out to avoid the risk of explosion if the battery has been frozen before charging have it checked by citro dealer or qualified workshop who will check that the internal component have not been damaged and the casing is not cracked which could cause leak of toxic and corrosive acid do not reverse the polarity and use only charger do not disconnect the terminal while the engine is running do not charge the battery without disconnecting the terminal first wash your hand afterward do not push the vehicle to start the engine if the vehicle is fitted with the electronic gearbox load reduction mode system which manage the use of certain function according to the level of charge remaining in the battery when the vehicle is being driven the load reduction function temporarily deactivate certain function such as the air conditioning the heated rear screen the deactivated function are reactivated automatically as soon as condition permit practical information energy economy mode system which manage the duration of use of certain function to conserve sufficient level of charge in the battery after the engine has stopped you can still use function such as the audio and telematic system wiper dipped beam headlamp courtesy lamp etc for maximum combined duration of about forty minute switching to economy mode message then appear in the instrument panel screen indicating that the vehicle has switched to economy mode and the active function are put on standby if telephone call is being made at this time it will be maintained for around minute with the bluetooth hand free system of your audio system exiting economy mode these function are reactivated automatically next time the vehicle is driven in order to restore the use of these function immediately start the engine and let it run for less than ten minute to use the equipment for approximately five minute for more than ten minute to use the equipment for up to approximately thirty minute let the engine run for the duration specified to ensure that the battery charge is sufficient do not repeatedly and continuously restart the engine in order to charge the battery flat battery prevent the engine from starting refer to the battery section practical information oo changing wiper blade before removing front fitting wiper blade put the corresponding new wiper blade in place and vehicle is primarily designed for transporting people and luggage but it may also be used for towing trailer we recommend the use of genuine citroen towbar and their harness that have been tested and approved from the design stage of your vehicle and that the fitting of the towbar is entrusted to citroen dealer or qualified workshop lf the towbar is not fitted by citroen dealer it is imperative that it is fitted in accordance with the vehicle manufacturer instruction practical information driving with trailer place greater demand on the towing vehicle and the driver must take particular care practical information driving advice distribution of load distribute the load in the trailer so that the heaviest item are as close as possible to the axle and the nose weight approach the maximum permitted without exceeding it air density decrease with altitude thus reducing engine performance above metre the maximum towed load must be reduced by for every metre of altitude refer to the technical data section for detail of the weight and towed load which apply to your vehicle side wind ze take into account the increased sensitivity to side wind cooling towing trailer on slope increase the temperature of the coolant as the fan is electrically controlled its cooling capacity is not dependent on the engine speed to lower the engine speed reduce your speed the maximum towed load on long incline depend on the gradient and the ambient temperature in all case keep check on the coolant temperature ifthe warning lamp and the stop warning lamp come on stop the vehicle and switch off the engine as soon as possible wps when not towing trailer remove the quickly detachable tow ball before driving braking towing trailer increase the braking distance to avoid overheating of the brake on long mountain type of descent the use of engine braking is recommended tyre check the tyre pressure of the towing vehicle and of the trailer observing the recommended pressure lighting ze check the electrical lighting and signalling on the trailer the rear parking sensor will be deactivated automatically if genuine citro towbar is used roof bar for safety reason and to avoid damaging the roof it is essential to use transverse roof bar that are approved for your vehicle observe the fitting instruction and the condition of use given in the information provided with the roof bar practical information version without longitudinal bar version with longitudinal bar nn you must fit the transverse bar to the longitudinal bar at the fixing point indicated by engraved marking on the longitudinal bar to install the roof bar you must secure them only to the four anchorage point in the roof frame these point are masked by the vehicle door when closed the roof bar fixing include stud that should be introduced into the aperture at each anchorage point practical information recommendation distribute the load evenly avoiding overloading on one side arrange the heaviest part of the load as close as possible to the roof ze anchor the load securely and fit warning flag if part of it overhang the vehicle drive gently as the vehicle will be more liable to the effect of side wind vehicle stability may be affected ze remove the roof bar as soon as they are no longer needed maximum load distributed over the roof bar for loading height not exceeding cm kg this value is subject to change please check the maximum load stated in the instruction provided with he roof bar if the height exceed cm adapt the speed of the vehicle to the profile of the road to avoid damaging the roof bar and the fixing on the vehicle please refer to national legislation so as to meet the requirement on carrying object that are longer than the vehicle very cold climate screen removable protective not to damage the safety catch unclip the stay from its housing and place it in the support slot to hold the bonnet open closing ls es take the stay out of its support slot clip the stay back into its chonda ryn according to engine checking level check check all of these level regularly in line with the maintenance and warranty guide top them up if necessary unless otherwise indicated if level drop significantly have the corresponding system checked by citroen dealer or qualified workshop take care when working under the bonnet as certain area of the engine may be extremely oil level the check is carried out either when dipstick the ignition is switched on using the oil level indicator in the instrument panel or using the dipstick it will only be correct if the vehicle is on level ground and the engine has been off for more than minute itis normal to top up the oil level between two service or oil change citro recommend that you check the level and top up if necessary every mile kms after topping up the oil the check when switching on the ignition with the oil level indicator in the instrument panel is not valid during the minute after topping up hot risk of burn and the cooling fan could start at any time even with the ignition off there are two mark on the dipstick maximum if the level is above this mark contact citroen dealer or qualified workshop minimum top up the level through the oil filler orifice using the correct grade of oil for your engine the level must never be below this mark engine oil change refer to the maintenance and warranty guide for detail of the interval for this operation in order to maintain the reliability of the engine and emission control system the use of additive in the engine oil is prohibited oil specification the oil must be the correct grade for your engine and conform to the manufacturer recommendation check brake fluid level the brake fluid level should be close to the max mark if it is not check the brake pad wear changing the fluid refer to the maintenance and warranty guide for detail of the interval for this operation fluid specification the brake fluid must conform to the manufacturer recommendation coolant level the coolant level should be close to the max mark but should never exceed it when the engine is warm the temperature of the coolant is regulated by the fan the cooling fan may start after switching off the engine take care with article and clothing that might become caught by the fan blade in addition as the cooling system is pressurised wait at least one hour after switching off the engine before carrying out any work to avoid any risk of scalding unscrew the cap by two turn to allow the pressure to drop when the pressure has dropped remove the cap and top up the level changing the coolant the coolant doe not have to be replaced at routine service fluid specification the coolant must conform to the manufacturer recommendation screenwash fluid level top up the level when necessary service fluid specification for optimum cleaning and to avoid freezing this fluid must not be topped up or replaced with plain water in wintry condition it is recommended that you use an ethyl alcohol or methanol based fluid diesel fuel additive level diesel with particle emission filter the additive reservoir low level is indicated by fixed illumination of this warning lamp accompanied by an audible signal and message that the particle emission filter additive level is too low service topping up the reservoir must be topped up without delay by citroen dealer or qualified workshop check used product avoid prolonged contact of used oil or fluid with the skin most of these fluid are harmful to health or indeed very corrosive do not discard used oil or fluid into sewer or onto the ground take used oil to citroen dealer or qualified workshop france or to an authorised waste disposal site check check unless otherwise indicated check these component in accordance with the maintenance and warranty guide and according to your engine otherwise have them checked by citroen dealer or qualified workshop battery the battery doe not require any maintenance na however check twice as often if necessary clogged passenger compartment filter may have an adverse effect on the performance of the air conditioning system and generate undesirable odour oil filter replace the oil filter each time the engine oil is changed refer to the maintenance and warranty guide for detail of the replacement interval for this component particle emission filter diesel the start of saturation of the service particle filter is indicated by the fixed illumination of this warning lamp accompanied by warning message as soon as the traffic condition permit regenerate the filter by driving ata by burning smell which is perfectly normal following prolonged operation of the vehicle at very low speed or at idle you may in exceptional circumstance notice the emission of water vapour at the exhaust on acceleration this doe not affect the behaviour of the vehicle or the environment manual gearbox the gearbox doe not require any maintenance no oil change refer to the maintenance and the audio equipment will only play audio file with wma aac flac ogg and file extension and with bit rate of between kbp and kbp it also support vbr variable bit rate mode no other type of file can be played wma file must be of the standard wma type the sampling rate supported are and khz it is advisable to restrict file name to character without using of special character to avoid any playing and displaying problem in order to be able to play recorded cdr or cdrw when recording it is preferable to select the iso level or joliet standard if the disc is recorded in another format it may not be played correctly it is recommended that the same recording standard is alway used for an individual disc with as low speed as possible maximum for optimum sound quality in the particular case of multi session cd the joliet standard is recommended the system support usb mass storage device blackberry device or apple player via usb port the adaptor cable not supplied control of the peripheral device is with the audio system control other peripheral not recognised on connection must be connected to the auxiliary socket using jack cable not supplied in order to be read usb memory stick must be formatted fat or the system doe not support two identical device connected at the same time two memory stick two apple player but it is possible to connect one memory stick and one apple player at the same time it is recommended that official apple usb cable are used to ensure correct operation music selecting track to play previous track next track previous folder next folder pause list of usb or unable to enter my post code the pol do not appear the risk area audible warning doe not work the system doe not suggest detour around an incident on the route receive risk area alert which is not on my route answer navigation guidance the guidance criteria may conflict with the current location exclusion of toll road on toll motorway the system only accept post code of up to character the pol have not been selected the audible warning is not active the guidance criteria do not take account of tmc message other than guidance the system announce all risk area positioned in cone located in front of the vehicle it may provide an alert for risk area located on nearby or parallel road solution check the guidance criteria on the navigation menu guidance option define calculation criteria select the pol in the list of pol activate audible warning in the guidance option menu navigation guidance set parameter for risk area select the traffic info function in the list of guidance criteria zoom in on the map to view the exact position of the risk area select on the route to no longer receive alert other than guidance or to reduce the time for the announcement frequently asked question question certain traffic jam along the route are not indicated in real time the altitude is not displayed cannot connect my bluetooth telephone the volume of the telephone connected in bluetooth mode is inaudible answer on starting it is several minute before the system begin to receive the traffic information the filter are too restrictive in certain country only major route motorway are listed for the traffic information on starting the initialisation of the gps may take up to minute to receive more than satellite correctly depending on the geographical environment tunnel or the weather the condition of reception of the gps signal may vary telephone bluetooth the telephone bluetooth function may be switched off or the telephone may not be visible the bluetooth telephone is not compatible with the system the volume depend both on the system and on the telephone the ambient noise level has an influence on the quality of telephone communication solution wait until the traffic information is being received correctly display of the traffic information icon on the map modify the geographic filter setting this phenomenon is normal the system is dependent on the traffic information available wait until the system has started up completely check that there is gps coverage of at least satellite long press on the setup button then select gps coverage this phenomenon is normal the system is dependent on the gps signal reception condition check that your telephone bluetooth function is switched on check in the telephone setting that it is visible to all you can check the compatibility of your telephone at citroen co uk service increase the volume of the audio system to maximum if required and increase the volume of the telephone if necessary reduce the ambient noise level close the window reduce the booster fan speed slow down frequently asked question question some contact are duplicated in the list contact are not shown in alphabetical order the system doe not receive sms text message the cd is ejected automatically or is not played by the player there is long waiting period following the insertion of cd or connection of usb memory stick the cd player sound is poor answer the option for contact are the contact on the sim card the contact on the telephone or both when both are selected some contact may be duplicated some telephone offer display option depending on the setting chosen contact can be transferred in specific order the bluetooth mode doe not permit sending sms text message to the system cd the cd is inserted upside down is unplayable doe not contain any audio data or contain an audio format which the player cannot play the cd has been recorded in format that is not compatible with the player ud the cd is protected by an anti pirating protection system which is not recognised by the audio system when new medium is inserted the system read certain amount of data directory title artist etc this may take from few second to few minute the cd used is scratched or of poor quality the audio equipment setting bass treble ambience are unsuitable solution select display sim card contact or display telephone contact modify the correct quality this phenomenon is normal insert good quality cds and store them in suitable condition set the treble or bass level to without selecting an ambience frequently asked question question some character in the media information are not displayed correctly while playing playing of streaming file doe not start the name of track and the track length are not displayed on the screen when streaming audio the quality of reception of the radio station listened to gradually deteriorate or the stored station do not function no sound mhz is displayed cannot find some radio station in the bluetooth profile doe not allow the transfer of this information radio the vehicle is too far from the transmitter used by the station listened to or there is no transmitter in the geographical area through which the vehicle is travelling the environment hill building tunnel basement car park block reception including in rds mode the aerial is absent or has been damaged for example when going through car wash or into an underground car park the station is not received or its name has changed in the list some radio station send other information in place of their name the title of the song for example the system interpret this information as the name of the station solution use standard character to name track and folder start the playback from the device activate the rds function by mean of the short cut menu to enable the system to check whether there is more powerful transmitter in the geographical area this phenomenon is normal and doe not indicate fault with the audio system have the aerial checked by citroen dealer frequently asked question question in changing the setting of treble and bass the equalizer setting is deselected in changing the equalizer setting treble and bass return to zero when changing the balance setting the distribution setting is deselected when changing an distribution setting the balance setting is deselected there is difference in sound quality between the different audio source radio cd answer setting configuration the selection of an equalizer setting impose the balance setting modifying one without the other is not possible the selection of distribution setting impose the balance setting modifying one without the other is not possible for optimum sound quality the audio setting for volume bass treble equalizer and loudness can be adapted to the different sound source which may result in audible difference when changing source radio cd solution modify the balance or equalizer setting to obtain the desired musical ambience modify the balance or distribution setting to obtain the desired musical ambience check that the audio setting for volume bass treble equalizer loudness are adapted to the source listened to it is advisable to set the audio function bass treble fr re balance le ri balance to the middle position select the none musical ambience and set the loudness correction to the active position in cd mxz on auto line correcting mxz outdoor unit has an auto line correcting function which automatically detect and correct improper wiring or piping improper wiring or piping can be automatically detected by pressing the piping wiring correction switch when improper wiring or piping is detected wiring line are corrected this will be completed in about to minute how to activate this function check that outside temperature is above this function doe not work when outside temperature is not above check that the stop valve of the liquid pipe and gas pipe are open check that the wiring between indoor and outdoor unit is correct if the wiring is not correct this function doe not work turn on the power supply and wait at least minute press the piping wiring correction switch on the electronic control board do not touch energized part press the switch to cancel note indoor unit cannot be operated while this function is activated when this function is activated while indoor unit is operating the operation will be stopped operate indoor unit after the auto line correcting is finished pressing the switch during detection cancel this function be sure to check for closed valve goe out red lighting blinking yellow goe out blinking other indication green lighting blinking completed detected successfully cannot be corrected refer to safety precaution when led flash located behind the service panel indication press the switch for more than second led will show the record of auto correcting for about second as shown in the table below note activate this function to confirm the correct wiring after replacing the electronic control board previous record are deleted when the electronic control board is replaced the record cannot be shown if auto line correcting is not canceled refer to how to activate this function red once time yellow once time green lighting lighting not corrected corrected number of blink wiring line the record of auto line correcting can be remote controller and then after confirm ing the horizontal vane is closed turn off the breaker and or disconnect the power plug be sure to turn off the power supply before removing the front panel the cabinet the top panel and the electronic control board when removing the electrical part be careful to the residual voltage of smoothing capacitor when removing the electronic control board hold the edge of the board with care not to apply stress on the component when connecting or disconnecting the connector hold the housing of the connector do not pull the lead wire mxz mxz mxz mxz failure effective may specification subject to change without notice vsg engine the service manuali uwer worldwide tm for engine produced and later important safety notice appropriate service method and proper repair procedure are essential for the safe reliable operation of all motor vehicle as well as the personal safety of the individual doing the work this shop manual provide general direc tion for accomplishing service and repair work with tested effective technique following them will help assure reliability there are numerous variation in procedure technique tool and part for servicing vehicle as well as in the skill of the individual doing the work this manual cannot possibly anticipate all such variation and provide advice or caution as to each accordingly anyone who depart from the instruction provided in this manual must first establish that he compromise neither his personal safety nor the vehicle integrity by his choice of method tool or part note caution and warning as you read through the procedure you will come across note caution and warning each one is there for specific purpose note give you added information that will help you to complete particular proce dure caution are given to prevent you from making an error that could damage the vehicle warning remind you to be especially careful in those area where carelessness can cause personal injury the following list contain some general warning that you should follow when you work on vehicle alway wear safety glass for eye protection use safety stand whenever procedure require you to be under the vehicle be sure that the ignition switch is alway in the off position unless otherwise required by the procedure set the parking brake when working on the vehicle if you have an automatic transmission set it in park unless instructed otherwise for specific operation if you have manual transmission it should be in reverse engine off or neutral engine on unless instructed otherwise for specific operation place wood block or larger to the front and rear surface of the tire to provide further restraint from inad vertent vehicle movement operate the engine only in well ventilated area to avoid the danger of carbon monoxide keep yourself and your clothing away from moving part when the engine is running especially the fan and belt to prevent serious burn avoid contact with hot metal part such as the radiator exhaust manifold tail pipe catalytic converter and muffler not smoke while working on the vehicle to avoid injury alway remove ring watch loose hanging jewelry and loose clothing before beginning to work on vehicle tie long hair securely behind the head keep hand and other object clear of the radiator fan blade electric cooling fan can start to operate at any time by an increase in underhood temperature even though the ignition is in the off position therefore care should be taken to ensure that the electric cooling fan is completely disconnected when workina under the hood the engine exhaust from this product contain chemical known to the state of california to cause cancer birth defect or other reproductive harm introduction in general this manual cover the servicing of the engine and asso ciated standard equipment in many case engine are supplied with accessory and equipment that are unique to the application if service information is ever required on such unique accessory or equipment it is suggested that power product division gri be con tacted the proper information will either be forwarded or the ser vice technician will be advised where it can be obtained the information in this manual is grouped in section according to the type of work being performed the various section are indi cated in the index in addition each section is subdivided to include topic such as diagnosis and testing cleaning and inspection over haul removal and installation procedure disassembly and assem bly procedure and service specification power product division gri the source for power telegraph road worldwide rev southfield michigan the description and specification contained in this manual were in effect at the time the book was released for printing power product division gm reserve the right to discontinue model at any time or change specification or design without notice and without incurring obligation note the recommendation and suggestion contained in
