and assembly engine assembly oil pump basic engine blank basic enaine identification an identification decal is affixed to the left side of the rocker cover of each engine the decal contain the engine serial number which identify this unit from all other next is the engine displacement which determine the engine specification then the model number and or special option which determine the part or component required on this unit use all number when seeking information or ordering replacement part for this engine identification of the cylinder block and therefore of the basic engine type can be made by reference to the stamping on the left rear top edge of the cylinder block serial number engine code engine build date the engine code will begin with this will indicate that it is liter engine the engine build date follow the corporate system example the first number indicate the year the letter indicate the month in alphabetical sequence january february etc omitting the last two number are the day of the month description basic engine the engine is water cooled cylinder stroke in line the oil pump is bolted onto the outside of the cylinder block gasoline engine the cylinder head in which the combustion chamber are located is of cross flow type the fresh fuel air mixture is drawn in on one side and burnt gase are passed into the exhaust system on the opposite side the valve are suspended in the cylinder head and are operated by mean of tappet pushrod and rocker arm the valve are disposed in an alternating pattern in the cylinder head starting with an exhaust valve by the thermostat housing the three bearing camshaft is located on the right side of the cylinder block and driven by roller chain from the crankshaft the common driving gear shared by the distributor and oil pump is located behind the second cam on the camshaft the eccentric cam driving the fuel pump is located between the sixth and seventh cam below the distributor the full flow oil filter is angled downward and mounted directly on the oil pump the crankshaft is mounted in five bearing the crankshaft end play is determined by thrust half ring at the center main bearing the front crankshaft journal is sealed by mean of an oil seal installed in the timing cover the rear crankshaft journal is sealed by mean of an oil seal pressed into an oil seal carrier the timing cover has cast ignition timing degree mark or tdc reference pointer notch on the crankshaft belt pulley is used in conjunction with either the degree or tdc reference point when installing the distributor and checking timing they are visible on the front left side of the engine basic engine description continued the spark plug have tapered seat without sealing ring the result is closed ventilation system in which the fume engine ventilation from the crankcase pass back via the inlet manifold into the the ventilation system consist of an oil filler cap with two cylinder for combustion connecting hose one of which pass to the inlet gas flow is regulated by calibrated orifice in the oil filler manifold and the other to the air cleaner cap ventilation at idle speed and half throttle ventilation at full throttle hose connection to air press in orifice hose intake manifold vacuum fitting lubrication circuit an eccentric twin rotor oil pump draw oil via strainer from the sump and force it into the full flow oil filter oil pressure is regulated by relief valve inside the pump the filtered oil pass through the center of the filter element then along short passage right hand side of the engine to the oil pressure switch and through transverse bore to the main oil gallery left hand side of the engine the crankshaft main bearing are fed directly from the main oil gallery and the camshaft bearing are linked in turn for their lubrication with the front center and rear main bearing each of the rod journal are supplied with oil by the nearest main bearing through oblique passage an oil hole in the connecting rod ensure splash lubrication of the piston pin and the trailing side of the cylinder timing chain and sprocket are also lubricated via splash hole the camshaft front bearing journal has machined groove through which oil is intermittently forced to the rocker shaft via passage in cylinder block and cylinder head lubrication circuit basic enaine diagnosis and testing camshaft lobe lift compression test check the lift of each lobe in consecutive order and make note of the reading remove the air cleaner and the valve rocker arm cover remove the valve rocker arm shaft assembly as detailed in the pertinent section make sure the push rod is in the valve lifter socket install dial indicator in such manner as to have the ball socket adapter of the indicator on the end of the push rod and in the same plane as the push rod movement compression gauge check be sure the crankcase is at the proper level and the battery is properly charged operate the engine for minimum of minute at rpm or until the engine is at normal operating temperature turn the ignition switch off then remove all the spark plug set the carburetor throttle plate and choke plate in the wide open position install compression gauge in no cylinder install an auxiliary starter switch in the starting circuit using the auxiliary starter switch crank the engine with the ignition switch off at least five compression stroke and record the highest reading note the approximate number of compression stroke required to obtain the highest reading repeat the test on each cylinder as was required to obtain the highest reading on the no cylinder test conclusion connect an auxiliary starter switch in the starting circuit crank the engine with the ignition switch off bump the crankshaft over until the tappet or lifter is on the base circle of the camshaft lobe at this point the push rod will be in its lowest position zero the dial indicator continue to rotate the crankshaft slowly until the push rod is in the fully raised position highest indicator reading compare the total lift recorded on the indicator with specification to check the accuracy of the original indicator reading continue to rotate the crankshaft until the indicator read zero if the lift on any lobe is below specified wear limit the camshaft and the valve lifter operating on the worn lobe must be replaced remove the dial indicator and auxiliary starter switch install the rocker arm shaft assembly as detailed under removal and installation io install the valve rocker arm cover and the air cleaner the indicated compression pressure are considered normal if the lowest reading cylinder is within of the highest refer to the following example seventy five percent of the highest cylinder reading is therefore cylinder no being less than of cylinder no indicate an improperly seated valve or worn or broken piston ring if one or more cylinder read low squirt approximately ml one tablespoon of engine oil on top of the piston in the low reading cylinder repeat compression pressure check on these cylinder if compression improve considerably the piston ring are at fault if compression doe not improve valve are sticking or seat ing poorly if two adjacent cylinder indicate low compression pressure and squirting oil on the piston doe not increase the compression the cause may be cylinder head gasket leak between the cylinder engine oil and or coolant in the cylinder could result from this problem it is recommended the following quick reference chart be used when checking cylinder compression pressure the chart has been calculated so that the lowest reading number is of the highest reading example after checking the compression pressure in all cylinder it was found that the highest reading obtained was psi the lowest pressure reading was psi the engine is within specification and the compression is considered satisfactory basic engine diagnosis and testing continued maximum minimum maximum minimum kpa psi kpa psi kpa psi kpa psi maximum minimum kpa psi kpa psi crankshaft end play force the crankshaft toward the rear of the engine install dial indicator so that the contact point rest against the crankshaft flange and the indicator axis is parallel to the crankshaft axis zero the dial indicator push the crankshaft forward and note the reading on the dial if the end play exceed the wear limit replace the thrust washer if the end play is less than the minimum limit inspect the thrust bearing face for scratch burr nick or dirt camshaft end play push the camshaft toward the rear of the engine install dial indicator so that the indicator point is on the camshaft sprocket attaching screw or gear hub zero the dial indicator position large screwdriver between the camshaft sprocket and the cylinder head pull the camshaft forward and release it compare the dial indicator reading with specification if the end play is excessive replace the thrust plate retaining the camshaft remove the dial indicator face runout install dial indicator so that the indicator point bear against the face turn the making sure that it is full forward or rearward so that the crankshaft end play will not be indicated as runout if the clutch face runout exceed specification remove the and check for burr between the and the face of the crankshaft mounting flange if no burr exist check the runout of the crankshaft mounting flange replace the or machine the crankshaft mounting face sufficiently to true up the surface if the mounting flange runout exceed specification replace it or reinstall it on the basic engine overhaul cylinder head replace the head if it is cracked do not plane or grind more than mm inch from the cylinder head gasket surface remove all burr or scratch with an oil stone reaming valve guide if it become necessary to ream the valve guide to an oversize valve alway use the reamer in sequence alway reface the valve seat after the valve guide has been reamed and use suitable scraper to break the sharp corner id at the top of the valve guide refacing valve seat refacing of the valve seat should be closely coordinated with the refacing of the valve face so that the finished seat and valve face will be concentric and the specified interference fit will be maintained this is important so that the valve and seat will have compression tight fit be sure that the refaced grinding wheel are properly dressed grind the valve seat to true degree angle remove only enough stock to clean up pit and groove or to correct the valve seat runout after the seat has been refaced use seat width scale or machinist scale to measure the seat width narrow the seat if necessary to bring it within specification if the valve seat width exceed the maximum limit remove enough stock from the top edge and or bottom edge of the seat to reduce the width to specification on the valve seat of all engine use degree angle grinding wheel to remove stock from the bottom of the seat raise the seat and use degree angle wheel to remove stock from the top of the seat lower the seat to remove stock from top of seat use wheel to remove stock from botxxa of seat use wheel the finished valve seat should contact the approximate center of the valve face it is good practice to determine where the valve seat contact the face to do this coat the seat with prussian blue and set the valve in place rotate the valve with light pressure if the blue is transferred to the center of the valve face the contact is satisfactory if the blue is transferred to the top edge of the valve face lower the valve seat if the blue is transferred to the bottom edge of the valve face raise the valve seat valve minor pit groove etc may be removed discard valve that are severely damaged if the face runout cannot be corrected by refinishing or stem clearance exceed specification discard any excessively worn or damaged valve train part refacing valve the valve refacing operation should be closely coordinated with the valve seat refacing operation so that the finished angle of the valve face and of the valve seat will be to specification and provide compression tight fit be sure that the refaced grinding wheel are properly dressed basic engine overhaul continued under no circumstance should the face of aluminized intake valve be ground or the valve lapped in as this will remove the diffused aluminum coating and reduce the valve wear and heat resistant property if the valve face are worn or pitted it will be necessary to install new valve and to resurface the valve seat or alternatively lap the seat using dummy valve the exhaust valve may be lapped in or the face ground if required if the valve face runout is excessive and or to remove pit and groove reface the valve to true degree angle remove only enough stock to correct the runout or to clean up the pit and groove if the edge of the valve head is less than mm inch thick after grinding replace the valve as the valve will run too hot in the engine the interference fit of the valve and seat should not be lapped out remove all groove or score mark from the end of the valve stem and chamfer it as necessary do not remove more than mm inch from the end of the valve stem for dimension refer to specification if the valve and or valve seat has been refaced it will be necessary to check the clearance between the rocker arm pad and the valve stem with the valve train assembly installed in the engine select fitting valve if the valve stem to valve guide clearance exceed the wear limit ream the valve guide for the next oversize valve stem valve with oversize stem diameter are available for service alway reface the valve seat after the guide has been reamed refer to reaming valve guide camshaft repair remove light scuff score or nick from the camshaft machined surface with smooth oil stone crankshaft dress minor score with an oil stone if the journal are severely marred or exceed the wear limit they should be refinished to size for the next undersize bearing refinishing journal refinish the journal to give the proper clearance with the next undersize bearing if the journal will not clean up to maximum undersize bearing available replace the crankshaft alway reproduce the same journal shoulder radius that existed originally too small radius will result in fatigue failure of the crankshaft too large radius will result in bearing failure due to radius ride of the bearing after refinishing the journal chamfer the oil hole then polish the journal with no grit polishing cloth and engine oil crocus cloth may also be used as polishing agent fitting main or connecting rod bearing with plastigage clean crankshaft journal inspect journal and thrust face thrust bearing for nick burr or bearing pickup that would cause premature bearing wear when replacing standard bearing with new bearing it is good practice to fit the bearing to minimum specified clearance if the desired clearance cannot be obtained with standard bearing try mm inch undersize in combination with standard bearing to obtain the proper clearance if fitting main bearing in the chassis position jack under the counterweight adjoining bearing which is being checked support crankshaft with jack so its weight will not compress plastigage and provide an erroneous reading place piece of plastigage on bearing surface across full width of bearing cap and about mm inch off center place plastigage full width of journal about inch off center installing plastigage check width of plastigage basic enoine overhaul continued install cap and torque bolt to specification do not turn crankshaft while plastigage is in place remove cap using plastigage scale check width of plastigage at widest point to get minimum clearance check at narrowest point to get maximum clearance difference between reading is taper of journal if clearance exceed specified limit on the connecting rod bearing try mm inch undersize bearing in combination with the standard bearing bearing clearance must be within specified limit if mm inch undersize main bearing are used on more than one journal be sure they are all installed in cylinder block side of bearing if standard and mm inch undersize bearing do not bring clearance within desired limit refinish crankshaft journal then install undersize bearing after bearing has been fitted remove plastigage apply light coat of engine oil to journal and bearing install bearing cap torque cap bolt to specification repeat procedure for remaining bearing that require replacement piston pin and ring fitting piston piston are available for service in standard siz and the oversiz shown in the part list measure the piston diameter to ensure that the specified clearance is obtained it may be necessary periodically to use another piston that is either larger or smaller to achieve the specified clearance if none can be fitted refinish the cylinder to provide the proper clearance for the piston when piston has been fitted mark it for assembly in the cylinder to which it was fitted if the taper out of round and piston to cylinder bore clearance condition of the cylinder bore are within specified limit new piston ring will give satisfactory service if new ring are to be installed in used cylinder that has not been refinished remove the cylinder wall glaze refer to cylinder block refinishing cylinder wall be sure to clean the cylinder bore thoroughly calculate the size piston to be used by taking cylinder bore check follow the procedure outlined under cleaning and inspection select the proper size piston to provide the desired clearance refer to the specification the piston should be measured mm inch below the dome and at to the piston pin bore make sure the piston and cylinder block are at room temperature degree degree after any refinishing operation allow the cylinder bore to cool and make sure the piston and bore are clean and dry before the piston fit is checked fitting piston ring three piston ring are fitted two compression and one oil control ring select the proper ring set for the size cylinder bore position the ring in the cylinder bore in which it is going to be used push the ring down into the bore area where normal ring wear is not encountered use the head of piston to position the ring in the bore so that the ring is square with the cylinder wall use caution to avoid damage to the ring or cylinder bore measure the gap between the end of the ring with feeler gauge if the ring gap is less or greater than the specified limit try another ring set check the ring side clearance of the compression ring with feeler gauge inserted between the ring and its lower land the gauge should slide freely around the entire ring circumference without binding any wear that occur will form step at the inner portion of the lower land if the lower land have high step the piston should be replaced basic enaine overhaul continued fitting piston pin the piston pin are selected to give the correct fit in the piston pin bore and bushing in the connecting rod piston are only supplied in service complete with the piston pin to ensure the correct fit the piston pin should not be interchanged valve rocker arm and or shaft assembly dress up minor surface defect on the rocker arm shaft and in the rocker arm bore with hone if the pad at the valve end of the rocker arm has grooved radius replace the rocker arm do not attempt to true this surface by grinding push rod following the procedure under push rod inspection check the push rod for straightness if the runout exceed the maximum limit at any point discard the rod do not attempt to straighten push rod cylinder block refinishing cylinder wall honing is recommended for refinishing cylinder wall only when the wall have minor scuff or scratch or for fitting piston to the specified clearance the grade of hone to be used is determined by the amount of metal to be removed follow the instruction of the hone manufacturer if coarse stone are used to start the honing operation leave enough material so that all hone mark can be removed with the finishing hone which is used to obtain the proper piston clearance cylinder wall that are severely marred and or worn beyond the specified limit should be refinished before any cylinder is refinished all main bearing cap must be in place and tightened to the proper torque so that the crankshaft bearing bore will not become distorted from there finishing operation refinish only the cylinder or cylinder that require it all piston are the same weight both standard and oversize therefore various siz of piston can be used without upsetting engine balance refinish the cylinder with the most wear first to determine the maximum oversize if the cylinder will not clean up when refinished for the maximum oversize piston recommended replace the block refinish the cylinder to within approximately mm inch of the required oversize diameter this will allow enough stock for the final step of honing so that the correct surface finish and pattern are obtained for the proper use of the refinishing equipment follow the instruction of the manufacturer only experienced personnel should be allowed to perform this work use motor driven spring pressure type hone at speed of rpm hone of grit siz will normally provide the desired bore surface finish of rms when honing the cylinder bore use lubricant mixture of equal part of kerosene and sae no motor oil operate the hone in such way to produce cross hatch finish on the cylinder bore the cross hatch pattern should be at an angle of approximately degree to the cylinder bore after the final operation in either of the two refinishing method described and prior to checking the piston fit thoroughly clean and oil the cylinder wall mark the piston to correspond to the cylinder in which they are to be installed when the refinishing of all cylinder that require it has been completed and all piston are fitted thoroughly clean the entire block and oil the cylinder wall repairing sand hole or porous engine casting porosity or sand hole which will cause oil seepage or leakage can occur with modern casting process complete inspection of engine and transmission should be made if the leak is attributed to the porous condition of the cylinder block or sand hole repair can be made with metallic plastic part no cgaz do not repair crack with this material repair with this metallic plastic must be confined to those cast iron engine component surface where the inner wall surface is not exposed to engine coolant pressure or oil pressure for example cylinder block surface extending along the length of the block upward from the oil pan rail to the cylinder water jacket but not including machined area lower rear face of the cylinder block intake manifold casting cylinder head along the rocker arm cover gasket surface the following procedure should be used to repair porous area or sand hole in cast iron clean the surface to be repaired by grinding or rotary filing to clean bright metal surface chamfer or undercut the hole or porosity to greater depth than the rest of the cleaned surface solid metal must surround the hole opening larger than mm inch should not be repaired using metallic plastic opening in excess of mm inch can be drilled tapped and plugged using common tool clean the repair area thoroughly metallic plastic will not stick to dirty or oily surface mix the metallic plastic base and hardener as directed on the container stir thoroughly until uniform apply the repair mixture with suitable clean tool putty knife wood spoon etc forcing the epoxy into the hole or porosity allow the repair mixture to harden this can be accomplished by two method heat cure with degree degree lamp placed mm inch from the repaired surface or air dry for hour at temperature above io degree degree sand or grind the repaired area to blend with the general contour of the surrounding surface paint the surface to match the rest of the block basic engine cleaning and inspection the cleaning and inspection procedure are for complete engine overhaul therefore for partial engine overhaul or part replacement follow the pertinent cleaning or inspection procedure intake manifold cleaning remove all gasket material from the machined surface of the manifold clean the manifold in suitable solvent and dry it with compressed air inspection inspect the manifold for crack damaged gasket surface or other defect that would make it unfit for further service replace all stud that are stripped or otherwise damaged remove all filing and foreign matter that may have entered the manifold as result of repair exhaust manifold cleaning remove all gasket material from the manifold inspection inspect the cylinder head joining flange of the exhaust manifold for evidence of exhaust gas leak inspect the manifold for crack damaged gasket surface or other defect that would make them unfit for further service valve rocker arm and or shaft assembly cleaning clean all the part thoroughly make sure all oil passage are open make sure the oil passage in the rocker arm is open inspection on rocker arm shaft assembly check the clearance between each rocker arm and the shaft by checking the id of the rocker arm bore and the od of the shaft if the clearance between any rocker arm and the shaft exceed the wear limit replace the shaft and or the rocker arm inspect the shaft and the rocker arm bore for nick scratch score or scuff inspect the pad at the valve end of the rocker arm for indication of scuffing or abnormal wear if the pad is grooved replace the rocker arm do not attempt to true this surface by grinding check the adjusting nut torque if not within specification replace the nut check the rocker arm pad and fulcrum seat for excessive wear crack nick or burr push rod cleaning clean the push rod in suitable solvent blow dry the push rod with compressed air inspection check the end of the push rod for nick groove roughness or excessive wear the push rod can be visually checked for straightness while they are installed in the engine by rotating them with the valve closed they also can be checked with dial indicator if the push rod is visibly bent it should be replaced cylinder head cleaning with the valve installed to protect the valve seat remove deposit from the combustion chamber and valve head with scraper and wire brush be careful not to damage the cylinder head gasket surface after the valve are removed clean the valve guide bore with valve guide cleaning tool use cleaning solvent to remove dirt grease and other deposit clean all bolt hole remove all deposit from the valve with fine wire brush or buffing wheel inspection check the cylinder head for crack and inspect the gasket surface for burr and nick replace the head if it is cracked the following inspection procedure are for cylinder head that is to be completely overhauled for individual repair operation use only the pertinent inspection procedure when cylinder head is removed because of gasket leak check the flatness of the cylinder head gasket surface for conformance to specification if necessary to refinish the cylinder head gasket surface do not plane or grind off more than mm inch check diagonally check across center azmfi basic enaine cleaning and inspection continued check the valve seat runout with an accurate gauge follow the instruction of the gauge manufacturer if the runout exceed the wear limit reface the valve and valve seat measure the valve seat width reface any valve seat whose width is not within specification inspect the valve face and the edge of the valve head for pit groove score or other damage inspect the stem for bent condition and the end of the valve head for pit groove score or other wear inspect the stem for bent condition and the end of the stem for groove or score check the valve head for sign of burning erosion warpage and cracking minor pit groove etc may be removed discard valve that are severely damaged inspect the valve spring valve spring retainer lock and sleeve for wear or damage discard any visually damaged part check the valve stem to valve guide clearance of each valve in its respective valve guide with the tool shown below or its equivalent use flat end indicator point with the cylinder head gasket face up slide new standard intake or exhaust valve into the valve guide to be checked until the tip of the valve stem is flush with the top of the valve guide mount dial test indicator on the cylinder head by the valve to be checked position the plunger of the dial test indicator on the edge of the valve head and measure the valve head movement by applying lateral pressure to the valve if the valve head movement is greater than the value given below the valve guide in question must be reamed using the appropriate reamer and new oversize valve installed maximum permissible valve head movement value for the above test procedure intake valve mm in exhaust valve mm in check the spring for proper pressure at the specified spring length tool dd manually rotating the valve spring assembly while installed in the engine must not be used to determine good and or bad valve spring weak valve spring cause poor engine performance replace any spring not within specification apply torque until click is heard read torque wrench and multiply reading by iwo valve clutch spring tester tool dd valve set knob to compressed length of spring basic enaine cleaning and inspection continued check each spring for squareness using steel square and flat surface stand the spring and square on end on the flat surface slide the spring up to the square revolve the spring slowly and observe the space between the top coil of the spring and the square the out of square limit are mm inch follow the same procedure to check new valve spring before installation make certain the proper spring color coded is installed closed coil end downward not more than mm tappet cleaning thoroughly clean the tappet in cleaning solvent and wipe them with clean lint free cloth inspection check th tappet for wear or score check the bottom end of tappet to make sure that it has slight convex replace tappet that are scored worn or if the bottom is not smooth if the bottom surface is worn flat it may be used with the original camshaft only timing chain and sprocket cleaning clean part in solvent and dry them with compressed air lubricate the timing chain with engine oil before installing it on the sprocket inspection inspect the chain for broken link inspect the sprocket for crack and worn or damaged tooth replace all the component of the timing chain and sprocket assembly if any le item need replacement camshaft cleaning clean the camshaft in solvent and wipe it dry inspection inspect the camshaft lobe for scoring and sign of abnormal wear lobe pitting except in the general area of the lobe toe is not detrimental to the operation of the camshaft therefore the camshaft should not be replaced unless the lobe lift loss has exceeded specification or pitting has occurred in the lobe lift area the lift of the camshaft lobe can be checked with the camshaft installed in the engine or on center refer to camshaft lobe lift to measure the camshaft lobe lift proceed as follow measure distance between the major and minor diameter of each cam lobe with vernier caliper and record the reading the difference in the reading on each cam diameter is the lobe lift no pitting mm mm allowed in this area dimension dimension the cam lob minus equal ie lift if the reading do not meet specification replace the camshaft camshaft bore inspection check camshaft bore for size taper roundness runout and finish if any of these dimension exceed the limit given in specification install new camshaft bearing crankshaft cleaning handle the crankshaft with care to avoid possible fracture or damage to the finished surface clean the crankshaft with solvent then blow out all oil passage with compressed air inspection inspect the main and connecting rod journal for crack scratch groove or score inspect the crankshaft oil seal surface for nick sharp edge or burr that might damage the oil seal during installation or cause premature seal wear measure the diameter of each journal in at least four place to determine an out of round taper or undersize condition basic enaine cleaning and inspection continued vs verttcal taper vs taf er vs and vs out of round check for out the installed on the crankshaft check the face runout following the procedure under diagnosis and testing connecting rod cleaning removing the bearing from the rod and cap identify the hearing if they are to be used again clean the connecting rod in solvent including the rod bore and the back of the insert do not use caustic cleaning solution blow out all passage with compressed air inspection the connecting rod and related part should be carefully inspected and checked for conformance to specification various form of engine wear caused by these part can be readily identified shiny surface on either pin boss side of the piston usually indicate that connecting rod is bent abnormal connecting rod bearing wear can be caused by either bent connecting rod worn or damaged crankpin or tapered connecting rod bore twisted connecting rod will not create an identifiable wear pattern but badly twisted rod will disturb the action of the entire piston ring and connecting rod assembly and may be the cause of excessive oil consumption inspect the connecting rod for sign of fracture and the bearing bore for out of round and taper if the bore exceed the recommended limit and or if the connecting rod is fractured it should be replaced check the id of the connecting rod piston pin bore if the pin bore in the connecting rod is larger than specification install mm inch oversize piston pin first prefit the oversize piston pin to the piston pin bore by reaming or honing the piston then assemble the piston piston pin and connecting rod following the procedure for assembly it is not necessary to ream or hone the pin bore in the connecting rod replace damaged connecting rod nut and bolt check the connecting rod for bend or twist on suitable alignment fixture follow the instruction of the fixture manufacturer if the bend and or twist exceed specification the connecting rod must be straightened or replaced basic engine cleaning and inspection continued piston pin and ring main and connecting rod bearing cleaning cleaning remove deposit from the piston surface clean gum or varnish from the piston skirt piston pin and ring with solvent do not use caustic cleaning solution or wire brush to clean piston clean the bearing insert and cap thoroughly in solvent and dry them with compressed air do not scrape gum or varnish deposit from the bearing shell clean the ring groove with ring groove cleaner make sure the oil ring slot or hole are clean cleaner inspection carefully inspect the piston for fracture at the ring land skirt and pin boss and for scuffed rough or scored skirt if the lower inner portion of the ring groove has high step replace the piston the step will interfere with ring operation and cause excessive ring side clearance spongy eroded area near the edge of the top of the piston are usually caused by detonation or pre ignition shiny surface on the thrust surface of the piston offset from the centerline between the piston pin hole can be caused by bent connecting rod replace piston that show sign of excessive wear wavy ring land or fracture or damage from detonation or pre ignition check the piston to cylinder bore clearance by measuring the piston and bore diameter refer to the specification for the proper clearance refer to cylinder block inspection for the bore measurement procedure measure the od of the piston with micrometer approximately mm inch below the dome and at degree to the piston pin bore check the ring side clearance replace piston pin showing sign of fracture etching or wear check the piston pin fit in the piston and rod refer to piston and connecting rod assembly check the od of the piston pin and the id of the pin bore in the piston replace any piston pin or piston that is not within specification replace all ring that are scored broken chipped or cracked check the end gap and side clearance ring should not be transferred from one piston to another regardless of mileage or hour section fatigue failure mproper seating scratch wlped out dirt imbedded into bearing material scratched by dirt lack of oil or mwroper clearance lc izzil from entire surface tapered journal or crankshaft radius ride hourglassing inspection inspect each bearing carefully bearing that have scored chipped or worn surface should be replaced the copper lead bearing base may be visible through the bearing overlay this doe not mean that the bearing is worn it is not necessary to replace the bearing if the bearing clearance is within recommended limit check the clearance of bearing that appear to be satisfactory with plastigage as detailed under overhaul main and connecting rod bearing cylinder block cleaning after any cylinder bore repair operation such as honing or deglazing clean the bore with soap or detergent and water then thoroughly rinse the bore with clean water to remove the soap or detergent and wipe the bore dry with clean lint free cloth finally wipe the bore with clean cloth dipped in engine oil if these procedure are not followed rusting of the cylinder bore may occur basic enaine cleaning and inspection continued if the engine is disassembled thoroughly clean the block with solvent remove old gasket material from all machined surface remove all pipe plug that seal oil passage then clean out all the passage blow out all passage bolt hole etc with compressed air make sure the thread in the cylinder head bolt hole are clean dirt in the thread may cause binding and result in false torque reading use tap to true up thread and to remove any deposit thoroughly clean the groove in the crankshaft bearing and bearing retainer inspection after the block has been thoroughly cleaned check it for crack minute crack not visible to the naked eye may be detected by coating the suspected area with mixture of kerosene and light engine oil wipe the part dry and immediately apply coating of zinc oxide dissolved in wood alcohol if crack are present the coating will become discolored at the defective area replace the block if it is cracked check all machined gasket surface for burr nick scratch and score remove minor imperfection with an oil stone replace all expansion type plug that show evidence of leakage inspect the cylinder wall for scoring roughness or other sign of wear check the cylinder bore for out of round and taper measure the bore with an accurate bore gauge following the instruction of the manufacturer measure the diameter of each cylinder bore at the top middle and bottom with the gauge placed at right angle and parallel to the centerline of the engine use only the measurement obtained at degree to the engine centerline when calculating the piston to cylinder bore clearance center line of engine at rlohf awleto center lme ff enowe parallel to center lmof out of round difference between and taper difference between the measurement at top of cylinoer bore and the measurement at bottom of cylinder bore refinish cylinder that are deeply scored and or when out of round and or taper exceed the wear limit if the cylinder wall have minor surface imperfection but the out of round and taper are within limit it may be possible to remove the imperfection by honing the cylinder wall and installing new service piston ring providing the piston clearance is within specified limit oil pan cleaning scrape any dirt or metal particle from the inside of the pan scrape all old gasket material from the gasket surface wash the pan in solvent and dry it thoroughly be sure all foreign particle are removed from below the baffle plate inspection check the pan for crack hole damaged drain plug thread and loose baffle or damaged gasket surface inspect for damage uneven surface at the bolt hole caused by over torquing the bolt straighten surface as required repair any damage or replace the pan if repair cannot be made satisfactorily oil pump cleaning wash all part in solvent and dry them thoroughly with compressed air use brush to clean the inside of the pump housing and the pressure relief valve chamber be sure all dirt and metal particle are removed inspection refer to the specification for clearance and wear limit check the inside of the pump housing and the outer race and rotor for damage or excessive wear check the mating surface of the pump cover for wear if the cover mating surface is worn scored or grooved replace the cover measure the outer race to housing clearance then check the clearance between the outer race and the rotor lobe basic enaine cleaning and inspection continued with the rotor assembly installed in the housing place straight edge over the rotor assembly and the housing measure the clearance rotor end play between the straight edge and the rotor and outer race the outer race shaft and rotor are replaceable only as an assembly check the drive shaft to housing bearing clearance by measuring the od of the shaft and the id of the housing bearing inspect the relief valve spring for collapsed or worn condition check the relief valve spring tension if the spring is worn or damaged replace the spring check the relief valve piston for score and free operation in the bore stftawit edge basic engine removal and remove the ring gear for repositioning on the basic enaine removal and installation continued when installing the ring gear it must be heated evenly to temperature of to do not exceed as the ring gear wear resistant property will be destroyed if the ring gear is to be heated by direct flame place the ring gear on metal plate approximately to mm in thick and heat plate from below in the area of the ring gear until it reach the required temperature the correct temperature can be detected by using special type of temperature sensitive crayon fit the ring gear with the chamfer on the leading face cooling system emission control system specification charging system section ignition system distributorless subject page description and operation diagnosing and testing dis engine speed and position dis diagnosis equipment engine load dis diagnosing engine temperature dis cleaning and inspection fuel octane level adjustment spark plug ignition coil driver ignition coil sensor fail safe ignition wire cranking mode spark plug inspection chart run mode dis engine harness check transient mode spark timing overspeed mode engine coolant temperature service adjustment and check sensor characteristic removal and installation wiring harness circuit ignition coil identification engine speed sensor wiring harness circuit engine coolant temperature sensor identification subject page lanition svstem distributorless description and operation warning high voltage produced by distributorless ignition system is higher than for conventional ignition system when carrying out service operation on an engine equipped with distributorless ignition it is important to be aware of the above point as well as all the usual safety measure to prevent the possibility of electric shock the purpose of an engine ignition system is to ignite the fuel air mixture at the correct time and sequence based upon the input it receive the distributorless ignition system dis used on the vsg engine is state of the art ignition system the brain of this system is the ignition control module icm also known as the universal electronic spark control uesc module this module normally receive four input from these input the icm compute spark strategy spark advance to obtain optimum engine performance for correct input condition with this system the electronic control module monitor the engine load speed and operating temperature and was on exhaust will be on compression and the one that decide what degree of spark advance is correct for all of the operating condition this system maximiz the was on compression will be on exhaust the spark in the benefit of the high compression swirl design because timing is set for life inherently in the design of the engine exhaust cylinder is wasted but little of the coil energy is lost and there are no moving part in the ignition system itself no maintenance is required except for periodic spark plug the spark strategy is based on sensor and manifold check the system provide for fixed spark advance at vacuum input to the icm module which include the start up for cold weather starting fixed advance for service checking and for average value default setting in case of component failure particular attention has been given to spark optimization for excellent fuel economy in the warm up mode which is coupled with improved warm up and new carburetor the spark plug are paired so that one plug fire during the compression stroke and its companion plug fire during the exhaust stroke the next time that coil is fired the plug that crankshaft position crankshaft speed engine temperature engine vacuum load following input compression exhaust stroke stroke engine speed and position the crankshaft position and speed information come to the icm from the crankshaft position sensor ckp also known as the variable reluctance sensor vrs the ckp is triggered by tooth cast into the engine side of the the tooth spaced io apart indicate to the icm the crankshaft speed the missing tooth indicate crankshaft position ignition system distributorless description and operation continued engine load the engine load information is processed into the icm electronic by pressure transducer located within the icm vacuum line connect the transducer to the engine intake manifold the engine vacuum is proportional to its load engine temperature the engine coolant temperature ect sensor send engine temperature information to the icm the ect sensor is located in the intake manifold water jacket fuel octane level adjustment this input to the icm retard the spark according to the spark timing table on page this input is not usually used in the the icm module output are ignition coil driver the icm switch two ignition coil on and off at the correct time to give the desired spark advance sensor fail safe if the icm identify failure of any of its input other than the engine speed position sensor it will substitute fixed value for that input until such time that the fault on the input is rectified failed sensor is defined as the instantaneous reading of sensor being made that is either above or below the maximum or minimum reading as defined by the system constant below engine coolant minimum temperature maximum manifold absolute minimum kpa hg pressure maximum kpa hg ignition timing is adjusted constantly by the icm many factor including all the sensor affect the final ignition setting cranking mode cranking mode is the area of engine operating speed within which the ignition timing is at static position the static spark advance is fixed at io degree btdc up to rpm run mode in this mode the rpm is above and the spark advance is calculated in three main section which are added together the icm section are base spark advance bsa plus spark advance off set temperature saot plus spark advance offset detonation ect saode the final spark advance is then corrected for propagation delay and finally the spark advance is limited by the system range and the spark slew rate limited the base spark advance bsa is calculated by the icm looking at speed and load input the spark advance offset temperature saot will change ignition timing from the function of engine coolant temperature ect this allow the spark advance to be altered during cold engine condition to improve starting and operation spark advance offset detonation ect saode the ignition timing is offset as function of engine coolant temperature ect this allow the spark advance to be reduced during hot engine condition to avoid detonation and allow base spark advance to be calibrated near to the best performance curve transient mode this function is to provide detonation protection when the engine load is increased rapidly by fast opening of the throttle plate rapid increase in engine load are determined by large change in consecutive manifold absolute pressure map value to the icm overspeed mode if the instantaneous engine speed is greater than the maximum speed threshold then the spark event are terminated until the instantaneous engine speed fall below rpm during this time all other icm calculation are performed as normal lanition system distributorless service adjustment and check each hour of engine operation remove the spark plug and clean adjust the electrode clean and visually check spark plug high tension lead and check for secure fit replace if necessary the icm must be mounted above the intake manifold vacuum fitting to prevent fuel from entering the icm chamber the connecting rubber hose must be compatible with gasoline and be as short as possible it is recommended that fuel vapor trap be used in line in the connecting hose ignition system distributorless removal and installation spark tester neon bulb type champion ct or equivalent there is no need to disconnect plug wire just place this spark tester on spark plug wire to determine if spark is being provided to the plug this is especially useful for those hard to reach plug wire spark tester gap type special service tool or equivalent connect this gap type spark tester between any spark plug wire and engine ground to instantly determine if spark is being provided to the plug spark plug with broken side electrode is not sufficient to check for spark and may lead to incorrect result volt ohmmeter rotunda or equivalent volt ohmmeter is essential for gathering system operating data during diagnosis testing and engine servicing procedure digital volt ohmmeter dvom can also be used for general purpose system ignition system distributorless governor fuel system cooling system emission control system specification charging system section fuel system subject page subject page description and operation general description identification diagnosis and testing general information fuel pump description diagnosis and testing pressure test capacity volume test removal and installation fuel pump assembly zenith model carburetor operation and service fuel supply system idle system high speed system accelerating pump system disassembly cleaning and inspection assembly of air intake assembly of fuel bowl automatic choke operation choke pull off automatic choke setup procedure adjusting the choke pull off adjusting the thermostatic spring fuel system description and operation general description the fuel system include permanently sealed single action fuel pump operated by lobe on the camshaft and single barrel carburetor with either manual choke or an automatic choke it also has screen filter located in the fuel inlet identification the carburetor is identified on the main body near the fuel inlet the basic part number for all carburetor is to procure part it is necessary to know the part number prefix and suffix fgjl aa part no prefix part no suffix fuel system diagnosis and testing general information water and dirt that accumulate in the fuel tank can cause restricted fuel line or filter and malfunction of the fuel pump or carburetor condensation which is the greatest source of water entering the fuel tank is formed by moisture in the air when it strike the cold interior wall of the fuel tank if the accumulation of dirt and water in the filter is excessive the fuel tank should be removed and flushed and the line from the fuel pump to the tank should be blown out air leakage in the fuel inlet line can cause low fuel pump pressure and volume restricted fuel tank vent can cause low fuel pump pressure and volume and can result in collapsed inlet hose or collapsed fuel tank high or low pressure are the two most likely fuel pump trouble that will affect engine performance low pressure will cause lean mixture and fuel starvation at high speed and excessive pressure will cause high fuel consumption and carburetor flooding dirt accumulation in the fuel and air passage improper idle adjustment and improper fuel level are the major source of carburetor trouble fuel svstem fuel pump description single action mechanical fuel pump is standard on this engine it is located on the right side of the engine and is driven by lobe on the camshaft the pump is permanently sealed and is serviced by replacing the entire unit diagnosis and testing to determine that the fuel pump is in satisfactory operating condition test for both fuel pump pressure and fuel pump capacity volume should be performed the test are performed with the fuel pump installed on the engine and the engine at normal operating temperature at idle speed before the test make sure the replaceable fuel filter has been changed within the recommended maintenance mileage interval when in doubt install new filter pressure test refer to the fuel pump specification and note the fuel pump pressure and capacity volume design tolerance remove the air cleaner assembly disconnect the fuel inlet line or the fuel filter at the carburetor use care to prevent combustion due to fuel spillage connect pressure gauge restrictor and flexible hose between the fuel filter and carburetor note inside diameter of smallest passage in test flow circuit must not be smaller than mm in position the flexible fuel outlet hose and the restrictor so the fuel can be discharged into the graduated container before taking pressure reading operate the engine at the specified idle rpm and vent the system into the container by opening the hose restrictor momentarily close the hose restrictor allow the pressure to stabilize and note the reading if the pump pressure is not within specification with temperature normalized at idle speed and in neutral and the fuel line and filter are in satisfactory condition the pump is worn or damaged and should be replaced if the pump pressure is within specification perform the test for fuel capacity volume capacity volume test with the fuel pump pressure within specification test the capacity volume as follow operate the engine at the specified idle rpm open the hose restrictor and expel the fuel into the container while observing the time required to expel liter one pint close the restrictor liter one pint or more of fuel should be expelled within the specified time limit sec if the pump volume is below specification repeat the test using an auxiliary fuel supply and new fuel filter if the pump volume meet specification while using the auxiliary fuel supply check for restriction in the fuel supply from the tank and for the tank not venting properly fuel system removal and installation fuel pump assembly removal disconnect the inlet and outlet line at the fuel pump remove the pump attaching screw then remove the pump and the gasket discard the gasket installation remove all the gasket material from the mounting pad and pump flange apply oil resistant sealer to both side of new gasket and to the thread on the attaching bolt position the new gasket on the pump flange and hold the pump in position against the mounting pad make sure the rocker arm is riding on the camshaft eccentric turn engine over until the fuel pump eccentric is on the low side of the stroke press the pump tight against the pad install the attaching screw and alternately tighten them to specification connect the fuel inlet and outlet line operate the engine and check for leak fuel system zenith model carburetor operation and service idle system the zenith model carburetor is singe downdraft unit of two piece construction consisting of cast aluminum air intake and throttle body with fuel bowl the single venturi is removable for easy adaptability to meet different size engine requirement the mounting flange hole are elongated to allow it to fit either the sae or flange vacuum operated accelerator pump is used fuel supply system the fuel supply system consist of the threaded fuel inlet connection and fuel valve seat fuel valve float and fuel chamber the fuel supply line is connected to the threaded inlet gasoline pass through the connection through the fuel valve seat and past the fuel valve and into the fuel chamber the level of the fuel in the fuel chamber is regulated by the float through its control of the fuel valve the fuel valve doe not open and close alternately it assume more or less fixed opening position as regulated by the float sufficient to maintain proper level in the fuel chamber equal to the fuel demand of the engine according to its speed and load the idle system supply fuel to run the engine at curb idle and also slow speed until sufficient velocity is built up in the main venturi to operate the main system this system consist of the idle discharge hole the idle adjusting needle the passage or channel between the idle jet and the discharge hole the idle air bleed and the idle jet and pickup tube at idling speed manifold vacuum is transmitted through the primary idle discharge hole to the idle jet through passage running through the throttle and air intake body fuel from the fuel bowl flow through the main jet into the fuel well where it is picked up by the pickup tube to be metered by the idle jet as the fuel leaf the idle jet it is mixed with air from the idle air bleed which is located just above the jet in the air intake therefore fuel and air mixture is provided just before the passage to the idle discharge hole this air bleed calibration is to provide better control of the fuel delivery and to prevent the fuel in the bowl from being siphoned into the intake manifold through the idle system when the engine is shut off secondary idle discharge hole idle air bleed jet and up tube adjusti needle idle system the discharge of the idle fuel into the air stream at curb idle is controlled by the idle adjusting needle in the primary idle discharge hole turning the needle in clockwise restrict the primary discharge hole to provide leaner mixture turning the needle out counterclockwise allow richer mixture fuel svstem zenith model carburetor continued as the throttle plate open extra fuel is discharged from the secondary idle discharge hole as the throttle plate pass them this hole is precisely located from the closed throttle plate with the size and location depending on fuel requirement as the throttle plate is opened wider the idle system gradually cease to function the delivery of fuel through the high speed system increase as the throttle plate is advanced although the delivery of fuel through the idle system diminish as the throttle plate approach the wide open position it continue to deliver small volume of fuel contributing to the fuel delivered by the high speed system it cannot be said that the idle system cease to deliver fuel entirely at wide open throttle but it must be made clear that the amount of fuel delivered from the idle system at wide open throttle is minute and relatively unimportant high speed system the high speed system consist of venturi main jet well vent and well tube this system also control the mixture at part throttle speed the main jet control fuel delivery from about one quarter to three quarter throttle opening to maintain proper mixture ratio small amount of air is admitted through the well vent there are also air bleed hole located in the well tube at point below the level of fuel in the well introducing air into the well tube below the level of fuel reduce the surface tension of the fuel and help fuel flow at low suction this bleed also restrict fuel flow through the main jet under high suction well vent discharge tube main system when the throttle plate is opened to point just above the idle position enough air pass through the carburetor to lower the pressure at the discharge tube the float chamber is open to atmospheric pressure consequently the greater pressure in the float chamber will cause the fuel to flow from the fuel bowl through the main jet into the main well and well tube air admitted through bleed hole in the well tube an amount measured by the well vent is mixed with the fuel this mixture of fuel from the main well tube pass through the discharge tube in the venturi and is added to the air stream in the venturi this mixture then pass into the intake manifold fuel system zenith model carburetor continued accelerating pump system the accelerating pump control the amount of fuel that is discharged into the air stream on sudden throttle opening when the throttle is opened suddenly air rush through the carburetor into the intake manifold and to the engine this air is lighter than the liquid fuel and get into motion more quickly that mean that the air reach the cylinder of the engine before the first charge of fuel supplied by the high speed system lean mixture would result momentarily in this case and to counteract the condition additional fuel must be supplied instantly the supply of that extra fuel is the job of the accelerating pump system accelerating jet spring protector channel accelerating system the accelerating system consist of the pump diaphragm assembly accelerating jet intake check valve and three part making up the refill check the pump is vacuum operated in this system the pump diaphragm is pulled against the pump spring by the engine manifold vacuum when the throttle is opened the pull on the diaphragm drop allowing the spring to push the diaphragm against the fuel causing it to discharge through the accelerator jet this amount may be varied by adjusting the travel of the pump spring such adjustment is made possible by changing the length of the diaphragm protector in the end of the pump spring each of these group use exploded illustration as guide for disassembly and reassembly cleaning and inspection thoroughly clean all metal part in solvent or deepclene blow out all part and channel with air pressure inspect for damage excessive wear burr or warpage do not clean non metallic part in solvent or deepclene note the following assembly instruction and the exploded illustration are generalized and include all part possibly found in the carburetor at this time therefore all of the part shown and mentioned may not be included in the particular assembly being worked on assembly of distort press idle tube into casting with rolled down orifice into casting until the bottom end is mm inch from the cast surface do not bend or distort assembly of fuel bowl install the following part plug over spark vacuum port spark vacuum tube to approximately mm inch from casting throttle body plug flush pump check valve flush using tool cl well tube making sure that end opening align with gently touch the seat back screw out approximately one turn install power jet valve using cl wrench no gasket required install main jet and seat firmly with screwdriver no gasket required install main jet plug and washer and tighten securely if main jet adjustment or solenoid shutoff are used install in place of plug turn body right side up insert float axle into hole in float lower float axle and float into slot provided in body with the float needle sticking into the threaded opening install the fuel valve seat and washer making sure that the fuel valve enter the seat properly the valve will enter the seat more easily if the float is held in the up position and tighten securely insert float axle clip in slot to bear against axle end holding axle in place raise float by applying light finger pressure to the float bracket float pontoon should be approximately level when the valve is seated if not bend tab to carefully adjust install ring on venturi skirt and place venturi io into recess provided in body making sure that the nozzle line up with the notch in the fuel well place intake gasket in position on body note if you have an older model unit with removable accelerator jet after intake gasket is in place an additional washer will be assembled over the accelerator jet tube and set inside the large hole in the intake gasket and on top of the smaller washer lower air intake carefully onto bowl and fasten securely and evenly using attaching screw place accelerator pump diaphragm in place on body with bagged section into cavity insert diaphragm protector into end of spring and install spring with protector against diaphragm place cover over spring and compress insert screw and tighten evenly and securely note pump spacer will install into cover before assembly if required automatic choke operation when the engine is cranked rich mixture is delivered to the engine when the engine start air movement into the carburetor cause the choke plate to open against the thermostatic spring pressure as the engine warm up the thermostatic coil unwind and the choke plate gradually open fuel svstem zenith model carburetor continued when the temperature drop below certain point thermostatic switch close and the heater circuit is complete electric current supplied by circuit now flow through the coil heater its electrical resistance to current flow generate the necessary heat choke pull off after the engine is started air flow into the carburetor will open the choke plate small amount against the tension of the thermostatic coil the choke plate wili open this slight amount because it is mounted off center on the choke shaft this slight opening of the choke plate is necessary to prevent an overly rich mixture after the engine start because the velocity of the air entering the carburetor will vary with engine speed the choke plate is linked to vacuum operated piston that provide positive pull against the closing tension of the thermostatic coil piston the action of the piston is called choke pull off and its purpose is two fold it help open the choke after the engine start it control the position of the choke plate depending on engine load when the throttle is opened to accelerate the air fuel mixture has to be enriched the action of the accelerator pump provide momentary enrichment but additional richness is required since the engine is cold this added richness is provided by the action of the pull off system the opening of the throttle valve cause drop in intake manifold vacuum which is transmitted to the diaphragm or piston with the lower vacuum signal the choke coil is able to move the choke valve toward the closed position the amount it is closed depend on how much vacuum drop occur during acceleration automatic choke setup procedure the automatic choke assembly contain bimetallic thermostatic coil spring which regulate the choke plate opening at different temperature and vacuum piston which regulate choke pull off the piston exert pull against the closing action of the thermostatic spring this help to open the choke after the engine start and control the position of the choke plate depending on engine load adjusting the choke pull off remove the choke housing cover remove the cork insulation strip remove the inner plate position the sliding pin in the slip link slot away from the piston place mm inch gage pin between the top of the choke plate and the inside of the air intake set opening to mm inch gauge pin fuel svstem zenith model carburetor continued adjust the stop screw until the slip link start to move away from the pin sliding pin tighten the lock nut to nom lb in to limit the travel of the piston so that the choke plate is pulled to the gage dimension install the inner plate install the cork insulation strip io install the choke housing cover adjusting the thermostatic spring manualiy open the choke plate by rotating the choke shaft allow the thermostatic spring inside the choke assembly to slowly close the choke plate when the choke housing is at the choke plate should be within mm inch of but not touching the choke bore if the choke plate doe not meet this dimension loosen the three cover plate screw to rotate the cover as necessary and repeat step and if the carburetor temperature is other than make the following correction set the choke plate as described in step and readjust the cover plate to compensate for the deviation from each mark on the cover plate denote change in the setting for example if the actual carburetor temperature is when the choke plate is adjusted rotate the cover two mark in the lean direction likewise if the carburetor temperature is rotate the cover four mark in the rich direction check the rotation of the choke shaft to be sure there is no sticking or binding before installing the carburetor emission control svstem section title page section title page starting system ignition system distributorless governor fuel system cooling system emission control system specification charging system section emission control system subject page subject page description and operation exhaust gas recirculation egr valve crankcase emission control system diagnosis and testing of egr system malfunction rough idle surge stall or won start detonation spark knock poor fuel economy egr system gas flow test diaphragm leak test egr valve leakage check testing for egr source vacuum tesing the port pvs valve removal and installation emission control system description and operation exhaust gas recirculation egr valve the exhaust gas recirculation egr system feed controlled amount of exhaust gas back through the cylinder to reduce the combustion temperature this reduce the formation of an atmospheric pollutant known as oxide of nitrogen nox carburetor spacer egr valve egr tube intake manifold the egr valve is vacuum operated flow control valve it is attached to the carburetor by an egr valve adapter between the egr valve adapter and the carburetor is spacer which has gasket on both side emission control svstem description and operation continued requir carburetor port vacuum switch pvs spacer egr valve egr gasket restrictor gasket egr spacer the egr valve is operated by vacuum signal from the carburetor which actuate the valve diaphragm as the vacuum increase sufficiently to overcome the diaphragm spring the valve is opened allowing egr flow the amount of flow is dependent on the location of tapered pintle which is direct result of the vacuum signal emission control svstem description and operation continued egr valve closed diaphragm spring vacuum nipple pintle intake manifold exhaust gas manifold vacuum pintle seat diaphragm diaphragm spring to vacuum pintle intake manifold exhaust gas manifold vacuum pintle seat diaphragm the egr vacuum port is open to vacuum when the throttle plate is opened off idle vacuum is then available to the egr control valve vacuum level decrease as the throttle plate open at closed throttle the egr port is blocked from vacuum at wide open throttle egr vacuum is weak to zero and will cause the egr valve to close for maximum engine power note that at idle when egr vacuum is off the egr system is least needed because nox formation is at minimum throt tle plate off idle position high vacuum flow to egr hlgh intake manifold vacuum vacuum under certain circumstance the egr valve will usually be off these include at idle when the engine need richer mixture when the engine is cold when there is heavy load wide open throttle at idle and at wide open throttle there is no vacuum at the egr vacuum port to control egr at various engine temperature port vacuum switch pvs is used emission control system description and operation continued cold engine warm engine ring is held seated to block port connected to vacuum souf tem when engine warm sensor expand to push plunger up flow ih valve the two port pvs sense engine coolant temperature so that it can cut off vacuum to the egr valve when the engine is cold and connect vacuum to the egr valve when the engine is warm crankcase emission control system the crankcase emission control system consist of an oil filler cap including pcv valve with two connecting hose one of which pass to the inlet manifold and the other to the air cleaner the result is closed ventilation system in which the fume from the crankcase pass back via the inlet manifold into the cylinder for combustion emission control svstem diagnosis and testing of egr system malfunction many problem of poor engine performance are incorrectly diagnosed as being related to the egr valve or egr system therefore it is important to know what engine can be egr related and how to test the system caution improper use of leaded gasoline can plug up an egr valve with deposit that restrict flow poor fuel economy this is an egr condition only if it is related to detonation or some other of restricted or zero egr flow rough idle this can be caused by an egr valve stuck open by dirt on the valve seat or by loose mounting bolt loose mounting will cause vacuum leak and hissing noise stuck valve can be diagnosed by functional test and by visual inspection surge stall or won start can be caused by the valve stuck open detonation spark knock any condition that prevent poor egr gas flow can cause detonation this include valve stuck closed leaking valve diaphragm restriction in flow passage egr disconnected or problem in the vacuum source on engine with high spark advance detonation is serious enough to destroy an engine diagnose the condition by performing the test in this section egr system gas flow test the exhaust gas flow test must be made with the engine idling and hot it tell you if the gas flow passage are open remove the vacuum line from the valve diaphragm plug the line to prevent vacuum leak or dirt entry attach hand vacuum pump to the valve diaphragm apply inch of vacuum to the valve little at time if the gas flow is okay the engine will begin to idle roughly at some point or it may stall if the idle doesn change there is restricted passage in the valve or the spacer remove the valve for inspection if the valve diaphragm fail to hold vacuum install new egr valve hand vacuum pump diaphragm leak test the diaphragm leak test can be performed with the engine off attach hand vacuum pump to the vacuum nipple of the egr valve apply eight inch of vacuum and trap hold it the diaphragm must hold inch of vacuum for at least second if it hold vacuum the diaphragm is okay proceed to the source vacuum test if the diaphragm won hold vacuum replace the egr valve vacl pi apply inch vacuum and trap second hand emission control svstem diagnosis and testing continued egr valve leakage check perform this test if the engine stall idle roughly or run poorly remove and cap the egr valve vacuum hose start the engine if the engine idle quality improve noticeably double check the vacuum hose routing because the valve may have vacuum supply at idle if the enaine idle auatitv doe not imdrove remove the egr valve froh the spacer blodk the egr passage with plate or install known good egr valve start the engine if the idle quality is still bad the problem is elsewhere reinstall the egr valve if the idle quality improve noticeably the egr valve has excessive leakage and should be replaced testing for egr source vacuum occasionally the egr system may fail to operate because the vacuum port on the carburetor is plugged or because of vacuum leak to test for source vacuum remove the egr supply vacuum hose from its connection at the carburetor attach vacuum gauge to the source start the engine and watch the gauge as you momen tarily accelerate to half throttle rpm maximum the vacuum should rise at off idle decrease at half throttle and go to zero at closed throttle if the source vacuum is okay test the pvs and inspect the hose if the source vacuum is not okay check for an obstruction in the carburetor or for vacuum leak caused by loose mounting carburetor egr hose testing the port pvs valve the port pvs valve is okay if it block vacuum from the egr valve when the engine is cold and allow vacuum to get to the valve when the engine is hot warning let the engine cool and release cooling system pressure before removing the valve or you could be burned it also may be necessary to drain out some of the coolant remove both hose connect vacuum gauge to one port and remote vacuum apply inch vacuum to valve operate engine until coolant warm above valve setting this gauge remove both hose from the pvs valve run the engine until the coolant is warmer than the pvs connect vacuum gauge to one port of the pvs valve setting pvs valve are color coded this engine valve and connect vacuum pump to the other has black pvs valve that open at port see if there is vacuum reading on the gauge that is apply io inch of vacuum to the pvs valve connected directly to the pvs valve there should be vacuum reading on the gauge if there is no vacuum replace the pvs valve emission control svstem removal and installation removal remove the vacuum hose that goe to the pvs valve remove the egr hose remove the two egr valve mounting bolt remove the egr valve gasket remove the egr restrictor requir port vacuum sw spacer egr valve egr gasket restrictor gasket egr spacer itch pvs installation seat the egr restrictor into the spacer block small amount of grease may be applied to the egr restrictor prior to installation this will ensure that the egr restrictor doe not become dislodged during the installation of the egr valve gasket and the egr valve do not allow grease to block the hole in the egr restrictor install the egr valve gasket install the egr valve install the two egr valve mounting bolt tighten to nom ft ibs install the egr hose tighten to nom ft ibs attach the vacuum hose that come from the pvs valve charaina svstem section title page
