subject page description and operation generator diagnosis and testing preliminary information isolating the problem battery testing capacity test system connection generator connection electrical testing precaution output voltage test regulator bypass test key off check regulator sense voltage external circuit test field voltage test initial excitation circuit subject page regulator input test generator brush check field ground and open test field voltage test overcharging generator disassembly adjustment belt adjustment removal and installation overhaul disassembly releasing front bearing retainer front or rear bearing removal front bearing installation pressing front bearing onto shaft assembly output voltage test charaina svstem description and operation generator the generator charging system is negative ground system and consist of an generator regulator charge indicator storage battery and associated wiring the present vsg range use prestolite amp or amp generator these unit have self contained regulator mounted directly to the generator case the generator is belt driven from the engine current is supplied from the generator regulator system to the rotating field of the generator through two brush to two slip ring the generator produce power in the form of alternating current the alternating current is rectified to direct current by six diode the generator regulator automatically adjust the generator field current to maintain the generator output voltage within prescribed limit to correctly charge the battery to output terminal excitation resistor pigtail ignition switch field terminal inside regulai or cover field terminal chargina system description and operation continued regulator ground wire tor charging system diagnosis and testing preliminary information before performing charging system test on the engine note the complaint such as slow cranking battery dead or using an excessive amount of water top of battery wet ammeter show charge at all time and or no charge generator warning lamp doe not come on and or never goe out voltmeter show above or below open circuit nominal voltage this information will aid in isolating the part of the system causing the next visually inspect as follow check battery post and battery cable terminal for clean and tight connection remove the battery cable if corroded clean and install them securely check for clean and tight wiring connection at the generator regulator and engine inspect for evidence of arcing check the generator belt tension using belt tension gauge model or equivalent and tighten to specification if necessary isolating the problem battery starting system and light system problem can be caused by poor charging system performance it is also possible to suspect the charging system because of an overload in another area of the electrical system to avoid guesswork it is necessary to isolate the battery the charging system and the electrical circuit to correctly identify the area where the difficulty lie the best method to do this is to check the battery first before any electrical system diagnosis the battery must be in proper state of charge the battery must be operating properly before the other area of the electrical system can perform normally battery testing warning keep battery out of reach of child battery contain sulfuric acid avoid contact with skin eye or clothing also shield your eye when working near the battery to protect against possible splashing of the acid solution in case of acid contact with skin eye or clothing flush immediately with water for minimum of minute if acid is swallowed drink large quantity of milk or water followed by milk of magnesia beaten egg or vegetable oil call physician immediately hydrogen and oxygen gase are produced during normal battery operation this gas mixture can explode if flame spark or lighted tobacco are brought near the battery when charging or using battery in an enclosed space alway provide ventilation and shield your eye warning battery are heavy weighing lbs or more lift them with your leg rather than your back to prevent muscle strain and be careful not to drop them possible breakage or to spill the content sulfuric acid caution volt starting motor can be damaged beyond repair if connected to volt power supply two volt battery in sery or volt motor generator set even when cranking load are relatively light extensive starting motor damage is more likely if the starter is connected to volt supply while being subjected to prolonged heavy cranking load such as attempting to start an engine in subzero temperature test are made on battery to determine the state of charge and also its capacity or ability to crank an engine the ultimate result of these test is to show that the battery is good need recharging or must be replaced before attempting to test battery it is important to give it thorough examination to determine if it has been damaged remove battery cable clamp negative terminal first check for dirty or corroded connection and loose battery post remove hold down and heat shield and inspect for broken or cracked case or cover if the battery is worn or damaged if there is loose or broken post or if there is cracked case or cover replace the battery the battery capacity test should be run next to remove any surface charge prior to determining the state of charge of maintenance free battery capacity test high rate discharge tester rotunda battery starter tester or equivalent in conjunction with voltmeter is used for this test turn the control knob on the battery starter tester to the off position turn the voltmeter selector switch to the volt position and test selector switch to amp connect both positive test lead to the positive battery post and both negative lead to the negative battery post the voltmeter clip must contact the battery post and not the high rate discharge tester clip unless this is done the actual battery terminal voltage will not be indicated turn the load control knob in clockwise direction until the ammeter reach the applicable discharge rate specified in the discharge rate table with the ammeter reading the required load for second note the voltmeter reading avoid leaving the high discharge load on the battery for period longer than second if the voltmeter reading is above the minimum specified in the table with the test equipment for that temperature the battery has good output capacity and will readily accept charge if required check the state of charge if the voltage reading obtained during the capacity test is below the minimum specified in the table check the state of charge charcring system diagnosis and testing continued battery discharge rate ampere hour discharge rate ampere temperature correction chart all battery temperature minimum acceptable load voltage or above io system connection check that all charging system connection cable and wire are sound clean and tight this include battery cable battery connection starter solenoid connection starter ground regulator terminal connection generator wiring harness generator connection check all the harness connection to the generator make sure they are clean and tight inspect the soldered connection to the output diode and the ground diode these connection are visible through the ventilation hole in the rear housing of the generator electrical testing precaution observe the following precaution to avoid permanent damage to the regulator or to the generator diode do not disconnect the generator output lead the ground lead or the battery cable while the engine is running do not disconnect the generator field terminal or the regulator connection while the engine is running do not short the generator field terminal or field lead to ground disconnect the battery ground terminal before removing the generator or the generator brush holder assembly never reverse polarity when charging or replacing the battery output voltage test perform this test if the trouble is warning light indication while running or no voltage indication on the voltmeter this test will check the generator output to verify that the problem is in the charging system and not in the instrument circuit if the regulator is hot do not perform this test until the regulator has cooled down the regulator will respond to and regulate at lower voltage as the temperature rise this offset the tendency toward battery overcharging in warm weather this built in temperature compensation reduce generator output by about one volt per temperature rise key off check the voltage at the generator output terminal it should be battery voltage start the engine with the engine running at to rpm check the voltage at the generator output terminal the voltage should rise to to volt as soon as the starting discharge has been restored generator voltmeter charainrr system diagnosis and testing continued regulator bypass test perform this test if the output voltage test indicate less than volt at light load key off disconnect the field lead from the terminal connect jumper from the terminal to the terminal start the engine caution be sure to keep the rpm low as an unregulated generator can produce voltage high enough to cause damage with the engine running at idle check the output voltage generator voltmeter if the output voltage is still low the problem is in the generator if the output voltage rise above volt with the regulator bypassed check the regulator circuit for low output although the problem may still be partly or entirely in the generator possible generator problem include defective output or field diode excess resistance in the field circuit which include the regulator excess resistance in the charging circuit external to the generator key off check key off remove the jumper between the and terminal if present connect the field lead to the terminal if it is not already corrected check the voltage at the terminal if any voltage is present it indicate shorted output diode or diode this cause undercharging and also allow the battery to discharge through the regulator and field with the engine stopped generator regulator sense voltage start the engine with the engine running at to rpm check the voltage from the terminal to ground generator voltmeter charaina system diagnosis and testing continued with the engine running at to rpm check the voltage from the terminal to ground generator voltmeter even if the output voltage is low the terminal voltage should be within volt of the terminal voltage if the terminal voltage is higher than the output voltage it indicate an open diode or diode and the higher voltage would signal the regulator to reduce output before normal charging voltage could be built up this cause an undercharging condition if the voltage at the terminal is more than volt lower than the output voltage one or more of the field diode is defective in this condition the regulator would normally increase the generator output to bring the sense voltage up to the regulating value and an overcharging condition would result continue with external circuit test external circuit test this test check the circuit between the generator and the battery by measuring the voltage from each battery terminal to ground with the engine running at to rpm measure the voltage from the positive battery terminal to ground voltage at the positive terminal should be the same as the generator output voltage if it is lower by more than volt excess resistance is present in the generator output lead or in the battery positive cable or connection check for wire or cable damage or for loose or dirty connection measure the voltage from the battery negative terminal to ground it should be zero if it is more than volt check the ground circuit the following connection must be clean and tight generator mounting bolt generator and engine mounting surface ground cable connection to the engine and battery field vdltage test this test check for initial excitation voltage at the terminal perform this test if the output voltage did not rise above battery voltage in the output voltage test but did rise with the regulator bypassed make sure the field lead is connected key on measure the field voltage by carefully probing the terminal generator voltmeter caution to avoid grounding the field and causing component damage use taped probe or connect the field lead through jumper to provide meter probe access to the terminal reading of to volt is normal zero volt reading indicate an open in the initial excitation circuit either in the regulator or in the circuit between the battery and the regulator if battery voltage appear at the terminal an open is present in the field circuit either in the coil or in the brush the meaning of voltage reading between these extreme depend on the specific equipment being tested charaina svstem diagnosis and testing continued initial excitation circuit generator regulator excltatlon resistor generator field fuse ignition switch generator warning light instrument if equipped fuse if equipped battery initial excitation voltage reach the field terminal through an excitation lead from the ignition switch to the regulator and an excitation resistor inside the regulator if the circuit is equipped with voltmeter this is the only path if the circuit is equipped with generator warning light the warning light provide an alternate path in parallel with the excitation resistor this excitation function of the warning light is an incidental result of its being positioned in the circuit so as to compare battery and output voltage but it is important because it can mask an open in the excitation circuit normally initial excitation current is drawn through both path but either one will pass sufficient current if the other is open if the excitation resistor is open or higher in value than it should be there will still be excitation current to magnetize the field at startup but the voltage at the field terminal will be lower because of the larger voltage drop through the increased resistance likewise if the warning light bulb is burned out the excitation resistor will provide field current but also at reduced voltage burned out bulb is easily detected if it fail to light when the key is turned on and the other warning light and gauge respond normally regulator input test this test measure excitation supply voltage to the regulator perform this test if the field terminal voltage is less than volt key on carefully probe the yellow wire terminal in the pigtail connector for the regulator excitation lead battery voltage should be present excitation resist yei voltmeter charging system diagnosis and testing continued if battery voltage is not present check the circuit from the ignition switch through the alternator field fuse if so equipped to the pigtail connector for the regulator excitation lead if battery voltage is present at the yellow regulator lead but the regulator output is low replace the regulator generator brush check if the field terminal voltage is more than volt undercharging may be caused by high resistance in the generator field circuit even though generator output was increased in the regulator bypass test even if field circuit resistance is normal it may be combination of low field coil resistance due to partially shorted coil and high brush resistance disconnect the battery ground cable at the battery remove the regulator housing remove the field wire from the field terminal remove the generator brush holder assembly brush assembly inspect the brush for abnormal wear damage dirty contact surface or sticky movement clean and free up the brush or replace the assembly while the brush holder is out release belt tension to allow free rotation of the rotor and clean the slip ring with crocus cloth or fine abrasive field ground and open test this test use an ohmmeter to check for open or short to ground in the field touch the meter probe to either of the slip ring and to ground if the meter show continuity or low resistance there is grounded condition in that case replace the rotor measure the resistance from one slip ring to the other field coil resistance should be to ohm if the ohmmeter indicate high resistance between the slip ring the field is partially open in that case replace the rotor field voltage test with the slip ring clean and known good brush installed repeat the field excitation voltage test at the terminal key on release belt tension to allow the rotor to spin freely while spinning the rotor by hand to assure good brush contact probe the terminal and observe the voltage if it still doe not fall within the to volt range the field coil resistance is incorrect in that case replace the rotor voltmeter generator voltmeter charging system diagnosis and testing continued if the foregoing test sequence doe not identify the cause of undercharging it may be due to low regulator output when the system is operating under load engine on while running the engine at rpm measure the terminal voltage field voltage should be to volt initially and quickly taper off to to volt as the starting discharge is re charged if the voltage is low or fluctuating the regulator is defective in that case replace the regulator overcharging if the output voltage test indicate on overcharging condition greater than volt it usually indicate defective regulator but not alway if the regulator sense voltage at the terminal is lower than the output voltage by more than volt due to defective diode trio good regulator will increase field current and generator output in an effort to bring the sense voltage up to regulating value whether and to what extent the system will overcharge will depend on the nature and degree of the field diode failure as field current must pass through the field diode generator disassembly if the foregoing sery of on equipment test has not identified the cause of charging system malfunction remove and disassemble the generator for further test see overhaul in this section if specialized test equipment is available for checking diode and stator winding under operating load condition follow the equipment manufacturer instruction if this equipment is not available refer the job to the nearest authorized service station charging system adjustment belt adjustment check the belt tension with tool the apply pressure on the generator front housing only and tighten the adjusting arm to generator bolt belt should be within specification specification check the belt tension using tool section adjust the belt for specified tension if the belt is not within specification loosen the tighten all mounting bolt generator mounting bolt to snug position and loosen the adjusting arm bolt charging system removal and installation removal disconnect the battery ground cable loosen the generator mounti ng bolt and remove adjustment arm to generator attaching bolt the remove the electrical connector from the generator disengage the generator belt remove the generator mounting bolt and remove the generator installation install the generator wiring connector position the generator to the engine and install the spacer if used and the generator mounting bolt tighten the bolt only finger tight install the adjustment arm to generator attaching bolt position the belt on the pulley and adjust the belt tension using tool apply pressure on the generator front housing only when tightening the belt tighten the adjusting arm bolt and the mounting bolt connect the battery ground cable charging system overhaul disassembly mark both end housing and the stator with scribe mark for assembly remove retaining nut lockwasher spacer pulley spacer dish washer fan and fan spacer note observe position and direction of spacer and dished washer between pulley and fan bearing support rear bearing with large washer incorporating cut out to accommodate the rotor shaft and press bearing from shaft remove rectifier diode assembly retaining screw and lift out stator and rectifier assembly unsolder stator to rectifier connection using pair of plier as heat sink to reduce heat spread to diode stator releasing front bearing retainer to remove the front bearing which is pressed on the rotor shaft it must first be removed from the front housing it is retained in the housing by snap ring which is accessible from the front when the pulley and fan are removed with pair of snap ring plier squeeze the ear of the snap ring together rock the snap ring out of its groove press or tap the rotor shaft and bearing out of the front housing front or rear bearing removal remove the bearing from the shaft with suitable puller and adapter charaincr svstem overhaul continued front bearing installation bearing installation is not the reverse of the removal procedure because the snap ring which retain the front bearing cannot be installed with the bearing in place on the rotor shaft seat the bearing in the front housing install the snap ring pressing front bearing onto shaft using suitable sleeve such as inch deep socket press the bearing with front housing onto the rotor shaft apply force to the inner race only press the bearing on until the inner race touch the shoulder on the shaft rear bearing recess the rear bearing is not locked into its recess in the rear housing the outer race is prevented from turning by snug fit in the recess plus the added friction of rubber seal ring in groove in the recess replace the seal ring whenever the generator is disassembled assembly resolder stator to rectifier use pair of plier as heat sink to reduce heat spread to diode stator position stator and rectifier assembly in rear housing and install retaining screw press rear bearing onto rotor shaft install front bearing into front housing position retainer plate and install retaining screw install rotor to front housing position the rear housing and stator assembly over the rotor and align the scribe mark made during disassembly seat the machined portion of the stator core into the step in both end housing install the housing through bolt install the fan spacer fan dished washer spacer pulley spacer lockwasher and nut tighten nut charaina svstem overhaul continued note dished washer must be fitted correctly with the outer circumference pressing against the fan in this way it act as vibration damper and prevent fatigue failure output voltage test if system section starting system subject page description and operation testing road service on engine testing starter cranking circuit test starter load test starter solenoid test bench test starter no overhaul disassembly assembly startina svstem description and operation there are two starter available in the vsg range most engine are equipped with the bosch electromagnetic field starter which is detailed in this section some engine are equipped with the united technology permanent magnet starter this starter feature an inertially actuated pinion gear which is described later in this section the electromagnetic field starter is four pole four brush motor with sery field and solenoid operated roller clutch drive the solenoid assembly is mounted to flange on the starter drive housing the entire shift lever mechanism and the solenoid plunger are enclosed in the drive housing thus protecting them from exposure to dirt and road splash the solenoid incorporate two winding pull in winding and hold in winding together they provide sufficient magnetic attraction to pull the solenoid plunger into the solenoid engine cranking occur when the starter solenoid on the starter is energized through the starter control ignition switch when energized the solenoid shift the starting motor pinion into mesh with the engine ring gear simultaneously the main contact of the solenoid are closed and battery current is directed to the starting motor causing the armature to rotate after the engine start the starter drive is disengaged when the ignition switch is returned from the start to the on or run position this open the circuit to the starter solenoid and the solenoid return spring cause the shift lever to disengage the starter drive from the engine ring gear the starting motor is protected from excessive speed by an overrunning clutch incorporated in the starter drive assembly the overrunning clutch permit the drive pinion gear to rotate faster than the armature thus disengaging itself from the engine ring gear when the engine start startina svstem testing road service on engine testing on road service call connect booster battery to the system for case of starter that will not crank the engine or starter that crank the engine very slowly if the starter doe not turn the engine over even with the booster battery attached refer to the following test be certain that correct battery polarity is observed when using booster battery positive to positive and negative to negative connection of the auxiliary cable starter cranking circuit test these test will determine whether or not there is excessive resistance in the cranking circuit make each test connection as shown while cranking the engine observe the voltage drop reading for each test disconnect and ground the high tension lead from the ignition coil to prevent the engine from starting connect remote control switch between the battery terminal of the starter relay and the terminal of the relay starter cranking circuit test the voltage drop in the circuit will be indicated by the voltmeter to volt range maximum allowable voltage drop should be with the voltmeter negative lead connected to the starter terminal and the positive lead connected to the battery positive terminal connection no volt with the voltmeter negative lead connected to the starter terminal and the positive lead connected to the battery terminal of the starter solenoid connection no volt with the voltmeter negative lead connected to the battery terminal of the starter solenoid and the positive lead connected to the positive terminal of the battery connection no volt with the voltmeter negative lead connected to the negative terminal of the battery and the positive lead connected to the engine ground connection no volt starting system testing continued starter load test connect the test equipment as shown be sure that no current is flowing through the ammeter and heavy duty carbon pile rheostat portion of the circuit rheostat at maximum counterclockwise position crank the engine with the ignition off and determine the exact reading on the voltmeter this test is accomplished by disconnecting and grounding the high tension lead from the ignition coil and by connecting jumper from the battery terminal of the starter solenoid to the ignition switch terminal of the solenoid stop cranking the engine and adjust the load of the carbon pile until the voltmeter indicate the same reading as that obtained while the starter cranked the engine the ammeter will indicate the starter current draw under load starter load test starter soqenoio carbon pile rheostat remote control starter switch positive lead starter solenoid test if the solenoid doe not pull in in the starter load test measure the voltage between the starter mounted solenoid switch terminal and ground with the ignition switch closed if the voltage is or more volt worn or damaged solenoid is indicated remove the starter assembly for solenoid replacement bench test starter no load test the starter no load test will uncover such fault as open or shorted winding rubbing armature and bent armature shaft the starter can be tested at no load on the test bench only make the test connection as shown the starter will run at no load be sure that no current is flowing through the ammeter rheostat at maximum counterclockwise position determine the exact reading on the voltmeter disconnect the starter from the battery and adjust the load of the rheostat until the voltmeter indicate the same reading as that obtained while the starter was running the ammeter will indicate the starter no load current draw starter no load bench test startina svstem testing continued armature open circuit test an open circuit armature may sometime be detected by examining the commutator for evidence of burning spot burned on the commutator is caused by an arc formed every time the commutator segment connected to the open circuit winding pass under brush armature and field grounded circuit test the test will determine if the winding insulation has failed permitting conductor to touch the frame or armature core to determine if the armature winding are grounded make the connection as shown if the voltmeter indicate any voltage the winding are grounded grounded field winding can be detected by first disconnecting the grounded end of the winding where it terminate at the frame then making the connection as shown if the voltmeter indicate any voltage the field winding are grounded armature grounded which is actuated by inertial force in place of starter solenoid when the starter is energized inertia cause the pinion gear to move out along spirally threaded shaft and engage the when the engine has started the starter motor is de energized and spring tension cause the pinion gear to move away from the and back to its original position starting system overhaul continued remove the spring system ignition system distributorless governor cooling system lfuel system emission control system specification charging system section governor subject page subject page description and operation adjustment preliminary check oil level belt tension throttle control rod rpm adjustment high speed spread or sensitivity increase spread decrease spread low speed no load surge removal and installation electronic description and operation electronic diagnosis and testing calibration governor description and operation mechanical flyweight type governor and an electronic increase the rotation of the governor shaft increase governor are available for this engine the mechanical centrifugal force cause the weight to move outward as governor is mounted on the right front of the engine and is belt driven from the engine accessory pulley direct mechanical linkage from the governor throttle control lever to the carburetor throttle lever limit carburetor action to the governor setting as the engine speed the rotation of the governor shaft increase however spring retard or limit the movement of the weight until centrifugal force overcome the spring tension at this time the weight are forced outward closing the throttle plate through the linkage to the throttle shaft governor adjustment preliminary check three preliminary check must be made on the mechanical governor before attempting any repair adjustment these are the governor oil level drive belt tension and the throttle control rod length oil level clean the body of the governor in the area of the fill plug remove the oil level plug if oil drip out the level is full if oil doesn drip out remove the oil fill plug and add or engine oil into the fill hole until is start dripping out of the oil level hole install the oil level and oil fill plug belt tension belt tension should be checked on cold belt only install belt tension tool on the drive belt and check the tension belt tension new nom lb ft used nom lb ft used belt is one that has be en in operation for minute or more if adjustment is required loosen the governor adjusting bolt and move the governor until the correct tension is obtained remove the gauge tighten the governor adjusting bolt install the tension gauge and recheck the belt tension throttle control rod manually move the governor throttle lever to the maximum open throttle with spring tension on the governor check the gap between the carburetor throttle shaft lever and its maximum open position stop it should be to inch wide if adjustment is necessary loosen the control rod ball joint locknut remove the rod from the carburetor throttle lever and adjust the length of the rod with the ball joint install the throttle control rod on the carburetor throttle lever and recheck the gap tighten the locknut caution be sure the throttle control rod is properly installed with the long end at the governor to prevent binding on the ignition wire rpm adjustment high speed first attach tachometer to the engine then run the engine until it reach normal operating temperature loosen the locknut on the high speed stop screw disengage engine load slowly pull the throttle to desired maximum engine speed adjust the high speed stop screw on the governor to attain the desired maximum engine speed do not exceed the recommended maximum rpm tighten the locknut spread or sensitivity proper governor operation require difference between full load and no load governed speed too small an rpm spread between the two speed will cause governor hunting and surging too large spread will cause low response for this governor normal rpm spread is approximately rpm with the full load speed range of rpm increase spread with the engine running under no load at maximum governed speed loosen the locknut and adjust the screw to move the spring away from the lever hub tighten the locknut recheck governor operation under full load and no load condition to determine if operation is stabilized and sensitivity is satisfactory readjust the governor high speed stop screw to maintain the correct high speed under load governor adjustment continued adjustment no load surge adjustment screw decrease spread no load surge with the engine under no load at maximum governed the no load surge adjustment is set at the factory and speed loosen the locknut and adjust the screw to rarely require adjustment if necessary this adjustment move the spring toward the lever hub tighten the can be used to prevent hunting and surging and no load locknut speed provided the rpm spread adjustment is set recheck governor operation under load and no load properly make the adjustment with the tachometer installed increase the engine speed with the hand throttle or variable speed lever to rpm lower than the maximum no load desired control rpm condition readjust the governor high speed stop screw to maintain the correct high speed under load low speed attach tachometer and move the hand throttle or variable speed lever to the closed position adjust the carburetor idle speed screw to obtain the desired idle speed loosen the no load surge adjustment screw locknut and turn the screw inward until the rpm increase to the desired control rpm caution do not turn the screw in all the way it will interfere with proper governor operation and prevent the governor from returning the engine to idle speed readjust the governor high speed stop screw to maintain the correct high speed under load governor removal and installation removal installation disconnect hand throttle variable speed lever connection at governor disconnect governor to carburetor throttle control rod at governor loosen governor mounting nut and bolt and governor toward engine to loosen drive belt move remove drive belt from governor pulley remove governor to bracket attaching bolt and remove governor position the governor to the mounting bracket and install the attaching bolt snugly position drive belt to governor pulley and move the governor away from the engine to tighten the belt tighten the attaching bolt adjust the belt tension connect the governor to carburetor throttle control rod adjust the rod as described previously connect hand throttle cable to governor variable speed lever adjust cable as necessary to permit operation from idle to maximum speed governor electronic description and operation bat bat dis ign dis ign actuator actuator spdsela spdselb asd bus tpl the barber colman electronic governor consist of three can be programmed with up to four separate engine main component speed the carburetor and actuator are integrated into controller one unit an actuator carburetor the controller is barber colman model digital unit this controller allow up to four engine speed to be used on one application the operator change the engine speed via run speed select switch mounted on the control panel of the machine these governor are isochronous in operation these is no governor droop the governor maintain the same engine speed from no load to full load operation each controller the carburetor is zenith model offered with either manual or automatic choke it is spring loaded to keep carburetor at its minimum fuel position when no power is applied the feature cause the carburetor to go fully closed in the event of governor power failure the rotary actuator is mounted directly to the end of the throttle shaft this feature eliminate any need for throttle linkage the actuator is electrically connected to the controller through two wire packard connector if the governor is used with any other fuel system such as lpg then linear actuator is available governor electronic diagnosis and testing this governor system is comprised of three major component the carburetor actuator and controller the objective of this document is to help technician identify which component is creating problem so it can be fixed or replaced condition governor appear dead possible source battery power is not supplied to controller action remove the connector from the controller and with voltmeter check for battery power across pin of the wiring harness connector if battery power is not present check wiring to the controller controller is not receiving the speed signal note this controller receive its speed signal from the ignition if the controller is connected to an engine with distributor pin should both be connected to the negative side of the coil or the tachometer output if the controller is connected to an engine with dis ignition pin should be connected to one of the two tachometer output and pin should be connected to the other tachometer output the dis ignition utiliz two coil note do not short the tach lead doing so could damage the spark controller using an ohmmeter check continuity from pin to the termination point if continuity is not present repair the wiring actuator fail to operate remove the pin connector at the actuator with dc voltmeter check between the purple wire and chassis battery ground the voltmeter should read volt volt if the voltage is low disconnect the pin connector from the controller and check continuity between the wiring harness pin to their respective actuator terminal if there is no continuity check for opening in the wire using voltmeter check for battery voltage from controller terminal to chassis battery ground if battery power is not found replace the controller and check the actuator and actuator wiring for ground and short note check wiring before replacing controller bad wiring might destroy the new controller check continuity across actuator wire ohm ohm should be measured check the continuity of actuator wire to the case the ohm measured should be or greater if these value are out of tolerance replace actuator ii engine doesn change speed incorrect engine speed with the engine running connect battery power to pin this will select run if run is set higher speed than run and the speed doe not change replace the controller contact oem for speed setting with the engine running connect battery power to pin this will select run if run is set higher than the previous speed and the speed doe not change replace the controller contact oem for speed setting with the engine running connect battery power to pin this will select run if run is set higher than the previous speed and the speed doe not change replace the controller contact oem for speed setting governor electronic diagnosis and testing continued condition ill engine is hunting possible source engine is misfiring due to idle mixture mis adjustment this cause engine hunt at no load condition engine is misfiring action disconnect the connector from the controller the governor is now disabled and warm engine should be running at mechanical idle of approximately rpm if the engine is not running at the mechanical idle and is governed by an integrated carburetor loosen the three actuator fastener and rotate the actuator to achieve an idle of rpm if the engine is governed by linear actuator adjust the idle speed screw to achieve rpm slowly rotate the idle mixture screw clockwise cw until the engine speed begin to decrease slowly turr the idle mixture screw counterclockwise ccw until the engine speed begin to decrease now turn the idle mixture screw clockwise cw until maximum idle speed is achieved the idle mixture is now adjusted disconnect the connector from the controller and consult the engine repair manual nameplate bat bat dis ign dis ign actuator actuator oi it spdsela spdselb asd bus tpl max actuator select coil bc de actuator actuator spdsela spdselb tach lead battery dis module connector coolina svstem section title page section title page basicengine starting system ignition system distributorless governor fuel system cooling system emission control system specification charging system section cooling system subject page subject page description and operation adjustment drive belt belt tension coolina svstem description and operation the system is of the full flow type with centrifugal pump the thermostat located in the cylinder head control the flow through the system maintaining the proper temperature the coolant flow is from the bottom of the radiator to the pump which deliver it to the cylinder block it then flow through the cored passage to cool the entire length of each cylinder wall upon reaching the rear of the cylinder block the coolant is directed upward into the cylinder head where seat it cool the combustion chamber valve and valve the coolant from the cylinder head flow past the thermostat if it is open through the coolant outlet housing and into the top of the radiator another passage in the head route the warm coolant through the intake manifold to help atomize the fuel mixture and then through hose to the inlet hose of the water pump adjustment cooling system drive belt belt tension the fan drive belt should be properly adjusted at all time install the belt tension tool on the drive belt and check the tension loose drive belt can cause improper generator fan and water pump operation belt that is too tight place severe strain on the water pump and the generator bearing properly tensioned drive belt minimiz noise and also prolong the service life of the belt therefore it is recommended that belt tension gauge be used to check and adjust the belt tension any belt that has been operated for minimum of minute is considered used belt and when adjusted it must be adjusted to the used tension shown in the specification if adjustment is necessary loosen the generator mounting and adjusting arm bolt move the generator toward or away from the engine until the correct tension is obtained remove the gauge tighten the generator adjusting arm and mounting bolt install the tension gauge and recheck the belt tension gauge belt or belt wecification section title page section title page basic engine starting system recommendation the unit has been filled with break in engine oil at the factory use high quality detergent oil classi fied for service cc sd se or sf detergent oil keep the engine cleaner and reduce carbon deposit use oil having the following sae viscosity rating based on the ambient temperature range anticipated before the next oil change engine lubrication system type of oil pump gear oil filter full flow cartridge crankcase oil capacity qts coolant use mixture of half low silicate ethylene glycol base antifreeze and half soft water use only soft water and only low silicate antifreeze if desired you may add high quality rust inhibitor to the recommended coolant mixture when adding coolant alway add the recommended mixture cooling system type pressurized closed recovery coolant capacity system gal engine gal do not remove the radiator pressure cap while the engine is hot or serious burn from boiling liquid or steam could result ethylene glycol base antifreeze is poisonous do not use your mouth to siphon coolant from the radiator recovery bottle or any container wash your hand thoroughly after handling never store used antifreeze in an open container because animal are attracted to the smell and taste of antifreeze even though it is poisonous to them do not use any chromate base rust inhibitor with ethylene glycol base antifreeze or chromium hydroxide green slime will form and cause overheating engine that have been operated with chromate base rust inhibitor must be chemically cleaned before adding ethylene glycol base antifreeze using any high silicate antifreeze booster or additive also will cause overheating we also recommend that you do not use any soluble oil inhibitor for this equipment danger temperature oil grade recommended above sae or to to sae or below sae or all season sae liter gas engine service recommendation periodic maintenance schedule service schedule authorized operator maintenance function every month or hour whichever come first test standby generator system inspect battery and cable check engine oil level check gearbox oil level if so equipped check engine coolant level check generator ground connection test inspect starting aid every three month or every hour whichever come first inspect and test fuel system and connection inspect exhaust system inspect test fuel supply system authorized service technician maintenance function after first hour of operation inspect wiring change engine crankcase oil and oil filter inspect engine fan belt inspect battery and cable every six month or every hour whichever come first change engine oil and filter lubricate engine control service engine air cleaner service engine fuel filter inspect ac generator test engine safety control inspect fan belt check engine coolant level inspect engine cooling system hose check optional starting aid check battery check engine compression check electrical connection check test annunciator panel perform operational test annually or every hour whichever come first check engine valve clearance test fuel injection nozzle test injection timing inspect all wiring test engine starter operation drain water from fuel tank retorque fan bolt drain and refill gearbox if so equipped every two year replace all rubber hose replace engine fan belt inspect the standby generator system drain flush refill cooling system every operating hour inspect engine dc alternator inspect engine starter retorque engine mounting bracket remove test fuel injection pump remove test cooling system thermostat as required bleed engine fuel damage the camera do not use strong or abrasive detergent when cleaning the camera body use dry cloth to clean the camera when dirty in case the dirt is hard to remove use mild detergent and wipe camera toward the sun do not aim the camera at bright object whether the camera is in use or not never aim it at the sun or other extremely bright object otherwise blooming or smear may be caused do lcd screen instruction manual ww english safety precaution note the illustration of the display and the panel appearing in this manual are example used to explain more clearly how the control are used therefore what appear on the display in the illustration may differ from what appear on the display on the actual equipment and some of the illustration on the display may represent something impossible in actual operation cleaning the unit if this unit is stained wipe it with dry soft cloth such as silicon cloth if this unit is stained badly wipe the stain off with cloth moistened with neutral cleaner then wipe it again with clean soft dry cloth applying spray cleaner directly to the unit may affect its mechanical part wiping this unit with hard cloth or using volatile liquid such as thinner or alcohol may scratch the surface or erase character screen brightness during low temperature when the temperature of the unit fall such as during winter the liquid panel screen will become darker than usual normal brightness will return after using the monitor for while warning to prevent injury or fire take the following precaution to prevent short circuit never put or leave any metallic object such as coin or metal tool inside the unit do not touch the liquid fluid if the lcd is damaged or broken due to shock the liquid fluid may be dangerous to your health or even fatal if the liquid fluid from the lcd contact your body or clothing wash it off with soap immediately caution to prevent damage to the machine take the following precaution make sure to ground the unit to negative dc power supply do not install the unit in spot exposed to direct sunlight or excessive heat or humidity also avoid place with too much dust or the possibility of water splashing do not use your own screw use only the screw provided if you use the wrong screw you could damage the unit if smoke or abnormal odor is detected turn off the unit immediately and contact the dealer using the unit continuously can result in fire or other accident be sure to have the unit repaired by the dealer if you repair the unit fire or other accident can result english component name and function gasket the engine has several area where the form in place gasket fipg is in use to ensure that the gasket fully serve its purpose it is necessary to observe some precaution when applying the gasket bead size continuity and location are of paramount importance too thin bead could cause leak too thick bead on the other hand could be squeezed out of location causing blocking or narrowing of the fluid feed line to eliminate the possibility of leak from joint therefore it is absolutely necessary to apply the gasket evenly without break while observing the correct bead size the fipg used in the engine is room temperature vulcanisation rtv type and is supplied in gram tube part no or since the rtv harden as it react with the moisture in the atmospheric air it is normally used in the metallic flange area the fipg part no can be used for sealing both engine oil and coolant while part no can only be used for engine oil sealing disassembly the part assembled with the fipg can be easily disassembled without use of special method in some case however the sealant between the joined surface may have to be broken by striking with mallet or similar tool flat and thin gasket scraper may be hammered in between the joined surface in this case however care must be taken to prevent damage to the joined surface surface preparation thoroughly remove all substance deposited on the gasket application surface using gasket scraper or wire brush check to ensure that the surface to which the fipg is to be applied is flat make sure that there are no oil grease and foreign substance deposited on the application surface do not forget to remove the old sealant remaining in the bolt hole form in place gasket application fipg when assembling part with the fipg you must observe some precaution but the procedure is very simple as in the case of conventional pre cut gasket applied fipg bead should be of the specified size and without break also be sure to encircle the bolt hole circumference with completely continuous bead the fipg can be wiped away unless it is hardened while the fipg is still moist in less than minute mount the part in position when the part are mounted make sure that the gasket is applied to the required area only the fipg application procedure may vary on different area observe the procedure described in the text when applying the fipg dec motor belt badly worn tooth initial stage canva is worn fluffy canva fibre are visible rubber is gone and colour has changed to white canva texture is not clear last stage canva is worn out and rubber measured value exceed the standard value replace the tensioner standard value mm or less installation service point aa camshaft sprocket bolt using the special tool hold the camshaft sprocket torque read it before using your projector english en caution risk of electric shock do not open caution to reduce the risk of electric shock do not remove cover or back no user serviceable part inside refer servicing to qualified service personnel the lightning flash with arrowhead symbol within an equilateral triangle is intended to alert the user to the presence of uninsulated dangerous voltage within the product enclosure that may be of sufficient magnitude to constitute risk of electric shock the exclamation point within an equilateral triangle is intended to alert the user to the presence of important operating and maintenance servicing instruction in the literature accompanying the appliance warning to prevent fire or shock hazard do not expose this appliance to rain or moisture caution to prevent electric shock do not use this polarized plug with an extension cord receptacle or other outlet unless the blade can be fully inserted to prevent blade exposure note since this projector is pluggable equipment the socket outlet shall be installed near the equipment and shall be easily accessible warning use the attached specified power supply cord if you use another power supply cord it may cause interference with radio and television reception use the attached rgb cable and rs cable with this equipment so as to keep interference within the limit of fcc class device this apparatus must be grounded do not look directly into the len when the projector is in the power on mode caution not for use in computer room as defined in the standard for the protection of electronic computer data processing equipment ansi nfpa when using the projector in europe compliance notice this projector comply with the requirement of the ec directive eec emc directive as amended by directive eec and eec and eec low voltage directive as amended by directive eec the electro magnetic susceptibility has been chosen at level that gain proper operation in residential area on business and light industrial premise and on small scale enterprise inside as well as outside of the building all place of operation are characterised by their connection to the public low voltage power supply system warning use the attached rgb cable and rs cable with this equipment so as to keep interference within the limit of an class device please follow warning instruction en english content important interference received including interference that may cause undesired operation en important safeguard please read all these instruction regarding your projector and retain them for future reference follow all warning and instruction marked on the projector read instruction all the safety and operating instruction should be read before the appliance is operated retain instruction the safety and operating instruction should be retained for future reference warning all warning on the appliance and in the operating instruction should be adhered to instruction all operating instruction must be followed cleaning unplug this projector from the wall outlet before cleaning it do not use liquid aerosol cleaner use damp soft cloth for cleaning attachment and equipment never add any attachment and or equipment without the approval of the manufacturer as such addition may result in the risk of fire electric shock or other personal injury water and moisture do not use this projector near water or in contact with water accessory do not place this projector on an unstable cart stand tripod bracket or table use only with cart stand tripod bracket or table recommended by the manufacturer or sold with the projector any mounting of the appliance should follow the manufacturer instruction and should use mounting accessory recommended by the manufacturer power source this projector should be operated only from the type of power source indicated on the marking label if you are not sure of the type of power please consult your appliance dealer or local power company power cord protection power supply cord should be routed so that they are not likely to be walked on or pinched by item placed upon or against them pay particular attention to cord at plug convenience receptacle and point where they exit from the appliance do not put the power cord under carpet overloading do not overload wall outlet and extension cord as this can result in fire or electric shock object and liquid never push object of any kind through opening of this projector as they may touch dangerous voltage point or short out part that could result in fire or electric shock never spill liquid of any kind on the projector servicing do not attempt to service this projector by yourself refer all servicing to qualified service personnel damage requiring service unplug this projector from the wall outlet and refer servicing to qualified service personnel under the following condition if the power supply cord or plug is damaged if liquid has been spilled or object have fallen into the projector if the projector doe not operate normally after you follow the operating instruction adjust only those control that are covered by the operating instruction an improper adjustment of other control may result in damage and may often require extensive work by qualified technician to restore the projector to its normal operation if the projector has been exposed to rain or water if the projector has been dropped or the cabinet has been damaged if the projector exhibit distinct change in performance this indicate need for service replacement part when replacement part are required be sure that the service technician has used replacement part specified by the manufacturer or part having the same characteristic as the original part unauthorized substitution may result in fire electric shock or other hazard safety check upon completion of any service or repair to this projector ask the service technician to perform safety check determining that the projector is in safe operating condition an appliance and cart combination should be moved with care quick stop excessive force and uneven surface may cause the appliance and cart combination to overturn ventilation slot and opening in the cabinet are provided for ventilation ensuring reliable operation of the projector and to protect it from overheating do not block these opening or allow them to be blocked by placing the projector on bed sofa rug or bookcase ensure that there is adequate ventilation and that the manufacturer instruction have been adhered to en englishwarning unplug immediately if there is something wrong with your projector do not operate if smoke strange noise or odor come out of your projector it might cause fire or electric shock in this case unplug immediately and contact your dealer never remove the cabinet this projector contain high voltage circuitry an inadvertent contact may result in an electric shock except as specifically explained in the user manual do not attempt to service this product by yourself please contact your dealer when you want to fix adjust or inspect the projector do not modify this equipment it can lead to fire or electric shock do not keep using the damaged projector if the projector is dropped and the cabinet is dam aged unplug the projector and contact your dealer for inspection it may lead to fire if you keep using the damaged projector do not face the projector len to the sun it can lead to fire use correct voltage if you use incorrect voltage it can lead to fire do not place the projector on uneven surface place the projection on leveled and stable surface only please do not place equipment on unstable surface do not look into the len when it is operating it may hurt your eye never let child look into the len when it is on do not touch the air outlet grille and bottom plate which become hot do not touch them or put other equipment in front of the air outlet grille the air outlet grille and bottom plate when heated may cause injury or damage to other equipment also do not set the projector on the desk which is easily affected by heat do not look into the air outlet grille when projector is operating heat dust etc may blow out of it and hurt your eye do not block the air inlet and outlet grille if they are blocked heat may be generated inside the projector causing deterioration in the projector quality and fi re place of installation for safety sake refrain from setting the projector at any place subjected to high temperature and high humidity please maintain an operating temperature humidity and altitude as specified below operating temperature between and operating humidity between and never put any heat producing device under the projector so that the projector doe not overheat do not attach the projector to place that is unstable or subjected to vibration do not install the projector near any equipment that produce strong magnetic field also refrain from installing near the projector any cable carrying large current place the projector on solid vibration free surface otherwise it may fall causing serious injury to child or adult and serious damage to the product do not stand the projector it may fall causing serious injury and damage to the projector slanting the projector more than right and left or front and rear may cause trouble or explosion of the lamp when you use the projector in tilted position however secure it to prevent it from slipping down do not place the projector near air conditioning unit or heater to avoid hot air to the exhaust and ventilation hole of the projector zinc or alkaline manganese dioxide type battery should be used dispose of used battery according to your local regulation battery may explode if misused do not recharge disassemble or dispose of them up the screen install the screen perpendicularly to the projector if the screen can not be installed in such way adjust the projection angle of the projector see below install the screen and projector so that the projector len is placed at the same height and horizontal position of the screen center do not install the screen where it is exposed to direct sunlight or lighting light directly reflecting on the screen make the projected image whitish and hard to view basic setup determine the distance from the screen to the projector according to the size of the image to be projected see page do not place this projector on carpet or blanket because the exhaust vent and the intake vent on the bottom surface are blocked and the inside of the projector is heated causing breakdown or fire depending on the installation condition warm air that is emitted from the exhaust vent may flow into the intake vent causing the projector to display temperature and then stop projecting image adjusting the projection angle this projector is provided with foot for adjusting the projection angle on the bottom surface adjust the projection angle depending on the position of the projector adjustment of the projection angle for the best projection project image on flat screen installed at degree to the floor if necessary tilt the projector using adjustment foot on the bottom of the projector tilt up the projector to the appropriate angle rotate the adjustment foot for fine adjustment after using the projector put the adjustment foot back into the projector by rotating the adjustment foot you can adjust the vertical and horizontal position of the displayed image using the len shift but ton see page keystone adjustment to correct vertical and horizontal keystone distor tion press the keystone button on the projector or the remote control to display keystone and adjust the image by pressing the or button or volume or button on the remote control you can correct the vertical and horizontal key stone at the same time however their adjust ment range are limited in such correction when the keystone adjustment is carried out the adjustment value is indicated note that this value doesn mean projection angle the allowable range of the adjustment value in the keystone adjustment vary depending on the installation condition when the keystone adjustment take effect the resolution decrease in addition stripe may appear or straight line may bend in image with complicated pattern they are not due to product malfunction noise may appear on the screen during the key stone adjustment because of the type of the video signal being projected and the setting value of the keystone adjustment in such case set the keystone adjustment value in the range where the image is displayed without noise when the keystone adjustment is carried out the image may not be displayed correctly because of the type of input signal displayed image may be distorted during keystone adjustment for proper keystone adjustment result reset the len to the factory adjusted position using len shift reset in the installation menu before carrying out keystone adjustment see page power steady green or steady red steady red steady green steady red steady red blinking green red blinking green red status blinking orange blinking green blinking green red blinking red steady red off steady or blinking condition abnormal temperature the ventilation grille are clogged with dust or dirt the room temperature is high the protection circuit is working or the lamp is in an abnormal condition lamp operation time warning total lamp operation time reached hour while the power was on lamp operation time warning total lamp operation time reached hour while the power was off lamp operation time warning total lamp operation time reached hour while the power was off the lamp cover isn closed abnormal possible solution clean the ventilation grille relocate the projector to cooler place wait until the status indicator goe out then press the power button if the above solution is not effective replace the lamp replace the lamp change with new lamp if this problem recur close the cover correctly contact your dealer duration when lamp mode of the installation menu is set to low when it is set to standard this duration will be shorted duration when lamp mode is set to standard this duration will be shorted up to hour this projector has indicator each of which show the operation condition of the projector the following offer solution to possible problem if these problem persist turn the projector off and consult your dealer normal condition abnormal condition power indicator status indicator power steady red steady green steady green status off blinking green steady green condition stand by lamp stand by the power is on normal condition note the power button cannot be used to switch between on and off en replacing the lamp this projector is equipped with lamp to project image this lamp is consumable it may burn out or its brightness may decrease during use in such case replace the lamp with new one as soon as possible be sure to replace the lamp with new lamp separately sold that is exclusive to this projector contact your dealer for purchase of the lamp warning be careful not to drop the lamp fixing screw into the inside of the projector don put metal piece or flammable object into the projector using the projector with any foreign object present inside may result in electric shock or fire when you can remove the foreign object from the inside contact your dealer for removal be sure to install the lamp securely when the lamp isn installed properly it won illuminate improper installation may also result in fire don shake the removed lamp or don lift it above your face you may be injured in your eye by flying or falling glass fragment don replace the lamp immediately after use because the lamp cover is very hot you may get burned turn off the power using the power button after the lamp goe out wait about minute until the intake and exhaust fan for cooling the lamp stop rotating then turn off the main power switch and unplug the power cord and wait at least hour to allow the lamp to cool down enough to the touch do not touch the lamp directly it may break and cause injury or burn interval of lamp replacement the recommended interval of lamp replacement is about hour of consecutive use such interval differ depending on the operating environment and may be shorter than hour decrease in luminescence and or color brightness indicate that the lamp need to be replaced when the lamp operation time exceed hour the indicator will blink alternately between green and red while the lamp is lit when the lamp is not lit the indicator will be lit red only and the lamp replacement message will appear on the screen for minute everytime the lamp is turned on when the lamp has been used for about hour the replacement message lamp exchange will appear on the screen for minute every hour thereafter when the lamp operation time exceed hour the projector automatically shut off and can be used until the lamp is replaced and the lamp operation time is reset duration when lamp mode of the installation menu is set to low when it is set to standard this duration will be shorted duration when lamp mode is set to standard this duration will be shorted to hour caution don remove the lamp for any purpose other than replacement unnecessary removal of the lamp may result in breakdown this projector use high pressure mercury lamp as the light source the high pressure mercury lamp may explode or fail to illuminate permanently because of an impact scratch or deterioration through use the period of time until explosion or permanent failure to illuminate vary considerably from lamp to lamp depending on operation condition therefore the lamp may explode soon after the start of use the possibility of explosion of the lamp increase if you use it beyond its replacement period when the instruction of lamp replacement appear replace it with new one immediately even if the lamp is still lighting normally when the high pressure mercury lamp explode glass fragment will scatter inside or outside the lamp box and the gas charged inside the lamp will diffuse inside or outside the projector the gas inside the lamp contain mercury be careful not to breathe it or avoid it from entering your eye or mouth if you should breathe it or if it should enter your eye or mouth see doctor immediately in the event that the high pressure mercury lamp exploded glass fragment may have been scattered inside the projector ask your dealer to replace the lamp and to inspect the inside of the projector when you clean or replace the lamp by yourself be sure to hold the handle of the lamp you may get injured by glass fragment don replace the lamp while the projector is installed on the ceiling glass fragment may fall from the projector entering your eye or mouth causing injury when placing the projector upside down to replace the lamp or conduct maintenance ensure that the projector is securely placed before working with it en english to replace the lamp reverse the projector gently loosen the screw using phillip screwdriver and remove the lamp cover loosen the screw using phillip screwdriver pull up the handle pull out the lamp box by the handle while holding the projector pull the lamp box out of the projector slowly if it is pulled out quickly the lamp may break scattering glass fragment do not spill liquid on the removed lamp box or do not place it near any flammable object or where child can reach to prevent injury or fire load new lamp box securely into the projector in the right orientation put the handle back to the original position make sure that the handle is locked tighten up the screw using phillip screwdriver tighten up the screw using phillip screwdriver to secure the lamp cover reset of the lamp operation time plug the power cord into the wall outlet and turn on the main power switch press the and power button on the control panel at the same time important unless these button are pressed at the same time the lamp operation time isn reset make sure that the status indicator is lit twice and the lamp operation time is reset successfully the projector doesn turn on if the lamp cover isn attached securely be sure to reset the lamp operation time whenever you replace the lamp do not reset the lamp operation time unless you replace the lamp the lamp is fragile if it is broken be careful not to cut yourself with glass fragment contact your dealer for new lamp this projector use lamp that contain mercury disposal of the lamp or the projector with the lamp may be regulated due to environmental consideration for disposal or recycling information please contact your local authority or the electronic industry alliance org for the only maintenance caution be sure to turn off the lamp and unplug the power cord from the wall outlet before you perform any maintenance on the projector cleaning of the projector and the ventilation slot use soft cloth to clean the projector and the ventilation grille when the grille become dirty wipe them with soft cloth dampened with diluted mild detergent and then wipe them with dry cloth to protect the surface of the projector from fading or deteriorating do not spray insecticide on or inside the projector do not use benzine or thinner to clean it do not let rubber or plastic material contact with projector cleaning of the len use standard len cleaning brush or piece of len tissue dampened with len cleaning fluid the len surface is fragile use only recommended nonabrasive len cleaning material do not touch the len with your finger en power off steady red blinking red green status off off blinking orange blinking green steady red off steady or blinking solution connect the power cord to the projector plug the power cord into wall outlet if any object are blocking the air inlet or outlet grille remove them and carry out the following turn off the main power swich make sure that the projector has cooled off turn on the main power swich press the power button if any object are blocking the air inlet or outlet grille remove them don expose the air outlet grille to hot air emitted from heating appliance after the status indicator stop blinking press the power button if the main power switch is turned off while the fan is rotating the lamp may not turn on about minute when the main power switch is turned on next time the lamp can turn on again for minute after it goe out press the power button several time replace the lamp the lamp is at the end of its life attach the lamp cover on the bottom unplug the power cord from the wall outlet and contact your dealer turn on the main power swich troubleshooting before asking for repair of the projector check the following if the persist stop using the projector be sure to unplug the power plug and then contact your dealer no image appear on the screen problem power can not be turned on no image appear on the screen projected image suddenly disappear solution check whether the indicator are on or off and how they are lightning cancel the av mute by pressing the mute button check that the len cap is removed it may take about minute for the lamp to light up the lamp fail to light up on rare occasion in such case wait for few minute and then try again if the projector was turned off before the lamp was cooled down sufficiently last time the fan may start rotating and the power button may not work after the main power switch is turned on after the fan stop rotating press the power button to turn back on the power indicator set scart input in the feature menu to off see page check that the intake vent is clean check that the lamp cover is closed see page check that the cable connected to the external device are not broken intake vent and or exhaust vent are blocked in such case the status indicator brink orange remove the object blocking the intake vent and or exhaust vent and then carry out the following procedure that customer pet child and you are not exposed to any live electrical supply insulation test the following test must be performed prior to and upon completion of all repair to dyson floorcare product and before any functional check you must ensure that full visual inspection of the product is completed prior to repair these test are vital to avoid any possibility of personal injury to the end user the seaward primetest or equivalent should be used to test the electrical insulation of class appliance it indicate any electrical leakage insulation test point test the two screw highlighted in the upright switch cover test result an insulation test reading of mq is acceptable reading of below mq is not considered safe and further investigation rectification and testing must be completed before the product is used the following component must be visually inspected the wiring behind the upright switch cover all wiring behind the switch housing test the two screw highlighted in the rear of the duct assembly if you cannot repair product with an insulation test reading of below you must inform the customer that the product is unsafe to use please inform the customer of the required action to repair the product including the charge structure if the product is left un repaired please indicate on your paperwork hand held device that the product is electrically unsafe and attach warning product electrically unsafe sticker in visable location on the product technical info technical info dc service manual wiring schematic brushbar switch switch area upright switch main on off switch diagnosis when is switched on in the upright position the brushbar motor is off tilting the machine into normal vacuuming mode will automatically operate the upright switch turning the brushbar motor on the brushbar motor can be switched off using the brushbar switch the vacuum motor of is fitted with heat sensitive thermal cut out tco this will shut the motor down for up to minute if it reach temperature degree celcius excessive temperature within the motor are usually caused by machine filter blockage the cleaner head is fitted with tco reset switch that will shut the brushbar motor off if it is put under an excessive load blocked seized brushbar etc the cause of the shut down should be cleared and the tco reset by pressing the switch if the cause of the brushbar motor cut out is not rectified the tco will repeatedly reactivate preventing the brushbar from turning electrical fault diaignosis note all connection number and continuity check refer to the illustration and table on machine have read and understood this instruction manual and that they work in accordance with all valid provision and regulation if an individual working with the machine detect any safety defect than signal word this indicate special situation or an activity required for proper machine handling failure to follow these instruction may cause disruption to the machine or to its surrounding height when at the correct loading height the ladder will be at good angle and the correct distance from the ground so that the operator can easily access the platform use the provided access way when approaching the platform adjust the level guard location of the level guard figure the height of the level guard can be adjusted so that the sensor can be moved between the different bracket when drilling higher rate or large size product the level guard can be placed higher up in the hopper when drilling low rate or small size product the level guard can be placed at lower level on the hopper disassembly and assembly turn the level guard counter clockwise and loosen from the bracket turn the level guard clockwise to assemble on to the other bracket level scale for seed scale with interval of is installed inside the hopper to measure the level of the product the scale has limit of en gb filling and emptying opening the discharge door figure before opening the discharge door check that
