with work using the hopper and when performing service and maintenance the machine must be supported safely on stand for all work underneath the machine or when there is risk of crush injury ensure also that the surface supporting the trestle is firm enough to support them alway lower the machine to the ground prior to performing any service on the hydraulic system depressurise the hydraulic system before service and maintenance the machine should be in the lowered position and standing on flat surface when carrying out service and maintenance alway switch off the ipad control when performing maintenance and service work on the machine regular maintenance to preserve the high quality of the drill alway use genuine derstad spare part perform maintenance check at regular interval and at the end of the season check that there is no seed or any bit of packaging remaining in the biodrill hopper seed feed housing rotor or feed system check that there has been no seed or any bit of packaging in the seed hose or in the outlet of the distributor head check that no seed has run down into the ejector tube and associated air hose check that the seed hose and coupling are not trapped or damaged cleaning cleanliness is of vital importance when undertaking any kind of work with the machine hydraulic system wipe with clean paper or cloth place part on clean surface not directly on the work bench rinse before assembly for example with degreasing agent never rinse the bearing using high pressure water supply it is important to grease the bearing after washing in order to remove any water that has remained behind never clean electrical electronic component directly with high pressure water jet clean electrical electronic component by blow ing them with air or by wiping them with dampened cloth exercise extreme caution when washing with high pressure water in the area around the seed feed housing after cleaning air and dry all component carefully before reassembly keep the fan running for while after cleaning so that the whole system is blown dry any remaining seed that sprout can block the air and seed hose seed can also attract small rodent which can damage the seed drill clean biodrill hopper rotor seed feed housing seed hose the tramlining motor and outlet in the distributor head clean the seed feed housing open and disassemble according to seed meter allow all the part to dry thoroughly prior to reassembly clean the radar en gb maintenance and service seed transport distributor head flex figure open the cover by loosening and removing the six wing nut replace the distributor head tramlining motor replace the distributor head tramlining motor if needed figure to remove faulty motor from the distributor head loosen and remove the three screw remove the rubber lid and the three electrical screw on the circuit board use the torx screwdriver supplied fit new motor with the three electric screw and the three electric screw use the torx screwdriver supplied to tighten the electrical screw assemble the rubber lid repair and replace the seed hose figure repair if seed hose is damaged due to abrasion or folding it can be jointed with sleeve cut the hose at the centre of the fold or damage where necessary the smallest possible part of the hose can be cut away if the hose become too stiff at the joint and cannot be bent sufficiently when the machine is lowered it may be necessary to replace the entire seed hose or to replace part of the hose and joint it in two place changing seed hose use soapy water on the hose when fitting them to seed coulter twist the hose anti clockwise when removing or fitting this will help the spiral reinforcement to open the hose little cut replacement hose to the same length as the piece it is to replace seed meter biodrill is fitted with an electrically powered fenix seed feed housing monitoring and manoeuvring can be done using control setting sliding hatch figure ac ba en gb maintenance and service when the sliding hatch located beneath the sealing cover is pressed in the supply from the hopper to the metering unit is blocked when the supply from the hopper is blocked it is possible to replace the roller or perform service on the seed feed housing even though the hopper is full during drilling the sliding hatch must alway be completely open and the wing nut tightened disassembly and assembly figure open the catch and turn the motor in clockwise direction remove the motor from the seed feed housing changing feed roller before changing the roller it may be necessary to empty the lower part of the seed feed housing to gather up the seed figure loosen the locking pin and pull out the cotter pin pull the plug out figure separate the roller and the motor switch to the required roller note that there are two groove along the motor shaft that must match the roller note that the roller can only be attached in one direction fit the output motor after cleaning carefully air and dry all component in the seed hopper before reassembly figure check that the seed feed housing is empty clean inside when necessary clean outside the flange on the outside of the housing en gb maintenance and service figure insert the motor and roller in the seed feed housing the motor can only be fitted with the motor cable downward and the arrow upward turn the motor counter clockwise until it click into place remove the plug in the seed feed housing loosen the locking pin and pull out the cotter pin in figure pull out the plug fit the plug in the seed feed housing figure attach the plug to the seed feed housing attach the plug in the correct direction check that the pinhole in the plug is in line with the pinhole in the plate check that the entire plug is inserted completely the flat surface of the plug must be on the same level as that of the profile assemble the cotter pin assemble the locking pin clean the feed system the feed system on the drill should be cleaned regularly and alway at the end of the season seed residue attract small rodent when in storage for long period when not in use the machine should be stored indoor to protect the machine electronic instrument these electronic instrument are of very high standard and have high tolerance for moisture we nonetheless recommend storage indoor make sure that the seed drill has been emptied and thoroughly cleaned the feed roller should be completely dry before being put into storage and should be kept in dry dark and rodent free place when the machine is not in use en gb troubleshooting decal carefully read through these instruction and ensure that you have understood their content refer to these instruction and safety pro vision as needed when working alway ensure that the implement entire working and extension area is alway kept clear never walk under suspended section make sure that the wing section are locked when folded up for road transport parking and or maintenance check that the functionality of the locking hook is not impeded by soil and plant residue warning tape beware of crush or shock hazard also used on safety component never work under the machine unless it has been properly secured with jack stand or other strong support on firm surface lock the lifting cylinder using the appropri ate yellow locking device crushing risk warning cfe en gb general safety provision alway make sure that the working area of the bout marker are free from obstruction bear in mind that there is risk of injury when the bout marker are being unfolded and risk of crushing between the seed drill and the bout marker when they are being retracted note the bout marker are alway retracted when the machine is lifted no matter what is shown on the control unit the indicated bout marker are alway extended when the machine is lowered for this reason alway switch off the control unit when the machine is not in the field setting and machine data are saved when the control unit is switched off do not climb on the machine wheel or packer as these may rotate even when the machine is stationary make sure nobody is on the drill during operation make sure nobody is on the seed drill when front loading seed and or fertiliser the machine ladder step and platform are not intended to be used for manual loading from small seed bag extra transport height warning beware of overhead cable via duct gate and tree etc alway check the maximum permitted navigable height warning against cutting oil jet as the hydraulic system contain accumulator exercise extreme caution when disconnecting hy draulic coupling from the machine never point hydraulic coupling at any part of the body empty the accumulator of oil before service and repair do not stand on the machine grating do not stand between the tractor and the machine when the tractor is being reversed for hitching jack en gb general safety provision safety instruction safety instruction during installation and maintenance alway park the implement on level and stable surface depressurise the machine until it rest on its wheel disc and the support stand keep in mind that the ground pressure under the support stand is considerable especially when the hopper is full never stand under the intermediate packer or seed drill if the intermediate packer is raised and only secured with the tractor hydraulic lifting arm prior to any service on the intermediate packer be sure to secure it properly with trestle etc on stable surface any welding work on the machine must be carried out to professional standard note that poor welding may result in serious personal injury or fatality if you are unsure contact professional welding technician for instruction on machine that are delivered packaged you will find two packing box of machine part in the seed hopper the packaging for these packing box can get damp in transit check the packaging in the seed hopper when unloading if the packaging is damp or wet the content must be unloaded by hand regularly inspect the tractor hitch device and the machine towing eye for wear replace the towing eye when it has been worn down to the wear limit the machine is hydraulically pressurised when the tractor is connected and in operation alway shut down the tractor and disconnect the feed electrical system when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill prior to connecting the hydraulic hose alway make sure the seed drill and the tractor coupling are clean and free of dirt to maintain the machine high level of quality and operational safety use only derstad genuine spare part the warranty and any claim under it will become void if part other than genuine part are used safety instruction during transport the owner operator has sole responsibility when transporting the seed drill on public road ensure that at least of the tractor weight is still being carried by its front wheel when the seed drill has been connected loaded this is to ensure that the steering ability of the whole vehicle is maintained good judgement and extreme caution should be exercised when transporting the seed drill on public road we recommend the use of tractor with gross weight that is at least equal to the gross weight of the seed drill if the seed drill is not equipped with brake alway comply with national legislation concerning brake equipment when transporting the machine on public road show good judgement and drive carefully pay close attention to the machine width and turning circle of the implement during transport the rear view is very restricted check the positioning of the tractor rear view mirror please be aware that if the machine is running on very steep and or rough terrain there is risk that the machine may overturn use the light on the seed drill in accordance with local traffic regulation en gb general safety provision in order to avoid any risk arising from mistake during road transport all electronic control equipment inside and outside of the tractor cab must be switched off before commencing transport on the road during transportation on public road the pre implement must be raised retracted so that the transport width and transport height of the implement comply with national regulation before driving ensure that all nut and bolt are tight after couple of hour driving re tighten the wheel nut regularly check that they are tight note that nut must be tightened to specific torque do not stand on the platform during operation keep the platform clean to prevent any risk of slipping use the yellow locking device to block the lifting cylinder when transporting the drill on the road over longer distance please be aware that if the machine is running on very steep and or rough terrain there is risk that the machine may overturn never fill the tyre to pressure higher than tyre manufacturer recommendation this may result in great danger on the road this machine and its tyre are designed for maximum speed of km during transportation on the public highway pay attention to national speed limit this speed should be reduced on uneven road surface the intermediate packer should alway be in the raised position when being transported on road this will avoid unnecessary tyre wear fold up the lower step before transport to avoid the tractor damaging it safety instruction during work ensure that people who are near the seed drill when the tractor engine is in operation maintain an adequate safety distance from suspended load and from raised or moving machine part alway make sure that the following harrow working area is free of obstruction bear in mind that there is risk of injury when the following harrow is being lowered to the ground alway shut down the tractor and take out the ignition key in conjunction with work in the seed hopper as well as when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill when carrying out service and maintenance the drill should be in the lowered position and standing on flat surface the ladder on the inside of the seed hopper is only intended for use in conjunction with service work the machine is equipped with platform do not stand on the platform during operation the machine platform is not intended to be used for manual loading from small bag if small bag are to be used the best way to fill the seed drill is to use loader with the bag on pallet watch out for hanging power line when the machine is being transported do not work with the machine during thunder storm and when there is risk of lightning strike do not stand on or next to the machine en gb general safety provision the intermediate packer should alway be in the raised position when reversing the seed drill if the packer is not in the raised position the wheel carrier and the wheel themselve could be damaged fold up the lower step before transport to avoid the tractor damaging it design change the user owner is liable for design change addition or modification that have not been approved by derstad ab design change can affect wear and may therefore cause the machine to no longer comply with the legislative requirement in accordance with which it has been approved configuration change the user owner is solely responsible for any configuration change that have not been approved by derstad ab conversion of the machine invalidate the type transport transport brake hydraulic brake the machine can be fitted with hydraulic brake the system comprise parking brake and an emergency brake system that is applied if the machine become unhitched from the tractor parking brake figure alway park the implement on stable and level surface activate the parking brake by undoing the pin and rotating the lever handle tighten the lever release the parking brake by moving the lever forward and then to the upright position if the implement has to be parked on or close to public road the machine must also be secured by putting chock under the transport wheel if the machine is to be parked for longer period of time the parking brake should be disengaged the machine should instead be secured with chock emergency brake in order for the emergency brake to function the wire in the parking brake lever must be secured to the tractor see figure the emergency brake engage if the machine become unhitched from the tractor pneumatic brake the machine can be fitted with pneumatic brake the pneumatic brake are linked to the tractor compressed air coupling and are controlled by the pressure applied to the tractor brake pedal the pneumatic brake have compressed air in the brake pipe but the last section out toward the brake drum consist of closed oil system when the tractor brake are applied the braking effect is proportionally transferred to the machine and ensure effective braking connecting the brake connect the brake system compressed air line and steering link to the tractor brake fitting the compressed air line has red mounting and must be connected to the tractor red coupling the control line has yellow mounting and must be connected to the tractor yellow coupling disconnecting the brake unhitching and parking must alway take place on flat firm surface disconnect the brake hose and electric cable from the tractor and hang them on the hose holder figure release the brake by pressing in the retarder valve lower and secure the support stand disconnect the hitching hook agricultural drawbar device parking pneumatic brake alway park the implement on level and stable surface the brake are automatically applied when the implement is unhitched from the tractor en gb transport figure when parking the implement on or close to public road be sure to put chock under the wheel the wheel chock are located on the front platform if the machine is to be parked for longer period of time the parking brake should be disengaged the machine should instead be secured with chock switching between transport and working retraction function alway use an appropriate tool when operating the locking device doe not apply to rda without idc the seed drill is equipped with function that allow the wing section wheel to be pulled in once the machine has been folded up into its transport position this is to minimise the machine transport width figure to activate the function the locking device one on each wing section must be lifted up to position once the machine has been folded up figure use the enclosed key or other suitable tool when the machine is folded out to working position the wheel retraction mechanism is reset automatically and the wheel return to normal position the seed drill should not be fully raised when the wing section are unfolded lower it approx cm from top position before starting the folding process if the wheel retraction mechanism automatic reset has not worked this is shown by the machine hanging at an angle in the extended position to rectify this retract the folding cylinder lower the machine until its wheel retraction mechanism engage the machine can be folded up and folded out even without this function being activated with the only consequence being that the wing section wheel then stay in normal position and the transport width is therefore greater to avoid serious damage to the machine if the machine is on soft ground and folded up do not lower it to the drilling position if the machine is folded up the wheel must not be drawn up from the ground using the hydraulic system ac en gb general setting general setting horizontal alignment figure adjust the seed coulter drill to the same depth as those at the rear first pack the soil and then scrape off very thin layer of soil until the seed are exposed the seed in all row should be exposed at the same cylinder connected in sery in so called master slave system before the seed drill is brought into use the cylinder must be bled and reset in relation to each other bleeding raise the machine to its during the working day after bleeding and resetting the master slave system the height level for the three section should then be checked and adjusted in relation to each other if necessary this is important in order to ensure that the drilling depth is identical in all section adjusting the height of the wing section against the centre section rda adjustment should be made with the seed drill satisfactorily then try with different scraper setting example if there is lot of plant residue in the soil and it is humid the scraper will usually provide better result when the distance is increased to approx mm if the soil is humid without plant residue the scraper will provide better result when mounted in the default position mm track eradicator for road transport the track think about safety never walk under suspended load make sure nobody is on the drill when the seed is brought up to it it is best to use an extension handle on the knife do not go up to the seed hopper unless absolutely necessary avoid contact with and inhalation of seed dressing figure the safest method of filling is from the rear or from the side with the wing folded up rda if the filling is done from the front this must take place diagonally from the right hand side with the tractor turned sideway so it is possible to get up on the platform without having to walk under the lifting arm filling from small bag put safety first never stand under suspended load make sure nobody is on the drill when the seed is brought up to it do not go up to the seed hopper unless absolutely necessary avoid contact with and inhalation of seed dressing the ladder and platform on the machine are not intended to be used for manual loading from small bag ok en gb filling and emptying figure the best way to fill is to use loader and put the bag on pallet lift the pallet diagonally from the front right with the tractor sideway at the same height as the hand rail so the platform can be accessed safely forget to close the sliding hatch after emptying en gb calibration calibration for control please refer to the separate user guide the seed drill is equipped with hydraulically powered feed output weighing of calibration sample should be carried out as follow press the start reset button hang the empty calibration bag on the scale hook the weight of the bag is displayed wait until hold is shown on the display the scale automatically shut off after approximately minute it is therefore advisable to note the empty bag weight press the start reset button hold en gb calibration remove the bag and fill it with the calibration sample weigh the now filled bag the scale now show the net weight of the sample whilst driving the scale should be stored safely in the calibration box regularly check the scale against known weight and alway prior to the start of the season replace the battery type when the battery indicator show one bar or less en gb seed drilling seed drilling the drilling depth used must be adjusted to suit the current crop the soil type and drilling condition at the time of drilling continuously and carefully monitor the drilling depth the drilling depth is set mechanically using the lifting cylinder stop device or from the tractor cab using the control unit in field with varying soil type the depth setting should be checked and adjusted to ensure the correct drilling depth across the entire field feed system operation at too high speed can lead to the seed bouncing around at the bottom of the seed furrow this result in an uneven drilling depth operation at too low speed in relation to the application rate speed can lead to the seed hose getting blocked setting the amount of air the fan rotation speed must not exceed rpm the amount of air is regulated by mean of the fan rotation speed the fan rotation speed is regulated by the tractor flow valve there is flow valve on most tractor model with constant pressure hydraulic system if the tractor has priority hydraulic coupling use that one controlling the flow from the tractor hydraulic system provide for optimum function of both the hydraulically powered fan and the tractor set the fan rotation speed in accordance with the table check the fan rotation speed in the display on ipad normal mode fan rotation speed rpm the recommended speed are guideline at low application rate it may be good idea to reduce the speed at extremely high application rate high speed the fan speed may need to be increased the alarm warning of too low rotation speed is best set for rpm below the preset rotation speed and the alarm warning of too high rotation speed to rpm above the preset rotation speed the alarm level are set in the general setting menu table recommended fan rotation speed rda rda rda small size seed rpm rpm rpm grain rpm rpm rpm if full fan speed is not required the fan rotation speed should be lowered using the tractor flow valve setting the drilling depth reset and bleed the system for approx second several time during the working day raise the machine to its top position so that all hydraulic cylinder are fully extended keep the hydraulic lever in this position and the tractor rda bar accumulator for machine with wing packer rda bar rda bar tramlining the most important thing to remember about tramlining when drilling is to switch off the auto advance function as soon as the drill finish drilling or making normal turn on headland in the bout this mean the auto advance is switched off before the seed drill is raised to drill around post well or other obstacle in the bout when it is time to fill the seed hopper or take break raise the seed drill out of the most recent bout and wait approx second then switch off the auto advance however the auto advance need not be switched off if no additional lifting will take place during the pause engage the auto advance before the seed drill is raised again for the first time when you continue drilling ad en gb seed drilling tramlining hatch figure tramlining shut off take place with tramlining hatch the hatch are integrated into the tramlining motor that is mounted next to every output that must be sealed when tramlining is active in activated mode the hatch are pulled up and seal each output the motor are controlled via four busbar located in the bottom section of the distributor head the connection are on right on left ground and alarm signal carry out function control of the tramlining at the start of the season and at regular interval throughout the season setting tramline with the rda tramline can with half machine shut off second bout overlap half the first bout first bout drive with half machine shut off second bout overlap half the first bout engaging the seed drill the seed drill should alway travel forward when it is lowered into the ground keep the hydraulic lever in the push position the lowered position until the bout marker has been folded out completely check that the tramlining indication is progressing and that the indication for right left bout marker arm switch when lifting take place while driving lowlift and auto advance should normally be engaged turning in lowlift mode the bout marker retract on arrival at the headland when the seed drill is lifted if lowlift is engaged the machine is lifted to lowlift height when the seed drill is lifted at the headland the bout marker and tramlining automatically advance by one step if the seed drill doe not lift up lift stop is probably engaged and if the bout marker and tramlining do not advance auto advance is probably disengaged when turning in lowlift mode the following harrow work throughout the turn before reversing the tractor train lowlift must be switched off and the seed drill raised to highlift position drilling the headland in dry and fine condition the headland can be drilled first in wet condition or when drilling soil sensitive to compaction higher yield can be obtained if the headland is drilled last figure the best way to mark out the headland is by completing the last tillage operation before drilling the autumn harrowing by harrowing the headland last so that the outermost headland pass has the right distance from the edge of the field if seed coulter become blocked do not go under the machine unless it has first been properly secured with the yellow locking device securing the machine for service drilling around obstacle switch off the auto advance before raising the seed drill to sow around post well or other obstacle in the bout en gb seed drilling the bout marker are retracted to get past an obstacle by activating the lift stop and moving the hydraulic lever to the lifting position the bout marker will retract while the seed drill will remain in its working position fold down the bout marker again in this case the function selector for the auto advance or the marker do not need to be touched mechanical following harrow do not reverse the tractor unless the drill has been fully raised and the following harrow has sufficient clearance height if the machine has been set to the maximum drilling depth and the following harrow is subject to high load the clearance in the transport position may be limited figure select suitable working angle from hole sery figure the work pressure of the following harrow is adjusted using the adjusting screw use the ratchet supplied adjust the following harrow to remain in the work position when turning on the headland in the low lift position this will eradicate out the track of the drill when turning figure check that the following harrow tine trail between the wheel and in the wheel track this indicate that the following harrow tine are going between the furrow when this is happening high pressure can be applied on the following harrow without disrupting the seed this allow vaporisation layer to be harrowed while sowing ba en gb maintenance and service maintenance and service the machine must alway be secured before any type of service or maintenance work is commenced never stand near hydraulic hose under pressure drain the system accumulator before servicing the hydraulic system securing prior to servicing alway shut down the tractor and take out the ignition key in conjunction with work in the seed hopper and when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill never work under the drill during maintenance or service work unless it has been secured with trestle and the lifting cylinder has been blocked machine should be placed in the topmost position before the catch can be mounted be sure to alway lower the drill to the ground prior to performing any service on the hydraulic system also ensure that the surface supporting the trestle is firm enough to support them if the hopper is full which it ought not to be during maintenance work the weight of the seed drill is considerable when carrying out service and maintenance the drill should be in the lowered position and standing on flat surface exercise extreme caution when undertaking any kind of work with the machine hydraulic system wipe with clean paper or cloth place part on clean surface not directly on the work bench rinse before assembly for example with decreasing agent never rinse the bearing with high pressure water supply it is important to grease the bearing after washing in order to remove any water that has remained behind never clean bearing directly with high pressure water jet clean electrical component by blowing them with air or by wiping them with dampened cloth to maintain the machine high level of quality and operational safety use only derstad genuine spare part the warranty and any claim under it will become void if part other than genuine part are used figure lubricate the machine according to the interval specified in the lubrication schedule and alway before and after winter storage and after high pressure water cleaning before operation ensure that all nut and bolt have been tightened doe not apply to bolt in flexible joint during the season check periodically that the nut and bolt have not worked loose and inspect joint and hydraulic ram mounting for wear securing the machine for service when work is to be performed on the implement the machine must alway be parked on stable and level surface en gb maintenance and service when working on the hydraulic system the wing must be folded down and the implement lowered to the ground if the hydraulic system include accumulator these must be drained of oil before carrying out the service or repair work be very careful when working with the hydraulic system even if the tractor has been shut down and is depressurised there may still be some pressure left in the hydraulic hose check that the wing section locking hook are engaged before carrying out work underneath the folded up section for all work underneath the seed drill or when there is risk of crush injury the machine must be supported safely on stand secure the seed drill using trestle or similar and lock all the lifting cylinder using the appropriate yellow locking device ensure that the surface supporting the trestle is sufficiently firm if the hopper is full which it ought not to be during maintenance work the weight of the seed drill is considerable figure secure the machine by placing trestle in the appropriate position which are marked by in figure mounting of safety catch there are lifting cylinder to control the machine depth adjustment raise the machine to full transport height figure safety catch for machine without idc block the lifting cylinder with the safety catch see figure safety catch for machine without idc or figure safety catch for machine with idc depending on whether the machine is equipped with idc or not figure safety catch for machine with idc lock the safety catch using the locking pin provided lower the machine secure the support stand when the machine is folded up neither of the slave cylinder need to be locked but check that both the locking pin of the wing section are fully locked en gb maintenance and service tool multi tool the multi tool has three use on this lower section is used to measure wear on system disc system disc aggressive drill disc the symbol show the factory fitted position of the seed coulter the symbol show when it is time to change the component on the fertiliser side galvanised bolt and other exposed place the protective waxy coating can be restored by using tectyl dinitrol or mercasol inspect the machine towing eye see inspecting seed that has germinated can block the seed hose seed can also attract small rodent which can damage the machine clean the radare en gb maintenance and service keep the fan running for while after cleaning so that the whole system is blown dry service hatch at the bottom of the seed hopper there is hatch that provide access for lubrication and service of the parallel linkage figure rda undo the screw the hatch can now be removed by pushing it upward and lifting it out remove the grille by undoing the locking screw that hold the grille in place for the hatch to seal properly make sure it is touching the seed hopper when it is fitted at the bottom of the seed hopper there are two hatch that provide access for lubrication and service of the parallel linkage figure rda loosen the nut and lift out the bracket the hatch can now be removed by pushing them upward and lifting them out remove the grille by undoing the locking screw that hold the grille in place for the hatch to seal properly make sure they are touching the seed hopper when they are fitted re tightening the screw and nut before driving off make sure that all nut and bolt are tight during the season check periodically that the nut and bolt have not worked loose and inspect joint and hydraulic cylinder mounting for wear bolt and nut forming part of joint must not be tightened so much that the jointed part are clamped to each other ba en gb maintenance and service lubrication point safety first do not crawl under the machine lubricate from above or support the machine securely on trestle figure machine is equipped with standardised towing interface check that the towing eye you have chosen is suitable for your machine inspecting the machine towing eye the towing eye should be changed when it become worn be sure to also check the towing eye bolted joint re tightening of screw joint and wear limit never weld towing eye as this can drastically reduce its durability we alway recommend that worn out towing eye are replaced figure the towing eye bolted joint must be re tightened at regular interval to use torque wrench when the diameter of the hole in the towing eye has increased by mm it has reached its wear limit and it is time to change the eye when fitting new towing eye new bolt must be used max mm en gb maintenance and service system disc checking disc make sure the front tool is securely supported figure checking disc wear the system disc is wearing part and must be replaced the multi tool can be used to measure the wear on the system disc place the multi tool on disc bolt read off the system disc radius the disc should be replaced when the radius is less than seal must alway be replaced at the same time as the hub changing disc the front tool must be securely placed on trestle before the disc are changed the disc are sharp so wear glove when replacing disc use ratchet wrench or better still nut runner the hub ring see figure must not be pinched during installation make sure the front tool is securely supported block the disc using wood block or similar to prevent it from turning loosen the disc bolt and replace the disc tighten the disc bolt to nm changing the disc hub ensure that the seed drill is safely supported on stand be sure to keep dirt away whenever work need to be performed on the bearing figure clean edge thread and the shaft with steel brush before fitting the hub fit the cover ring and hub to the shaft fit the nut tighten the nut to tightening torque nm fit the ring at the same time as the disc the ring must not be pinched seed coulter installation height before commencing any work under the drill be sure to secure it in the raised position figure en gb maintenance and service to ensure satisfactory sowing result it is important that the seed coulter are correctly installed on the drill select the installation height based on the condition and disc wear when the drill has been lowered with the disc resting on firm ground the seed coulter should not touch the surface mm please note that as the measurement is reduced the seed coulter become more susceptible to impact with stone upon delivery the seed coulter are installed in position this installation height suit most operating condition figure drilling under very moist condition with lot of plant residue in the soil surface or shallow drilling in light or humus soil may cause the disc to stop occasionally this can be avoided by moving the coulter up to position position however can result in impaired seed placement the illustration below show an example of drilling when there is lot of plant residue on the soil surface in this case the seed coulter should be installed in position figure tightening nut figure the seed coulter are spring mounted on two bolt and the soft washer press the seed coulter closer to the disc as the nut are tightened the nut should not be tightened more than allow for the coulter to be rocked with the hand the coulter must not have too close contact with the disc since this will cause increased wear and rotational friction for very loose soil and or shallow drilling depth it may be necessary to loosen the nut when new seed coulter are mounted it is necessary to check the width of the slot between the disc and the coulter the spring should be wider at the top otherwise straw and plant residue may collect here if the seed coulter do not make contact at the correct place this can be adjusted by moving the metal plate at position and inside or outside the fixing bracket the point of contact can also be moved somewhat by tightening the front nut more than the rear one position reference measurement between the disc and the seed coulter mm mm mm changing the seed coulter mounting bolt the disc arm has two recessed bolt for coulter mounting if these cannot be driven out they must be cut and ground level with the arm it may be necessary to remove the whole disc arm to facilitate this figure en gb maintenance and service the bolt can then be driven out using mm diameter punch burr may be left in the hole after the bolt have been removed file them away to facilitate driving in the new bolt drive or press in the new bolt or pull them into place with the aid of few washer and nut not locknut oil the thread the bottom of the washer and the nut thread liberally use the nut to pull the bolt into place be sure to use sufficient number of washer to prevent the nut from reaching the bottom of the threaded section of the bolt locking device figure do not lubricate the locking device check its function regularly the locking hook on the back of the seed hopper must go into the lug on the side section front tool system disc system disc aggressive figure after couple of hour of work retighten the bolt intermediate packer never stand under the intermediate packer or seed drill if the intermediate packer is raised and only secured with the tractor hydraulic lifting arm prior to any service on the intermediate packer be sure to secure it properly with trestle etc on stable surface figure retightening all bolt it is imperative that all bolted joint be retightened after the first hectare work following this bolted joint need only be retightened once at the end of each season re adjustment figure if longitudinal play approx mm occur in the intermediate packer drawbar this can be adjusted by installing washer on the shaft in front of the drawbar if play of more than approx mm occur in the steering head pivot pin this may also be adjusted by installing washer on the underside en gb maintenance and service wheel leg pivot pin check that the large nut is fully tightened if play more than mm can still be felt in the large pivot pin spacer should be installed as follow figure raise the packer and disconnect the parallel stay loosen the nut after which the wheel leg can be retracted install new washer let the old one remain in place check for abnormal wear clean all pivot pin surface with diesel fuel or similar apply small amount of grease and reinstall figure tighten the nut to torque of nm check that all joint can move without any excessive effort and that there is no longer any play if the play still exist two additional spacer may be required wheel bearing adjustment it is important that the wheel hub bearing are retightened check that the wheel hub are free of play when the packer is in the raised position if considerable play can be felt the bearing should be tightened figure disassemble the wheel hub cap undo the locking pin that hold the castle nut in place and tighten the castle nut until the play disappear lock the nut with the pin clean the hub cap and fill it with fresh grease then refit it wheel changing wheel set the drill on solid surface and retract the wheel to allow the machine to rest on the disc figure raise the machine using jack until the tyre is about cm above the ground remove the scraper remove the split cotter and the washer on the wheel fork swivel stay then loosen the stay pin by wedging chisel or similar tool in the mechanism en gb maintenance and service figure loosen the wheel shaft and pull out the wheel downward and backward place spacer board or similar below the new wheel when mounting it mount the swivel stay push down the wheel together with the hydraulic mechanism toward the spacer until the wheel reach the bottom of the wheel fork tighten the wheel shaft tyre and air pressure check the tyre pressure see recommendation in table table model transport wheel support wheel bar kpa bar kpa rda rda rda the hydraulic system the hydraulic system must alway be bled after servicing make sure there is nobody in the immediate working area of the machine move the lifting cylinder the bout marker cylinder and the front tool cylinder to their fully extended and fully retracted position few time until all air has been evacuated from the hydraulic system service of hydraulic component during servicing of the hydraulic system the machine must alway be folded out and lowered to the ground the hydraulic circuit must be depressurised never stand near hydraulic hose under pressure drain the system accumulator before servicing the hydraulic system all work and repair must be carried out in line with qualified procedure the highest possible level of cleanliness should be observed when replacing sealing kit be careful not to damage any surface of the hydraulic component while working and that all gasket are mounted correctly check the component for abnormal wear and tear for example burr or scratch which may indicate the presence of dirt in the hydraulic system or wrongly weighted component emptying the hydraulic system accumulator before starting service work on the part of the hydraulic covering the folding and weight transfer system the accumulator must first be drained of oil emptying the hydraulic system accumulator mean that the pressure on the manometer will be zero when performing service and or repair work on the hydraulic system the seed drill must alway be folded out figure open the weight transfer cock en gb maintenance and service figure raise the seed drill to its top position release the pressure from the seed drill folding system using the hydraulic control check that the pressure on the manometer is zero lower the seed drill back down to set the pressure again see setting the weight transfer inspection of hydraulic hose check the hydraulic hose every three month or every hour whichever come first date marking and max pressure on hydraulic hose the hose end are marked with the date of manufacture year and month and max pressure bleeding the hydraulic system see adjusting the master slave system replacing the oil filter make sure that service and maintenance work to the hydraulic system is done in clean surrounding alway depressurise the system before changing the filter the oil filter must be changed according to the interval in the lubrication schedule and after every season figure unscrew and remove the filter housing place the filter housing on clean surface take off the old filter element and fit new one replace and tighten the filter housing by brake hydraulic brake at the start of the season figure adjust the brake pad screw the adjustment screw clockwise while rotating the wheel slowly turn the adjustment screw until you feel resistance in the wheel as they rotate this mean that the brake pad are in gentle contact with the brake drum to decrease the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum turn the adjustment screw clockwise to increase the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum turn the adjustment screw anticlockwise check that all line hose and brake cylinder are undamaged and that they are not leaking ca ba en gb maintenance and service changing brake equipment component the brake pad must not be replaced individually all the brake pad must be replaced simultaneously the same apply when replacing the wheel cylinder which must also all be replaced at the same time the brake system wheel cylinder brake pad and brake drum are wearing part bleeding the brake system following maintenance or other work on the hydraulic system the brake system must be bled before use bleed as follow figure top up the container with oil to the max marking connect brake bleeding tool max bar to the main cylinder or connect the compressed air line and control line to the tractor and apply the brake or connect the compressed air line and the control line to an external compressed air source max bar figure bleed the system using the nipple which is located adjacent to the brake line connection at each wheel be careful first bleed the wheel that is furthest away from the main cylinder the wheel furthest away on the right hand side and continue to bleed left wheel right wheel until all the air has been forced out of the line connect the transparent hose to the nipple and allow surplus oil to run into suitable container close the nipple when no more air bubble can be seen in the hose if the oil is not topped up when the level is below the min marking there is risk of air penetrating the system en gb maintenance and service pneumatic brake daily maintenance drain off the condensation water from the compressed air container figure open the discharge valve on the underside of the container figure check the oil level in the container on the main cylinder the level should be between the max and min marking on the container top up with mineral oil type gl or equivalent if the level is too low the mineral oil should be type gl or equivalent other type of brake fluid will damage the system seal at the start of the season figure adjust the brake pad turn the adjustment screw clockwise while rotating the wheel slowly turn the adjustment screw until you feel resistance in the wheel as they rotate this mean that the brake pad are in gentle contact with the brake drum turn the adjustment screw clockwise to decrease the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum turn the adjustment screw anticlockwise to increase the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum check the oil level top up with fresh oil if necessary only use mineral oil type gl or equivalent the mineral oil should be type gl or equivalent other type of brake fluid will damage the system seal check that all line hose and brake cylinder are undamaged and that they are not leaking checking the pneumatic brake system figure en gb maintenance and service check for wear in the brake on the indicator plate when the indicator pin move from to it is time to check and service the system check that the fluid level in the brake fluid reservoir doe not fall below the minimum indicator line check that all line hose and brake cylinder are undamaged and that they are not leaking figure if the brake action is slow remove the line filter on the air pipe and clean or replace the filter as necessary changing brake equipment component incorrect handling can result in the brake not working risk of great danger in road traffic in case of any uncertainty contact professional service personnel the brake shoe must not be changed separately all brake shoe on the same shaft must be changed at the same time the same apply to brake cylinder which must also be changed in pair bleeding the pneumatic brake following maintenance or other work on the brake system it must be bled before use figure top up the container with iso mineral oil or equivalent for hydraulic system to the max marking figure connect transparent hose to the nipple and allow any excess oil to run into suitable container close the nipple when no more air bubble can be seen in the hose connect brake bleeding tool max bar to the main cylinder or connect the compressed air line and control line to the tractor and apply the brake or connect the compressed air line and control line to an external compressed air source max bar the mineral oil should be iso or equivalent other type of brake fluid will damage the system seal if the oil is not topped up when the level is below the min mark there is risk of air penetrating the system bleed the system using the nipple which is located adjacent to the brake line connection on the left of each wheel be careful bleed the wheel from the right and continue to bleed all the wheel until all air has been forced out of the line bc en gb maintenance and service figure when the system has been fully bled the indicator pin should be shorter than mm and indicate figure press in the indicator pin turn the lever on the control panel back to half full hopper and apply the brake check that the indicator pin still point to if not turn the lever to the maintenance position and repeat it click into the catch position close the distributor head check that the contact surface and the ring that is to be sealed against the delivery pipe part are free from waste matter or seed if the ring doe not seal properly air leakage will occur and this will disturb the seed feed output press down on the catch and lower the lever crushing risk lower the lever all the way down and press down until it click into the catch position aa en gb maintenance and service replacement of the distributor head tramlining motor figure the distributor head valve can be replaced as needed to undo these motor and circuit board repair and replacement of the seed hose figure repair if seed hose is damaged due to abrasion or folding it can be jointed with sleeve cut the hose at the centre of the fold or damage where necessary the smallest possible part of the hose can be cut away if the hose become too stiff at the joint and cannot be bent sufficiently when the machine is lowered it may be necessary to replace the entire seed hose or to replace part of the hose and joint it in two place changing seed hose use soapy water on the hose when fitting them to seed coulter twist the hose anticlockwise when removing or fitting which will help the spiral reinforcement to open the hose little cut replacement hose to the same length as the one it is to replace when storing for longer period when the seed drill is not being used it should be stored indoor this is very important given that the seed drill has electronic instrument disconnect the battery to prevent current leakage discharging from the battery braked machine should not have the parking brake applied but be secured with chock with pneumatic brake the brake are released by pressing the retarder valve in the control box and the battery should be stored at room temperature when stored for long period machine part with shiny finish such as piston rod and wearing part should be treated with rust protection before longer storage derstad ab visit vaderstad com en gb troubleshooting troubleshooting general information on troubleshooting many of the function of the seed drill are controlled by sery of electrical hydraulic and mechanical component good way to immediately exclude many of the source of fault is to first determine whether or not the fault is electrical because of this first check whether the electrical circuit is intact right up to the last electrical component in the chain then continue troubleshooting by first performing the easiest inspection in order to quickly exclude other source of fault electrical fault general check in the event of electrical fault check that all the connector pin and socket are clean free from damage and are not pushed inward spray the switch with contact spray that is intended for use with electronic equipment check that all cable are correctly connected and that no cable is trapped or otherwise damaged check that the power cable fuse is intact hydraulic fault general check in the event of hydraulic fault check that the hydraulic hose are connected to the correct socket on the tractor hose with the same colour coding constitute pair make sure the hydraulic quick coupler are designed for and suited to the tractor coupling there is wide variety of connector on the market all standardised but still there are problem problem can manifest themselve so that female and male coupling operate as non return valve that is that machine can be raised but not lowered or vice versa the problem may be aggravated by large flow or worn coupling hydraulic electric valve an electric valve has coil which act as an electromagnet when an electrical current is connected to the valve it is easy to determine whether or not the power is on the connecting contact diode light up and the coil become warm after few minute the top nut will also become magnetic figure use small chisel or the side of blade to determine whether or not the top nut has become magnetic the nut is alway weakly magnetic so try both with and without power figure the bout marker valve and receive current when the control unit indicate active bout marker while the machine is below lowlift height the lift stop valve receive current when the lift stop is engaged or during lift in the lowlift mode the pre emergence bout marker solenoid valve receive current when the control unit indicate active tramlining while the machine is below lowlift height the bout marker stop valve receive current when the control system is activated to finely adjust the drilling depth the sink stop valve receive current when the machine reach the set drilling depth outgoing signal to bout marker and the pre emergence bout marker are not activated until the machine is below lowlift height en gb troubleshooting reed switch figure magnetic switch known also as reed switch is switch or sensor that react to magnetic field the magnetic switch is glass tube containing two metal reed that attract each other when subjected to magnetic field from magnet see the illustration reed switch operation can easily be tested using multimeter and magnet inductive sensor figure react to metal object passing within distance of mm function testing can be carried out easily since diode at the rear of the sensor light up each time an object is detected capacitive sensor figure react to object that contain moisture for example grain or hand and so on function testing is easily carried out since diode at the rear of the sensor light up each time such an object is detected the sensitivity of the sensor can be adjusted by use of the screw next to the led various type of grain and fertiliser contain different amount of moisture for this reason adjustment may be necessary under certain condition en gb troubleshooting troubleshooting list the control unit doe not operate when the main switch is switched on see electrical fault the bout marker cannot be unfolded check in accordance with electrical fault check in accordance with hydraulic fault check whether the control unit is indicating that bout marker is active check whether the machine is at or above the lowlift height for safety reason the bout marker cannot be unfolded in the lowlift mode or at higher height check the lowlift switch remove the switch connector to workstation and check the bout marker function check the wing lowering switch this switch block the bout marker valve current signal when the seed drill is folded up to prevent bout marker from unintentionally being lowered in this mode remove the switch connector to workstation and check the bout marker function check the power supply to the two centre solenoid valve and on the top of the valve block see hydraulic electric valve the bout marker valve must receive current when the respective indication lamp are lit the bout marker fail to retract probably due to dirt in the solenoid valve note that dirt causing fault is often invisible it is recommended that solenoid valve are changed it should alway be possible to retract both bout marker regardless of the setting and indication on the control unit and even if the control unit is switched off the folded up bout marker slowly extend inadvertently check whether the indication for this bout marker show that it is inactive on the control unit check that there is no current being supplied to the solenoid valve see hydraulic electric valve swap the right and left bout marker solenoid valve if the fault shift to the other side that solenoid valve must be faulty if the fault remain the bout marker hydraulic cylinder is probably defective remove the solenoid valve and check that it is free from dirt and that the outer seal of the valve are intact and undamaged note first lower the seed drilling unit to the ground and release the hydraulic pressure check that the bout marker cylinder is not leaking externally or internally if the seal need to be changed see service of hydraulic component if the bout marker unfold when the machine is parked set the stop device so that the machine is resting on the wheel disc and support stand use the cotter pin en gb troubleshooting automatic switching of the bout or tramline marker doe not take place is auto advance selected on the control unit has the bout marker function selector been set to the alternating position the indicator lamp in the button should light has the correct tramlining program been selected is the lowlift switch correctly adjusted see setting the lowlift height when lowlift position is reached the signal for auto advance is given check whether the lowlift switch holder is crooked the holder is stuck in the wrong position or the magnetic sensor is damaged has the magnet come off or become loose check for possible break in the wiring or short circuit the seed drill can be raised even though the lift stop is engaged check that the solenoid valve on the valve block is receiving current see hydraulic electric valve check the lowlift switch as described above if the lift stop valve has no current see electrical fault if the lift stop valve has voltage see hydraulic fault the seed drill cannot be raised or lowered check that the lift stop or lowlift mode has not been engaged see hydraulic fault if the seed drill cannot be raised check that the lift stop or lowlift mode has not been engaged see hydraulic fault check whether the lift stop solenoid valve is receiving current either via short circuit or because of another fault see hydraulic electric valve switch off the control unit and check whether the seed drill can be raised if the unit still cannot be raised the fault must be hydraulic check whether the short circuit is in the cable braiding or intermediate cable and which function is affected the control unit is giving warning that the fan speed is too low or too high note no warning occur if fan hose has come loose or the ejector hook are not in place fan check that the hydraulic hose are connected properly and that the hydraulic outlet is activated check the control unit programming are the pressure and flow as they should be on the tractor bar and at least min is the tractor flow valve correctly adjusted is the fan speed regulator correctly adjusted not supplied as standard used when the tractor doe not have flow control en gb troubleshooting the shaft seal in the fan hydraulic motor is leaking leakage from the hydraulic motor shaft seal may be due to damage during assembly wear because of dirt wear because of high pressure in the drain line destroyed seal because of extremely high pressure in the drain line the pressure in the drain line can be measured on the block use bar pressure gauge the maximum pressure during operation should be bar high pressure may be due to large oil flow in the drain line back pressure in the tractor hydraulic valve detach the drain line from the tractor and remove the quick release coupling start the fan collect the drained oil in container measure the pressure measure the amount of oil that arrive over period of one minute considerable decrease in pressure indicate problem with the tractor hydraulic valve slight decrease in pressure indicate large oil flow the drain flow should normally be less than litre per minute large oil flow may be due to worn motor leaking non return valve in the connection block oil from the return line leaking into the drain line the control unit warn there is problem with the metering unit operation if there is sensor fault check the operation of the sensor see inductive sensor check that the toothed washer in the metering unit is not damaged check that the inductive sensor is sufficiently close to the rotating toothed washer adjust if necessary the area meter speedometer show no value or an incorrect value has the correct number of pulse per metre been programmed in if too low speed or too small an area is displayed then reduce the number of pulse per metre if too high speed or too large an area is displayed then increase the number of pulse per metre isobus control change the number of pulse by pressing the field which display the radar pulse metre see the section on calibration in the instruction manual for service the tramlining hatch get stuck check whether the long piston rod of the folding cylinder is completely extended and that the rod doe not slowly retract during driving check the setting of the wing section see adjusting the wing section on rda for the weight transfer setting see setting the weight transfer check the setting of the master slave system see adjusting the master slave system en gb troubleshooting disc do not rotate properly are the seed coulter exposed to undue stress the seed coulter should sit very is the soil very loose it may be necessary to compact the soil using roller is the drilling depth too shallow have the seed coulter been installed too low the disc provide better traction if the seed coulter are raised one notch are the disc excessively worn doe the soil surface contain lot of plant residue are the front tool tine being used too roughly making the soil very loose the seed drill doe not place seed at the bottom of the furrow are the seed coulter excessively worn are the seed coulter set one notch too high the drill will place the seed more precisely if the coulter are set low this doe not normally need to be changed for different soil type the fan speed is irregular replace the hydraulic oil filter the seed application rate is irregular replace the proportional valve in the fan block the left metering unit doe not rotate even though the drive unit is rotating check the locking pin between the metering unit and the drive unit and replace if necessary en gb seeding table seeding table alway perform seed calibration test the content of the seeding table must be viewed as guideline only new feed sample should be taken regularly in the case of small feed quantity check the area driven and the amount of seed fed every time you fill up wheat rye corn oat wheat rye corn oat kg scale value kg ha bean pea lupin vetch maize bean pea lupin vetch maize kg scale value kg ha part no provide the technician with information on the operation and service of the pro fessional dual fuel range it is to be used as training job aid and service manual the wiring diagram and strip circuit used in this job aid are typical and should be used for training purpose only alway use the wiring diagram supplied with the product when servicing the unit goal and objective the goal of this job aid is to provide detailed information that will enable the service technician to properly diagnose malfunction and repair the professional dual fuel range the objective of this job aid are to understand and follow proper safety precaution successfully troubleshoot and diagnose malfunction successfully perform necessary repair successfully return the professional dual fuel range to the proper operational status whirlpool corporation assume no responsibility for any repair made on our product by anyone other than authorized factory service technician copyright whirlpool corporation chassis important safety information your safety and the safety of other is very important important safety message have been provided in this job aid alway read and obey all safety message this is the safety alert symbol this symbol alert you to hazard that can kill or hurt you and other all safety message will be preceded by the safety alert symbol and the word warning all safety message will identify the hazard tell you how to reduce the chance of injury and tell you what can happen if the instruction are not followed important electrostatic discharge esd sensitive electronic esd problem connection point or to an unpainted metal surface in the appliance before removing the part from its package touch the anti static bag to green ground connection point or to an unpainted metal surface company whirl corporation mg the service manual provide information about the specification system introduction service procedure and adjustment of the new mg vehicle how to use this manual general to assist in the use of this manual it is divided into section and sub section the relevant sub section title is given at the header of each page there is single content section at the front of the manual which is divided by section and sub section the book is numbered from page the page number is given at the bottom of each page the individual action of the repair operation must be followed in the sequence in which they appear item number in the illustration refer to the relevant numbered text under the illustration adjustment and repair operation include reference to service tool number and the associated illustration depict the tool in use adjustment and repair operation also include reference to wear limit relevant data torque figure and specialist information and useful assembly detail each adjustment or repair operation is given unique repair operation number warning caution note and tip have the following meaning when encountering warning you will be asked to take necessary action or not to take prohibited action if warning is not heeded the following consequence may occur serious bodily injury or death to the technician serious bodily injury or death to other technician in the workplace area serious bodily injury to the driver and or passenger of the vehicle if the vehicle has been improperly repaired caution call special attention to necessary action or to prohibited action if caution is not heeded the following consequence may occur damage to the vehicle unnecessary vehicle repair unnecessary component replacement improper operation or performance of the system or component under repair damage to any system or component which are dependent upon the proper operation of the system or component under repair improper operation or performance of any system or component which are dependent upon the proper operation or performance of the system or component under repair damage to fastener basic tool or special tool the leakage of coolant lubricant or other vital fluid note statement emphasize necessary characteristic of diagnostic or repair procedure note statement are designed to do the following clarify procedure present additional information for accomplishing procedure give insight into the reason or reason for performing procedure in the manner recommended present information that will help to accomplish procedure in more effective manner present information that give the technician the benefit of past experience in accomplishing procedure with greater ease tip give helpful information reference reference to the lh or rh side given in this manual are made when viewing the vehicle from the rear with the engine and gearbox assembly removed the crankshaft pulley end of the engine is referred to as the front operation covered in this manual do not include reference to testing the vehicle after repair it is essential that work is inspected and tested after completion and if necessary road test of the vehicle is carried out this is of particular importance where safety related item are concerned dimension the dimension quoted are to design engineering specification service limit are included where applicable our company are constantly seeking to improve the specification design and production of their vehicle and alteration take place accordingly while every effort has been made to ensure the accuracy of this manual it should not be regarded as an infallible guide to current specification of any particular vehicle repair and replacement part when replacement part are required it is essential that only our company recommended part are used attention is particularly drawn to the following point concerning repair and the fitting of replacement part and accessory safety feature and corrosion prevention treatment embodied in the vehicle may be impaired if other than smc recommended part are fitted torque wrench setting figure given in this manual must be used locking device where specified must be fitted if the efficiency of locking device is impaired during removal it must be renewed the term of the vehicle warranty may be invalidated by the fitting of part other than smc recommended part warranty smc dealer are obliged to supply only smc recommended part special tool special tool have been developed to facilitate removal dismantling and assembly of mechanical component in cost effective and time efficient manner the use of special tool also help prevent the potential for damage to component some operation in the manual cannot be carried out properly without the aid of the
