internal failure occur the quality of atf will change accordingly therefore the condition of atf will be the key element in failure diagnosis low fluid level oil pump will inhale air which can result in low oil pressure and functional malfunction of transmission high fluid level large number of bubble produced by the stir of the gear set will overflow from the vent pipe normal atf red with moderate viscosity abnormal atf water white emulsion or mixture of different atf will lead to shift impact and rough running degrading the performance check atf level automatic transmission fluid refill check atf quality check the quality of atf with or without metal particle powder of friction plate burning smell white emulsion and nigrescence excessive metal chip and bushing powder could cause the automatic transmission internal wear check if the atf discolor if yes it indicate that atf may be mixed with water special attention shall be paid and the cooling system will be inspected if transmission mt transmission transmission layout reverse lamp switch assembly gear shift cable bracket bolt gear selection shift cable bracket gear selection cable bracket washer oil plug oil filler bearing drive gear gear assembly drive gear drive shaft rear bearing drive gear gear assembly driven shaft driven shaft bearing driven gear gear assembly and driven reverse gear driven gear driven gear driven gear transmission mt transmission driven gear driven shaft locking nut differential bearing main reduction driven gear differential transmission transmission mt description overview this vehicle is equipped with speed manual transmission the shift lever is located at the centre console of the passenger compartment it provide the driver with choice for variable speed position namely gear gear gear gear gear and reverse gear the forward gear are engaged by the and the reverse gear is driven by the sliding idler gear without the drive shaft is fitted in the differential assembly at the rear of the transmission and it is used to transmit the driving force to the wheel the transmission housing is made of cast aluminum and is connected to the engine cylinder block with bolt the transmission input shaft and output shaft are supported by bearing the transmission housing is equipped with an oil filler plug which is located at the front lower side of the housing and there is an oil drain plug at the rear lower side of the transmission housing both of them are sealed with an aluminum washer the transmission is filled with lubricating oil so as to lubricate the internal component in splashing manner the breather plug is fitted at the rear of the control seat the stamped steel closing panel seal the opening between the transmission and engine cylinder block gear shift and gear selection gear selection is realized by manipulating the shift lever fitted at the centre console of the passenger compartment both cable have one end connected to the bottom of the shift lever the other end is connected to the gear selection rocker arm and gear shift rocker arm fitted outside the transmission through the connector ring assembly in the transmission housing the fork shaft is connected with the fork turn an appropriate to shift to the forward gear or turn the sliding idler gear to shift to the reverse gear reverse gear protection and reverse lamp switch the reverse gear lock mechanism is used to prevent accidental shift from the gear to the reverse gear directly therefore you must ensure that the reverse gear can only be shifted to from the neutral position the reverse lamp plunger switch is fitted on the transmission rear housing differential differential with traditional design fix the main reduction driven gear to the differential housing by bolt differential housing support the planetary shaft pin planetary gear and sun gear the differential assembly is supported by bearing in the transmission housing neutral position sensor start stop system the neutral position sensor fixed on the transmission main housing is hall effect sensor which is used to identify when the transmission is in neutral position when the neutral position is selected if the neutral position signal block is aligned with the sensor the ecm will identify this signal as the neutral position and the area within range at both left and right side are considered as the neutral area the ecm will identify this signal as gear selected the neutral signal is used for the start stop of the engine please refer to engine fuel and control system description for detail transmission mt transmission operation transmission assembly drivetrain change the speed and direction of rotation through the engagement between different gear of the drive shaft and driven shaft thereby generating forward gear of different speed ratio and reverse gear except the reverse gear the gear wheel of all other gear are constantly engaged if the gear is selected the gear ring is pressed by the gear bushing so as to contact with the conical ring of the corresponding gear selected in this way the speed of both hub and gear are then the gear bushing protrude in the gear wheel through the slider assembly and the protrusion tooth of the gear bushing ensure that the taper head on the gear selection shift shaft can select the gear accurately and the gear are remained in the work position the torque is transmitted from the engine to the transmission input shaft through the clutch then the torque is transmitted to the output shaft pinion and main reduction driven gear and then to the drive shaft through the gear selected the operation of the and gear is the same with that of the gear when the reverse gear is selected the reverse gear tooth integratedwiththedriveshaft isengagedwiththedrivenshaft reverse gear tooth and reverse idler gear hence the rotation direction of the driven shaft is changed and the reverse gear is achieved gear shift and gear selection when the shift lever is moved to the gear position the shift lever will control the gear selection shift rocker arm through the cable the gear selection rocker arm rotate and move the gear selection shift shaft through the torque of the cable and the gear selection shift taper head on the gear selection shift shaft move along with it and locate at the groove of the taper block at this time the gear selection shift taper head shall be completely separated from the groove of the taper head one end of the interlock ring will lock the groove of the gear reverse gear taper block the other end will be completely separated from the groove of the gear taper block then the gear shift rocker arm rotate the gear selection shift shaft through the torque of the cable and the gear selection shift taper head rotate along with the gear selection shift shaft one side of the gear selection shift taper head turn gear taper block to drive the operation of the gear so as to achieve the gear selection shift function of the gear the operation of other forward gear are the same as that of the gear when the shift lever is moved to reverse gear position one side of the gear selection shift taper head turn reverse gear taper block to let the reverse gear fork drive the reverse gear rocker arm assembly slide the reverse idler gear to respectively engage it with the drive shaft reverse gear and driven shaft reverse gear driven shaft reverse gear is integrated in the gear assembly so as to change the rotation direction of the driven shaft reverse gear protection reverse gear lock plate gear selection return spring cushion control seat housing reverse gear lock plate return spring when it is shifted to gear gear selection return spring cushion will push against the reverse gear lock plate to move up together then the gear selection shift shaft rotate to the left until snapping into the groove of the gear shift positioning cam when it is shifted directly from gear to gear by the reverse gear lock plate whose motion is restricted by the control seat housing so it is impossible to shift to the gear when it is shifted from the neutral position between gear and gear to gear gear selection return spring cushion will push against the reverse gear lock plate to move up together then the gear selection shift shaft rotate to the transmission transmission mt right until snapping into the groove of the gear shift positioning cam so the reverse gear can be shifted to differential the differential allow the wheel to rotate at different speed when the output torque are the same the pinion integrated with the driven shaft is engaged with the main reduction driven gearofthedifferentialassembly whenthedrivenshaftrotate and the wheel swivel straight forward the torque will be applied to the whole assembly and the planetary gear doe not rotate the torque is transmitted to the wheel through the drive shaft when the vehicle take turn the inner wheel will pass shorter distance at lower speed thus it drive the planetary gear to rotate and the outer sun gear offer higher speed for outer wheel transmission mt transmission service procedure manual transmission assembly remove cylinder bolt clutch slave cylinder transmission clutch description overview this is traditional three piece diaphragm clutch with hydraulic clutch release system which has no need to adjust the clutch to compensate clutch wear clutch hydraulic system mt model the master cylinder of the clutch is directly fitted on the pedal bracket in the passenger compartment and the push rod of the master cylinder piston is fixed to the clutch pedal with clip and pin two end of the hydraulic pipeline are connected to the master and slave cylinder with threaded joint the clutch salve cylinder fitted on the bracket of the manual transmission housing release rocker arm release fork and release bearing compose release mechanism while the release bearing is supported and fixed by the guide sleeve fitted on the transmission input shaft the clutch pipeline from the slave cylinder is connected to the pipeline of the master cylinder through quick connector the release fork lean the release bearing on the diaphragm spring of the clutch during separation clutch mechanical system the clutch cover assembly is fitted on the and secured with six bolt clutch cover assembly diaphragm spring clutch cover pressure plate support ring leaf spring the clutch cover assembly is composed of the diaphragm spring pressure plate and cover the pressure plate is made of cast iron and has smooth plane machined to connect the driven plate there are pull ring on the outer ring of the pressure plate which is then connected to the housing through spring the spring is composed of three quenched and tempered steel sheet when the clutch pedal is depressed it will pull the pressure plate away from the driven plate clutch housing is punched by steal plate the diaphragm spring is cast ring with release finger in its inner ring there are two round support ring fitted on the housing so that the diaphragm spring can rotate between them the diaphragm spring is not directly connected to the pressure plate when the release bearing apply stress on the release finger of the diaphragm spring the diaphragm spring will rotate around the support ring to leave the pressure plate and meanwhile the spring of the pressure plate connected to the housing will also be away from the driven plate driven plate driven plate body friction plate spline hub torsional shock absorber the driven plate is fitted between the and the pressure plate its feature is that the spline hub engage the spline on the transmission input shaft the friction plate is composed of two disc which are riveted on each side of the driven plate body the rivet are fitted in the notch in the friction plate as well as that at the other side to protect the pressure plate and from damage clutch sensor mt the clutch sensor is fitted on the master cylinder of the clutch and is powered by the ecm when the clutch pedal is depressed the ecm will receive the clutch release signal when the clutch pedal is reset the ecm will receive the clutch engagement signal clutch transmission operation hydraulic operation mt model when the clutch pedal is depressed the piston is pushed into the master cylinder the piston drive the fluid in the master cylinder to move along the clutch pipeline this pressure is applied on the piston of the slave cylinder and the piston will move under the action of hydraulic pressure when the clutch pedal is released the force on the release finger of the diaphragm spring will drive the release bearing the release bearing then push the piston back to the slave cylinder by the releaseforkandreleaserockerarm andfurtherdrivesthefluid back to the clutch pipeline and master cylinder mechanical operation when the fluid in the slave cylinder drive the piston to move piston movement push the clutch release bearing to the release finger of the diaphragm spring by the release rocker arm and release fork and the diaphragm spring will rotate around the support ring on the pressure plate housing following the deformation of the diaphragm spring its pressure on the pressure plate will disappear three leaf spring will pull the pressure plate away from the driven plate disappearance of the pressure on the driven plate applied by the pressure plate reduce the frictional force between the driven plate and the driven plate will skid on the and can not transmit the power of the to the transmission input shaft when the clutch pedal is released the pressure on the slave cylinder piston will disappear disappearance of this pressure allow the release finger of the diaphragm spring to push the release bearing to skid along the guide sleeve so that the release fork and release rocker arm are driven to push the slave cylinder piston back to the slave cylinder the diaphragm spring rotate around the support ring on the pressure plate housing and apply pressure on the pressure plate and the pressure plate will overcome the resistance of the spring move toward the and apply pressure on the driven plate the pressure on the driven plate applied by the pressure plate increase the frictional force between the driven plate and when the clutch pedal is released the frictional force will increase the rotational motion of is transmitted to the driven plate and then it is transferred to the transmission input shaft when the pedal is fully released the force on the pressure plate applied by the diaphragm spring will press the driven plate on the making no skidding between them clutch sensor mt when the clutch pedal is depressed the clutch sensor is activated ecm use the signal from the clutch sensor for the following function reduce impact during gear the shift reduce impact to prevent sharp increase of engine speed during the gear shift the reduction of the impact can improve drivability in the following aspect smoother gear shift better exhaust emission control fuel consumption improvement the clutch sensor receive reference voltage from the ecm when the clutch pedal is in the reset position the clutch sensor is connected to the ground when the clutch pedal is depressed ecm will receive signal in case of failure of the clutch sensor any following sign may occur shifting impact reduction is not available transmission clutch service procedure clutch assembly remove remove the transmission assembly manual transmission assembly remove lock the with gradually loosen and remove bolt fixing the clutch cover assembly to the remove the clutch cover assembly and take out the driven plate remove the release bearing clutch release bearing remove check visually check the diaphragm release finger for wear or irregular height check the clutch driven plate for any sign of wear or oil contamination replace the driven plate if necessary measure the thickness of the clutch plate replace the driven plate if the thickness is less than the service limit thickness of driven plate compressed new driven plate service limit measure the depth of rivet replace the driven plate if the depth is less than the service limit depth of rivet new driven plate service limit check the pressure plate for any sign of wear or damage check the drive plate for any sign of overheat with color from deep yellow to blue replace the pressure plate if necessary caution renew clutch pressure plate which has been accidentally dropped check the surface levelness of the pressure plate at separate point with square edge gauge and feeler gauge replace the pressure plate if deformation exceed the service limit value deformation of pressure plate service limit check the release bearing for any sign of wear or damage replace it if necessary clean the pressure plate locating pin and clutch transmission pin hole check the for any sign of scratch or other damage replace it if it is scratched or damaged refit fit the release bearing clutch release bearing refit apply molybdenum disulphide grease to the spline of the clutch driven plate locate the clutch cover assembly on the make sure that the driven plate is aligned with the centre of the pressure plate insert the into the driven plate and ensure that the driven plate is aligned with the centre of the crankshaft to lock the pressure plate with bolt remove the fit the transmission assembly manual transmission assembly refit clutch release bearing remove remove the transmission assembly manual transmission brake fluid into reservoir up to the max mark bleed the clutch pipeline system clutch pipeline system bleeding clutch pipeline system bleeding bleeding bleed the air that enter into the hydraulic system when disconnecting the pipeline for repair during the air bleeding the brake fluid in the brake fluid reservoir must be maintained at or above the min mark warning do not allow brake fluid to come into contact with eye or skin caution do not reuse the brake fluid drained from the clutch pipeline system lift the vehicle on the lifting platform unscrew the bleed plug dust cover from the cylinder slave cylinder and fit the hose to the bleed plug immerse the free end of the hose into the glass container half filled with fresh brake fluid press the clutch pedal to the maximum stroke and hold so as to ensure the maximum system pressure loosen the bleed plug remove the air in the fluid the system pressure will drop at this time tighten the bleed plug to avoid the suck back of the fluid at last release the clutch pedal to the maximum stoke steadily allowing the master cylinder piston to return to its original position without any external force supplement with sufficient brake fluid repeat the operation in the above sequence for several time until the brake fluid without any air flow into the container then fix the clutch pedal to the maximum stroke tighten the bleed plug to note large leak cracked hose impact or open relief valve can cause sudden loss of the refrigerant refrigerant leak or slow leak will not cause this degree of fluid loss when replacing component which cause huge loss of refrigerant add an appropriate amount of fluid to specific component if the lost fluid of the component exceed the specified value add as per the amount of loss hvac system hvac structure description and operation system layout heating and ventilation component instrument panel air duct left front window air duct left windshield air duct front window air duct right instrument panel air duct right instrument panel air duct face footwell air duct hvac structure hvac system box assembly mode damper actuator temperature damper actuator recirculated damper actuator filter blower assembly blower speed regulation resistor heater core evaporator core hvac system hvac structure cooling system evaporator and thermostatic expansion valve txv pressure switch low pressure service connector high pressure service connector pipeline condenser compressor hvac structure hvac system description overview the heating ventilation and air conditioning system control the vehicle interior temperature and air distribution the system contain the box assembly filter heater assembly and evaporator box assembly air duct refrigerant pipe controller and control panel the air outlet are integrated in the luggage compartment fresh air flow into the box assembly through the filter assembly and flow through the entire system by the blower operation according to the setting on the control panel the heated or cooled air is supplied to the air outlet of face footwell and defrost via the air distribution duct according to model the vehicle will be provided with one of the two air conditioning system mtc or etc in the mtc system function such as the air circulation air temperature air distribution and blower speed are selected manually and the air temperature and air distribution are operated and controlled with cable in the etc system function such as air recirculation air temperature air distribution and blower speed etc are selected manually ventilation component ventilation component filter air duct box housing damper damper actuator blower and air outlet air filter the air filter is used to improve the quality of the fresh air supplied to the interior of the vehicle and filter fine particle such as dust and pollen etc the air filter is fitted on the box assembly it shall be checked on regular basis and replace when necessary air duct the face air outlet in the instrument panel is fitted with an individual air distribution duct air distribution duct for the windshield and front side window are integrated in the instrument panel air outlet assembly in the instrument panel allow occupant to control the flow and direction of air distribution to face the front seat footwell air outlet is fitted in the port beside the box assembly box assembly the box assembly heat and distribute the fresh air or circulating air according to the mode selected on the control panel the box assembly is below the instrument panel the box include the blower air filter heater core evaporator core and control damper the passage in the cboxanddividesitinto three part air is delivered to the air outlet for the face footwell and defrost fresh recirculating air damper is mounted on the box assembly housing which adjust the vehicle interior air or outside air and take it as the circulating air the drain outlet at the bottom of the box body direct condensed water from the box body to the lower side of the vehicle the damper is in the box which control the inlet air outlet air temperature and position of the outlet air recirculation damper the damper actuator drive the recirculation damper to open and close the fresh and recirculating air inlet so as to control the inlet air temperature damper the damper actuator or the damper cable drive the temperature damper to vary the mixing ratio of the cold warm air so as to control the temperature of the outlet air mode damper the damper actuator or the damper cable drive the mode damper to vary the air passing through the box and air duct so as to meet the user different need such as face footwell windshield etc blower the blower is the power source of the whole ventilation system use the blower to drive interior air circulation or introduce fresh air into the vehicle the blower is in the box assembly control of the blower air volume can be realized by using the button on the control panel to control the blower speed control resistor of the box assembly body relief valve the body relief valve allow the warm air and vent air to go through the passenger compartment freely the body relief valve is located at both side of the luggage compartment which allow air in the passenger compartment to be discharged to the covered area between the body and hvac system hvac structure rear bumper the air vent is an effective check valve and each vent contain grille covered by soft rubber damper the damper can open or close automatically according to the pressure difference between the passenger compartment and the external environment heater component it provide heat for the heating heater core the heater core heat the air supplied to each distribution outlet the heater core fitted in the box assembly is an aluminum unidirectional flat tube heat exchanger two aluminum tube are fitted on the heater core they extend through the engine compartment wall and connect the heater core assembly to the engine coolant circulation system when the engine work coolant constantly flow through the heater core under the action of coolant pump hvac structure hvac system cooling system system schematic liquid refrigerant gas refrigerant evaporator txv pressure switch high pressure service connector cooling fan filter desiccant condenser compressor low pressure service connector the cooling system transfer heat from the vehicle to the outside atmosphere and provide the heater assembly with dehumidified cool air the system comprise compressor condenser txv pipeline and an evaporator the system is closed loop system which apply refrigerant as the heat transfer medium add oil to the refrigerant to lubricate the internal component of the compressor to accomplish the transfer of heat the refrigerant is circulated around the system where it pass through two pressure temperature mode in each pressure temperature mode thestateoftherefrigerantchange duringthisprocess the refrigerant absorb and release maximum heat the low pressure temperature mode start with the txv through the evaporator to the compressor in the txv the refrigerant decrease in pressure and temperature then it change from moderate temperature liquid to low temperature vapour in the evaporator before absorbing heat from the ambient air of the evaporator the high pressure temperature mode start with the compressor through the condenser to the txv the pressure and temperature of the refrigerant increase as it pass through the compressor then it release heat to the atmosphere via the condenser and change from high temperature vapour to moderate high temperature liquid hvac system hvac structure compressor air intake port relief valve exhaust port electromagnetic clutch connector the compressor circulate the refrigerant by compressing the low pressure low temperature vapour from the evaporator and converting it into the high pressure high temperature vapour to the condenser the compressor under the mounting bracket of the transmission is driven by high pressure motor with fixed displacement the compressor provide the system with required amount of refrigerant by the change of high pressure motor speed condenser condenser air intake port liquid outlet port the condenser transfer the refrigerant heat into the ambient air and change the refrigerant vapour from the compressor into liquid the condenser also remove moisture and solid impurity from the refrigerant with its drying module and function as reservoir for liquid refrigerant so as to accommodate change of heat load in the evaporator with the impact effect and or cooling fan the air passing through the heat exchanger absorb heat from the refrigerant and change the refrigerant from vapour to liquid the condenser cool and liquify the refrigerant before it enter the modulator in the modulator most of the remaining gas in the refrigerant is separated the refrigerant use the desiccant and filter to remove moisture and particle before entering into the secondary cooler the refrigerant is further cooled as it flow through the secondary cooler this result in the refrigerant from the condenser outlet to the evaporator is almost converted into liquid thermostatic expansion valve txv txv txv and evaporator evaporator ring txv bolt the txv regulate the flow of the refrigerant and match the refrigerant flow with the heat load of the air passing through the evaporator core the txv is fitted on the inlet and outlet port of the evaporator the valve consist of an aluminum housing containing inlet and outlet passage metering valve is fitted in the inlet passage and the metering valve is controlled by temperature sensitive tube connected to diaphragm the top of the diaphragm is filled with refrigerant and sense evaporator outlet pressure and the tube sense evaporator outlet temperature by regulating the opening of the thermostatic expansion valve the force can be balanced this keep proper degree of superheat for the evaporator outlet and balance the refrigerant flow and the thermal load of the air hvac structure hvac system thermostatic expansion valve membrane housing metering valve inlet passage to evaporator outlet passage from evaporator thermo sensitive tube the liquid coolant flow from the metering valve to the evaporator the metering valve reduce the pressure and temperature of the refrigerant change the solid stream of refrigerant into fine spray and improve the evaporation effect as the refrigerant pass through the evaporator it absorb heat from the ambient air flowing through the evaporator core the increase of the temperature cause the refrigerant to vaporize and increase its pressure the temperature and pressure of the refrigerant from the evaporator drive the diaphragm and temperature sensitive tube to move and regulate the metering valve opening thereby controlling the volume of the refrigerant flowing through the evaporator the warmer the air flowing through the evaporator core the more heat available to evaporate refrigerant and this allow more refrigerant to pass through the metering valve evaporator the evaporator is in the air inlet of the heater assembly and absorb heat from the exterior or recirculated inlet air low pressure and low temperature refrigerant change from liquid to vapour in the evaporator absorbing large quantity of heat during this process pipeline aluminum pipeline connect the system component together with ring between the connection to ensure secure seal to maintain similar flow velocity in the system the diameter of the refrigerant line may vary so as to suit the two pressure temperature mode the pipeline with larger diameter are fitted in the low pressure temperature mode and those with smaller diameter are fitted in the high pressure temperature mode and integrate the refrigerant refill port into the pipeline so as to facilitate the system service hvac system hvac structure service procedure refrigerant recovery and refill refrigerant recovery connect the high pressure side hose with quick disconnection interface to the high pressure service side connector of the system tip use refrigerant tank designed only for service station overcharging restraint system is designed based on this kind of refrigerant tank the tank valve is also designed for the restraint system open the interface valve connect the low pressure side hose with quick disconnection interface to the low pressure service side connector of the system open the interface valve check the high pressure side and low pressure side gauge on the refilling station control panel and ensure there is pressure in the if there is no pressure there is recoverable refrigerant in the system open the high pressure side and low pressure side valve open the gas and liquid valve on the refrigerant reservoir drain refrigerant in the oil separator close the drain valve connect the refilling station to the appropriate power socket connect the main power switch caution reuse the recycled lubrication is prohibited the recycled lubrication will damage the equipment caution part of lubrication pag could be recycled together with refrigerant but service station can separate lubrication from refrigerant to calculate the quantity of lubrication start the recycle process for information about the refilling station refer to the manufacturer instruction for use wait for before checking the low pressure side gauge on the control panel if the system is capable of keeping vacuum the recovery is completed warning if warning indicator illuminate it mean the tank is overfull and service station will stop install new empty tank to continue this process the different type of tank is prohibited using if the pressure at the low pressure side start to rise from there remain refrigerant in the system recover the residual refrigerant repeat this step until the system can keep vacuum for minute drain check if the high pressure side and low pressure side hose are connected to the system open the high low pressure side valve on the refilling station control panel open the gas and liquid valve on the refrigerant reservoir caution the evacuating procedure must be carried out before recharging process start the vacuum pump and start the drain process in the process of recirculation non condensable gase mostly air will be automatically exhausted from the reservoir sound of pressure release may be heard check the system for leak for information about the refilling station refer to the manufacturer instruction for use system lubricating oil refill at this time the lubricating oil discharged from the system during recovery must be supplemented note alway keep the lubricating oil bottle cap tight to prevent moisture or contaminant from entering the lubricating oil this operation require vacuum system it is forbidden to open the lubricating oil filling valve when the system is at positive pressure this will result in backflow of lubricating oil through the bottle air vent an oil level lower than the suction pipe is prohibited otherwise air will enter the system use the special polyether type lubricating oil contained in graduated bottle for system for information about the refilling station refer to the manufacturer instruction for use add an appropriate amount of lubricating oil to the system when the oil refilled reach the required point close the valve refill refrigerant refill close the low pressure side valve on the control panel open the high pressure side valve on the control panel for information about the refilling station refer to the manufacturer instruction for use fill refrigerant of the required amount into the and ensure the unit of measurement is correct for example kg or lb start refill hvac structure hvac system refrigerant refill completion refrigerant refill completion close the high pressure side valve on the refilling station control panel both valve shall be closed start the vehicle and system keep the engine running until the reading on the high pressure side pressure gauge and the low pressure side pressure gauge are stable compare the reading with the system specification check the outlet temperature of the evaporator and ensure that the system operation conform to the system specification keep the running close the high pressure side interface valve disconnect the high pressure side hose from the vehicle open the low pressure side valve on the control panel the system will quickly suck in refrigerant from two hose through the low pressure side hose close the low pressure side interface valve disconnect the low pressure side hose from the vehicle refrigerant refill failure refrigerant refill failure sometime the refrigerant is not completely refilled into the system there are two reason the pressure of the refrigerant reservoir in the refilling station is close to that of the system which lead to slow oil delivery for information about the refilling station refer to the manufacturer instruction for use of the refilling station therefore part of the refrigerant refilled must be recovered from the vehicle then drain the system before refilling for information about the refilling station refer to the manufacturer instruction for use compressor remove recycle the system negative temperature coefficient thermistor the sensor is in the evaporator which is used to measure the surface temperature of the evaporator when the temperature monitored is lower than certain value the compressor will be requested to turn off to prevent the evaporator freezing pressure switch the pressure sensor signal vary within when the refrigerant pressure is low its signal value will approach to when the refrigerant pressure is high its signal value will approach to the engine control module ecm convert the pressure sensor voltage signal to pressure value when the pressure is too high or too low the engine control module will stop the compressor hvac etc hvac system operation overview the electric controller operate according to the following input mode actuator position temperature actuator position blower speed evaporator temperature value pressure value the electric controller control the following after manual setting on the panel blower speed mode damper actuator position temperature damper actuator position internal external circulation damper motor position blower speed control when the request of blower speed change is shown on the control panel the controller control the speed of the blower motor by increasing or decreasing the voltage at the blower motor ground end with the blower speed control resistor intake control when the request of intake air source change is shown on the control panel the controller enable the internal external circulation actuator to change the damper angle temperature control when the request of temperature change is shown on the control panel the controller enable the temperature actuator to change the damper angle mode control when the request of mode change is shown on the control panel the controller enable the mode actuator to drive the damper angle compressor control when refrigeration request is shown on the control panel the controller will receive the switch signal and then transmit to the engine control module via the bus the engine control module will engage the compressor clutch meanwhile the engine accessory belt drive the compressor to operate thereby driving the refrigerant to circulate during the operation of compressor the engine control module may cut off the supply of clutch in the following situation the engine control module receive feedback from the pressure switch and the value is higher or lower than certain threshold the engine control module receive the temperature value from the evaporator temperature sensor and the value is lower than certain threshold the engine control module receive the request of acceleration hvac system hvac etc service procedure controller assembly etc remove disconnect the battery negative remove the panel etc ca ca panel etc remove front wheel steering steering system description and operation system layout steering system component layout steering wheel steering column assembly steering gear assembly steering pump steering reservoir assembly steering oil inlet pipe assembly steering oil return pipe assembly steering system front wheel steering power steering gear exploded view power steering gear subassembly nut steering gear outer tie rod steering gear outer tie rod assembly nut steering gear outer tie rod to knuckle steering gear boot nut steering gear bolt steering gear steering gear input shaft seal steering gear outer tie rod assembly steering gear inner tie rod front wheel steering steering system power steering hydraulic system layout steering reservoir assembly cooler to reservoir oil return pipe assembly steering reservoir to steering pump oil suction pipe assembly clamp steering reservoir to steering pump oil suction pipe steering reservoir bracket clamp steering reservoir to steering pump oil suction pipe steering pump to steering gear oil inlet pipe assembly steering gear to cooler oil return pipe assembly steering cooling pipe assembly bolt steering cooling pipe steering pump assembly bolt power steering pump bolt power steering pump nut power steering pump steering gear to cooler oil return pipe clamp bolt steering pump to steering gear oil inlet pipe bolt steering pump to steering gear oil inlet pipe bolt steering gear to cooler oil return pipe steering system front wheel steering description overview the steering system mainly include two part foldaway steering column power steering gear power steering fluid pump power steering fluid reservoir steering fluid cooler and steering fluid hard pipe and hose power steering gear the power steering gear is fitted in an appropriate position under the rear beam of the front subframe since the mounting point of the power steering gear and the suspension pivot are determined by the precisely machined rigid front subframe it is possible to maintain the relative designed geometric dimension between the steering pinion and rack mechanism and the suspension at any time the power steering gear is fixed by two bolt and nut the power steering gear shall rotate cycle from one locking position to another and the rubber bellow hood cover the moving area of the steering pinion and rack one end of the steering outer tie rod is fitted on the threaded steering gear and fixed with locking nut which can be used to adjust alignment of the steering system the other end of the steering outer tie rod is entirely fixed with locking nut to the steering arm stretching out from the front wheel hub power steering pump the pressure of the steering system is supplied by the vane power steering pump which is driven by the crankshaft through steering pump belt when the engine operate hydraulic oil is pumped out of the fluid reservoir by the steering pump the fluid amount to be transferred and pressure are adjusted by the flow control valve in the power steering pump and the excess fluid return to the inlet of the oil pump through internal oil passage warning care is required when working on the hydraulic system since the high pressure fluid can be hot and when the pump is operating reach pressure in excess of kpa lbf causing risk of personal injury power steering fluid flow the fluid pressure from the power steering fluid pump is transferred to the conversion valve via the steering pinion the control valve control the fluid pressure and change the fluid flow direction the fluid is directed to the pressure cylinder on the corresponding side of the steering gear generating steering pinion and rack propelling force the pressure fluid return from the pressure cylinder of the steering gear through circular channel of the fluid return pipe before the power fluid return to the steering fluid reservoir in this circular channel the power steering fluid cool the steering fluid reservoir store the power steering fluid power steering fluid reservoir the power steering fluid reservoir consist of the body lid and filter assembly on the lid there is fluid level gauge of steering fluid reservoir integrated with the lid and an ring preventing fluid leakage the filter assembly is fitted at the bottom of the steering fluid reservoir the filter is made of fine stainless steel wire mesh molded in the filter bracket the filter detract particle in the steering fluid reservoir before the steering fluid enter into the fluid supply pipeline the filter is maintenance free component the basic function of the steering fluid reservoir is to maintain certain amount of surplus steering fluid in the hydraulic system to allow the expansion and contraction of the steering fluid at different temperature ensure that the fluid supply pipe is alway covered by the steering fluid under all operating condition any air in the hydraulic system will be discharged from the liquid inside the steering fluid reservoir front wheel steering steering system operation the turning of the steering wheel is transferred to the conversion valve on the rack mechanism of steering gear by the steering column the rotational motion of the steering wheel is transformed to linear motion of the steering rack through the gear and rack when the engine and the power steering pump operate the high pressure fluid flow in the steering gear offer steering power neutral position rotor torsion bar slide valve pressure oil flow in the cylinder of steering gear pressure oil in power steering pump oil return to reservoir reservoir supply when the steering wheel is in the driving forward state the power steering fluid will flow from the power steering pump to the conversion valve slot of the outer cylindrical slide valve are in line with the rotor slot so that the fluid pressure is over the conversion valve some pressure are applied to the fluid inlet and outlet side of the power steering cylinder and sensed by piston on both side of the power steering cylinder as the power steering cylinder has equal pressure on both side the steering wheel shall be kept in the middle position because most fluid has returned to the steering fluid reservoir the pressure at each side of the piston is very low steering system front wheel steering turn left rotor torsion bar slide valve pressure oil flow in the left cylinder of steering gear pressure oil in power steering pump oil return to reservoir oil flow in the right cylinder of steering gear when the steering wheel is rotated counterclockwise the rotor and torsion bar rotate in the same direction the slot position on the rotor move from the middle aligned position blocking the fluid flow from returning to the steering fluid reservoir from now on the fluid pressure from the power steering pump flow into the slot newly aligned by the rotor and the outer cylindrical slide valve from the power steering pump the liquid pressure is directed to the left side of the power steering cylinder through the aligned slot so that the pressure of the left power steering cylinder increase the return flow port of the right power steering cylinder is open allowing the fluid to flow into the power steering fluid reservoir from the right power steering cylinder piston the pressure difference between left and right power steering cylinder piston generate steering power front wheel steering steering system turn right rotor torsion bar slide valve oil flow in the left cylinder of steering gear pressure oil in power steering pump oil return to reservoir pressure oil flow in the right cylinder of steering gear when the steering wheel is rotated clockwise the rotor and torsion bar rotate in the same direction the slot position on the rotor move from the middle aligned position blocking the fluid flow from returning to the steering fluid reservoir from now on the fluid pressure from the power steering pump flow into the slot newly aligned by the rotor and the outer cylindrical slide valve from the power steering pump the liquid pressure is directed to the right power steering cylinder throughthealignedslot steering cylinder increase the return flow port at the left allowingthefluidto steering cylinder piston the pressure difference between left and right power steering cylinder piston generate steering power cumulative power the cumulative power steering depend on the road resistance of the wheel opposite to the rotation direction of the front wheel when the steering wheel is turned left or right the rotational motion is passed to the rotor shaft through the steering column making the rotor shaft rotate in the same amount of rotation as the steering wheel the rotational motion is also passed to the torsion bar from the rotor if the wheel resistance is very high for example in the parking state the torsion bar will rotate the rotation of the torsion bar mean that the amount of rotation of the pinion and slide valve is less than that of the rotor the rotation of the torsion bar make the slot in the rotor and the torsion bar out of the alignment position the gap between the two slot become bigger the greater the road resistance of the wheel to the steering rotation is the larger the misalignment steering system front wheel steering of the slot in the rotor and the torsion bar become as the misalignment increase the liquid pressure flowing through the corresponding side of the power steering cylinder increase when the road resistance of the wheel or the steering force applied to the steering wheel decrease the torque applied to the rotor decrease the torsion bar is released thus the misalignment of the slot in the rotor and the torsion bar decrease and the fluid pressure applied to the corresponding side of the power steering cylinder decrease hydraulic circuit steering oil reservoir low pressure oil inlet pipe power assisted steering pas pump oil outlet flow control safety valve pressure area of power steering cylinder rack mechanism of steering gear conversion valve unit power steering oil cooling pipe after the engine start the power steering pump suck the power steering fluid from the steering fluid reservoir into the low pressure fluid inlet pipe and turn the fluid to the high pressure fluid at the fluid outlet though high pressure fluid pipe the high pressure fluid come to the conversion valve if no steering force is applied the pressure at both side of the power steering cylinder piston are equal the remaining fluid return to the steering fluid reservoir from front wheel steering steering system the conversion valve through power steering fluid cooling pipe if steering force of turning to any side is applied the fluid pressure is directed to the corresponding side of the power steering cylinder piston to offer steering power so as to reduce the necessary steering force the discharged power steering fluid due to piston movement in the power steering cylinder return to the steering fluid reservoir from the conversion valve through power steering fluid cooling pipe the power steering fluid cooling pipe reduce the temperature of the power steering fluid extending the service life of the hose and seal of the system steering system front wheel steering service procedure power steering system pressure test check test equipment fit the pressure gauge and hose to the valve assembly and tighten the connector fit hose to the valve assembly and tighten the connector disconnect the battery negative caution care must be taken to ensure that oil or fluid doe not enter or contaminate the alternator place container to collect the oil spilled from the pas system clean the power steering pump connecting pipe and pipe connector remove the connector connecting the oil inlet pipe of the steering gear to the power steering pump fit the connector and seal washer to the power steering pump fit the connector to the oil inlet pipe of the steering gear connect the hose to the and the ensure all pipe and mounting part are not in contact with rotating part hang the pressure gauge at safe place below the bonnet connect the battery negative start the engine and bleed the power steering system power steering system bleeding check the liquid level of the power steering pump and fill it up capacity test procedure ensure there is no leakage in the steering system and the liquid level is at the maximum position at the time of testing start the engine when the test equipment valve is open slowly rotate the steering wheel and keep it at lock position when the engine is running at idle speed and at normal operating temperature repeat this pressure test at relative lock position the test pressure shall be increased gradually from to and the pressure may rise to bar according to road condition when the steering wheel is at full lock position release the steering wheel when the engine is running at idle speed and the reading shall be or less if the pressure exceed the above value there may be something wrong adjustment keep the test valve closed for no more than second determine if the failure occur in the steering pump or in the steering gear if the test valve is off for an extended time the pump will be damaged if the reading on the pressure gauge can not reach maximum pump pressure there may be something wrong with the pump if the maximum pump pressure is correct there may be something wrong with the steering gear stop the engine after the test disconnect the battery negative remove the connector of the test equipment remove the connector from the power steering pump and the oil inlet pipe of the power steering gear fit the new seal washer fit the oil inlet pipe to the power steering pump and tighten to clean the chassis and its surrounding area check the liquid level of the power steering system capacity fluid connect the battery negative front wheel steering steering system steering gear outer specification torque description value nut steering rod parameter front wheel camber angle king pin caster angle king pin inclination angle toe in total toe toe in one side rear wheel wheel camber angle toe in total toe suspension system wheel alignment description and operation description the primary goal of mechanical design is to ensure the safety and reliability of the steering system and suspension system each component shall be strong enough to bear and absorb extreme impact the steering system and front rear suspension system shall be in geometrical shape with the body the steering system and suspension system require the front wheel can return automatically keeping the rolling force of the front wheel and the road friction within the minimum value so that the user can control direction easily and comfortably thorough wheel positioning inspection involve rear wheel toe angle and camber measurement four wheel positioning can ensure the four wheel to rotate toward the same direction geometric positioning can help the vehicle to realize optimum fuel economy and maximum tyre service life and thus improve the steering and performance front wheel toe in the front wheel toe in is observed from the top the wheel rotate inward or outward normally the actual value of the toe in angle is less than one degree merely several cent the toe in of the static wheel enable the wheel to roll levelly under dynamic state large toe in or the front tensioner may increase the wheel friction for the rear wheel driving vehicle smaller toe angle the value measured when the vehicle is stopped shall be used to compensate the weak disturbance caused by rolling resistance and brake operation this disturbance may cause the wheel open outward wheel alignment suspension system service procedure four wheel alignment inspection only the recommended equipment by the company can be used for four wheel positioning adjustment the equipment must be calibrated according to the manufacturer requirement ensure that the vehicle data input into the equipment is correct check the steering rod ball joint front lower arm ball joint and wheel bearing for wear or proper running replace or repair it if necessary check and adjust the tyre pressure raise the vehicle on four wheel aligner ensure that the vehicle is in unladen mass condition ensure that the wheel mounting clamp has been adjusted to correct specification and has been fixed firmly to the vehicle ensure that the camera has been fitted and aligned properly align and adjust the wheel according to the manufacturer instruction lower the vehicle and keep the vehicle suspension in normal state ensure that the steering wheel is positioned in the centre adjustment observe the reading indicated on the test equipment and adjust the four wheel positioning dada according to the requirement front wheel alignment adjustment release the locking nut of the steering rod adjust the steering rod and allow the front wheel positional parameter to achieve the specified value tighten the locking nut to for the other side repeat the above step suspension system front suspension front suspension specification torque description value steel forged with lightening hole ball joint at forward inner and outer joint anti roll bar diameter front spiral spring data total turn active coil wire diameter front suspension suspension system description and operation system layout front suspension front suspension shock absorber assembly front anti roll bar link front subframe assembly front anti roll bar front wheel hub assembly front anti roll bar bush clamp front lower arm assembly suspension system front suspension front suspension front shock absorber column front anti roll bar link front anti roll bar front anti roll bar bush front anti roll bar anchorage bracket front lower arm front bushing front lower arm assembly front lower arm rear bushing front suspension knuckle dust cover front wheel hub front wheel hub bearing front wheel hub circlip front wheel hub flange front drive shaft nut front shock absorber coil spring front suspension suspension system front damper front shock absorber column front suspension coil spring front shock absorber column dust cover front shock absorber column auxiliary spring front suspension shock absorber column insulating pad front shock absorber lower spring cushion front shock absorber bearing seat nut front shock absorber upper spring cushion front shock absorber rebound gasket nut suspension system front suspension description front suspension comprise of front macpherson strut damper front lower arm anti roll bar anti roll bar link front subframe front wheel hub bearing and drive flange the front suspension design feature anti dive and anti squat geometry via the anti roll bar and strut movement ratio the layout and design of the strut minimiz camber loss due to side force improving handling and steering response front subframe assembly the front subframe is fabricated steel construction made up of precise component welded together to form light weight high structural strength assembly geometric machining of all key position take place after construction to ensure the precise location of front suspension and steering system component the subframe is attached to the body via solid mounting point this help to increase the stiffness of the front subframe the arrangement of the supporting point avoid the displacement of the front subframe under high lateral force which could affect handling characteristic macpherson strut two macpherson strut control the damping of the front suspension each strut assembly comprise damper unit coil spring and top mount assembly the coil spring is retained in compressed condition between the strut spring seat and the top mount assembly isolator at each end of the spring reduce noise transmitted from the suspension to the body bearing is fitted to the underside of the top mount assembly and allow the spring to rotate as the steering is operated dust cover is fitted between the bump stop and the damper and protect the damper rod from dirt and damage the coil spring axis is offset to the axis of the damper this arrangement provide side load compensation which reduce friction within the damper damper each damper is handed is gas charged and oil filled and is of twin tube construction the twin tube construction allow the damper piston to operate on an inner tube with an oil gas cavity between the inner and outer tube this minimise oil cavitation and eliminate the effect of damage to the outer tube affecting piston operation the damper is attached at its lower end to location on the swivel hub and is secured with nut bolt correctly orientation is achieved by machined section of the front hub being inserted in between steel tang welded to the base of the damper the machined hub and tang contain hole which allow the fitment of the retaining nut bolt the damper rod is located in steel insert in rubber bush andaresecured with washer and nut hexagon is provided in the outer end of the damper rod to restrain the rod from rotating when the nut is tightened the damper rod is sealed in the damper with low friction seal an angled spring seat is integral with the damper body welded bracket on the body of the damper provide the location point for the anti roll bar link front lower arm assembly the front lower arm connect the front wheel hub to the front subframe the fabricated steel front lower arm feature an outer ball joint front bush and rear bush all component are serviceable the outer ball joint feature parallel pin with circumferentially machined groove that locate into the hub assembly and is clamped secured using nut and bolt anti roll bar and link solid spring steel arb is used that operate via arb link secured to each damper dedicated bracket the arb is attached to the front of the subframe with two polytetrafluoroethylene ptfe bush that are secured with clamp plate and bolt the low friction bush ptfe require no additional greasing and allow the arb to rotate freely with quiet operation this allow the bar to respond quickly to roll input and allow stiffer bush to be used as there is relatively little compression or twisting washer are attached to the arb to locate the outside of each bush and prevent sideway movement of the bar front suspension suspension system the end of the arb are attached to each strut spring seat via an arb link this arrangement allow the arb to act on ratio of approximately with the wheel travel providing maximum roll bar effectiveness whichimprove response and efficiency directly to the damper strut and is secured with locknut the lower ball joint is mounted at to the axis of the link and attach to the arb and is secured with locknut the link must be attached to the arb with the ball joint on the outboard side of the bar and the locknut facing inward the ball joint on the arb link are not serviceable and if replacement of either is necessary new link will be required suspension system front suspension service procedure front hub remove raise vehicle on lift remove front wheel wheel remove with an rear suspension description rear suspension include coil spring damper frame member frame member the frame member consist of couple of trailing arm cross beam lower coil spring seat damper fixing point and stub axle the trailing arm are fabricated structure which is attached to the body via two fabricate mounting bracket forward of the rear wheel fabricated bracket inboard of each stub axle connected to the trailing arm and cross beam provide the lower coil spring seat the trailing arm rearward of the stub axle provide the lower damper fixing point stub axle is bolted with four bolt to each side of the frame and provide location for the rear brake and road wheel the twist beam principle use angled pivot bush which have special construction to decrease the vertical vibration of the body coil spring coil spring manufactured from chromium steel are fitted between the body and the lower arm each spring is located in upper and lower spring isolator which reduce noise transmitted to the body from the suspension both isolator are positively retained to the spring the lower isolator is positively retained by location pip on the lower arm hole in the lower isolator is provided for water drainage and must be aligned with corresponding hole in the lower arm when fitted rear shock absorber the damper are gas charged and oil filled and are of twin tube construction the twin tube construction allow the damper piston to operate on an inner tube with an oil gas cavity between the inner and outer tube this minimise oil cavitation and eliminate the effect of damage to the outer tube affecting piston operation the lower damper mounting is fitted with non serviceable bush that is attached to the trailing arm with bolt and washer this mounting position give the damper an approximate ratio of which is beneficial for damping control the damper rod is located in bush in top mounting and is secured with rebound plate and locknut the top mounting is attached to the body with two bolt the rebound plate fitted between the damper rod locknut and the top mounting cushion force as the damper rod reach full extension spring aid plate and spring aid are fitted over the rod and cushion the damper operation if maximum bump travel is reached rear suspension suspension system service procedure rear damper assembly remove remove wheel remove place suitable support under rear not use silicone lubricant on the or wheel rim or tyre edge clean tyre edge before removing use non silicone lubricant and water based lubricant to these area prior to refitting remove remove the wheel trim cover assembly with screwdriver remove highway type wheel bolt with circular ended wrench of the vehicle hand tool place the vehicle at the lifting platform and lift to certain height remove wheel remove the tyre and the wheel valve assembly warning use tyre changer to remove the tyre manual tool or prybar could damage the road wheel or tyre caution do not place the wheel face down on the ground to avoid scratching refit caution please clean the corrosive on the surface of the road wheel drum and brake disc before refitting if wheel disc and drum contact surface are not properly cleaned the wheel bolt could become loose and may cause the wheel to fall off while the vehicle is moving this will cause the vehicle to go out of control and could cause injury refit the wheel valve assembly refit the tyre with tyre changer and charge the tyre until the rim is put in place ensure that the locating ring at the outside of the tyre bead is around the wheel rim flange this method can ensure the right position of tyre bead perform the wheel dynamic balance operation again refit new and appropriate wheel balance block refit wheel tighten steel highway type wheel bolt to for tightening order please see the picture below to warning if there is oil leakage between the wheel and the brake disc or drum it will cause the wheel bolt to become loose and result in injury with the vehicle out of control never use heat to remove seized wheel this will shorten the life of the wheel wheel bolt hub and bearing wheel bolt must be tightened in correct sequence and to correct torque bleeding bolt evp to bracket brake system brake description and operation system layout brake system front brake caliper assembly front ventilated brake hole disc abs modulator brake booster assembly brake master cylinder front wheel speed sensor rear brake drum rear wheel speed sensor brake reservoir air intake manifold vacuum tube brake brake system description vacuum servo assembly the vacuum servo booster assembly provide boost during braking to reduce the pedal force required for braking in case of the vacuum booster assembly failure the hydraulic system maintain brake function but greater pedal braking force is required due to lack of vacuum assistance the vacuum booster assembly is connected to the bulkhead lhd pedal box rhd via stud the brake master cylinder assembly is connected via two fixing bolt at the front end of the vacuum booster assembly the brake servo assembly consist of circular housing which contain two diaphragm central plate control valve assembly input and output push rod and filter the input push rod is connected to the brake pedal the output push rod locate in the primary piston of the master cylinder protective gaiter is installed on the control valve assembly where it extend from the rear of the housing line from the engine non return valve is incorporated into the vacuum line the control valve assembly consist of valve body containing valve piston valve spring and an input rod spring the valve control vacuum port in the valve body the piston control an air inlet port between the valve and the piston reaction disc and ratio disc separate the piston from the output push rod guide tube on the front of the valve body is attached to the front diaphragm and supported in bush in the central plate return spring locate in the open end of the guide tube brake master cylinder assembly tandem brake master cylinder is installed on the brake servo assembly fluid is supplied via remote reservoir connected to the master cylinder via two rubber hose the master cylinder is fixed to the front of the brake servo via stud fixing when the brake pedal is pressed the brake master cylinder assembly produce hydraulic pressure so that the brake operate the tandem master cylinder contain two chamber and associated piston each piston chamber control circuit the rear piston produce brake pressure for the primary circuit this cover lhf rhr the front piston produce brake pressure for the secondary circuit and this cover rhf lhr the remote brake fluid reservoir is installed higher than the master cylinder in order to maintain gravitational feed the fluid reservoir is partitioned inside to feed fluid to each brake circuit independently thus avoiding the condition that both circuit could become devoid of fluid the brake fluid reservoir utiliz fluid level switch reed style switch featuring permanent magnet and float is installed in the fluid reservoir which is linked to the fluid level handbrake and ebd warning lamp in the instrument pack front brake each front brake consist of single piston sliding brake caliper assembly fitted to the hub and ventilated brake disc rear brake each rear brake consist of back plate brake drum brake shoe ahydraulicwheelcylinder aselfadjustingrodandreturn spring the back plate is installed on the stub axle retained by the stub axle bolt and provide location for brake shoe and hydraulic wheel cylinder the brake drum is fitted to the flange of bearing and rotate with the wheel each rearward brake shoe is fitted with handbrake cable lever brake pedal and brake lamp switch the brake pedal is installed on pivot shaft in the pedal mounting bracket on the engine bulkhead clevis pin and clip connect the input rod of the brake servo to the brake pedal tang on the end of the brake pedal activate plunger style brake switch in the pedal mounting bracket the brake switch is adjustable and secured in position using lock nut the brake lamp switch provide two brake pedal state output one power the other ground this signal is supplied to the ecm and used to illuminate the stop lamp brake system brake service procedure low brake fluid level indicator switch rhd remove module specification torque description value screw front speed wheel sensor screw rear speed wheel sensor bolt abs to bracket rear brake pipe to modulator brake system brake module description and operation system layout brake system control component layout brake fluid reservoir brake fluid level sensor vacuum booster brake master cylinder brake modulator front wheel speed sensor assembly rear wheel speed sensor assembly brake module brake system system control diagram brake system control chart brake system brake module description anti lock brake system abs during braking the function of the abs prevent wheel lock and thus the vehicle can stay stable even in emergency warning abs is an aid to retaining steering control and stability while braking abs cannot defy the natural law of physic acting on the vehicle abs will not prevent accident resulting from excessive cornering speed following another vehicle too closely aquaplaning etc the additional control provided by abs must never user the fitting of abs doe not imply that the vehicle will alway stop in shorter distance tip during normal braking the feel of the brake pedal on vehicle equipped with abs will be the same as that on non abs vehicle during anti lock braking operation the driver will experience feedback in the form of pulsating brake pedal and solenoid pump motor noise from the abs modulator in conventional brake the abs doe not work brake fluid directly flow into each wheel cylinder from the reservoir through hydraulic control unit hcu channel to apply brake to each wheel once abs ecu detect that wheel locking occur through the wheel speed sensor ecu will give an order to close the inlet valve and the brake fluid will no longer flow into the wheel cylinder meanwhile the outlet valve is opened to discharge the hydraulic oil from the wheel cylinder to allow the wheel to rotate if it is detected that wheel slip normally the outlet valve will be closed due to low brake pressure and the brake fluid will flow into the wheel cylinder from the inlet valve to increase the brake force if the wheel lock in emergency the inlet outlet valve will be closed at the same time to keep the brake pressure in the wheel cylinder so as to exert the best brake performance electronic brake distribution ebd ebdthe function of the ebd is to optimize the distribution of the hydraulic pressure between the front and rear axle to keep the vehicle at stable state in brake process according to the vehicle load condition and road condition with the abs ecu in the normal brake mode the vehicle deceleration of and above moderate to high brake pedal load the ebd function can be automatically implemented in forward or reverse mode buttheebdoperate at lower wheel slip limit and the ebd is only applicable to the rear wheel brake during braking if the abs ecu detect that the rear axle wheel is slower than the front axle wheel that is the possibility of wheel slip exist the abs ecu will send signal to the hydraulic pressure regulator to turn off the fluid inlet solenoid valve of the rear wheel brake this prevent further increase of the hydraulic pressure of the rear wheel brake but allow the hydraulic pressure of the front wheel brake to increase the whole brake force is thus maximized if the rear wheel speed return to the calibration wheel slip limit range the abs ecu will send signal to turn on the fluid inlet valve gradually which allow progressive increase of the hydraulic pressure of the rear axle brake ebdit operation can be felt through the following way the movement of the brake pedal is more violent by turning off the fluid inlet solenoid valve and the brake pedal has slight pulse as result of the fluid inlet solenoid valve turning on when the brake pedal is released the ebd will stop immediately ebdthe wheel slip limit that enable the ebd vary with the vehicle speed brake module brake system service procedure abs sensor front remove warning do not work circlip driveline axle driveline axle description each drive shaft comprise of solid shaft with inner and outer constant velocity joint the inner joint is of the tripode type with spherical bushing to reduce sliding resistance the shaft and joint are one assembly is splined to the shaft and secured with snap ring all joint are packed with grease and protected by gaiter both drive shaft are fitted with dynamic damper the drive shaft transmit engine torque via the gearbox to the front wheel the dynamic damper reduce any vibration produced by the drive shaft rotation driveline axle driveline axle diagnostic diagnosis mechanical check when rotating rattle sound can be heard when the worn or damaged constant velocity universal joint of the front wheel drive shaft rotate rattle sound may be heard if there rattle sound check the constant velocity universal joint shield of the front wheel drive shaft for crack or damage clunk sound can be heard during accelerating and sliding if click sound can be heard during rotation check the following check the external universal joint for wear or damage check the seal for breakage or damage shake or vibration when accelerating start shake start shake refer to the vibration felt on the steering wheel and or the front of the vehicle when the vehicle is shifted from static starting to rapid accelerating during acceleration start shake may also refer to the swing occur in the front and the rear of the vehicle the start shake of the front wheel drive vehicle may caused by the following internal tee universal joint is worn or damaged internal universal joint is severely worn or ceratoid the universal joint show severe ceratoid angle which is normally caused by the excessive height of the front cocked head or the spring when the power assembly suspension is damaged or mismatched following situation may occur angle of the universal joint is oversize start shake during rapid acceleration high torque of the vehicle power assembly cause increase of the front suspension when the suspension raise the angle of the internal tee universal joint willincreaseaccordingly situation appear universal joint is worn oversize angle exist to floor bolt squab to cushion bolt striker to body interior fitting seat description and operation system layout seat layout driver seat front passenger seat rear seat seat interior fitting description front seat assembly overview seat headrest seat back assembly seat back cover and foam seat back frame seat cushion assembly seat cushion cover and foam seat cushion frame the common seat mainly consist of headrest seat back seat cushion and accessory among them both seat back assembly and seat cushion assembly consist of cover foam and frame the seat assembly and the main component are repaired as fitting according to the vulnerability and maintainability of the component the front seat assembly may have different configuration according to vehicle configuration and seat exterior for example way manual seat forward backward adjustment seat back inclination way power seat forward backward adjustment seat back inclination and seat height adjustment fabric of high quality or pvc and fabric optional seat side airbag rear seat assembly the rear seat of this model is type seat with reclinable seat back if you want to expand the luggage compartment space first fully lower or remove all the rear seat headrest then pull up the unlock strap on both side respectively and fold the seat back forward when unfolding the rear seat back pull up the seat back unlock strap push the seat back until it reach an appropriate position and the seat back is locked when it click interior fitting seat operation manual seat driver seat seat longitudinal position adjusting handle seat height adjusting handle seat back adjusting handle the front rear position of the seat can be adjusted by the handle in the front of the seat lift the handle and pull it to the desired position release the handle to lock the seat to the selected position the adjusting handle is located in the front of the trim panel outside the seat cushion which can adjust the seat height lift or press the handle repeatedly to raise or lower the seat the adjusting handle is located in the rear of the height adjusting handle which can adjust the seat back inclination lift the handle and pull it to the desired position release the handle to lock the seat to the selected position front passenger seat the passenger seat is adjusted in the same way as the driver seat the front rear position of the seat can be adjusted by the handle in the front of the seat the position of the sear back can be adjusted by the side handle seat interior fitting service procedure front seat remove if the seat is adjustable raise the seat fully fully move the seat backward using suitable lever remove fixing trim cover remove bolt fixing front of seat to body discard fully move the seat forward using suitable lever remove fixing trim cover remove bolt fixing front of seat to body discard disconnect all electrical connector remove seat from vehicle refit place the front seat in the body front seat mounting position connect all electrical connector warning alway ensure srs wiring is routed correctly be careful to avoidtrapping or pinching the srs wiring do not leave the connector hanging loose or allow srs component to hang from their harness look for possible point of chafing fix the seat to the vehicle body fit new rear bolt and tighten them to refit fixing trim cover fully move the seat backward fit new front retaining bolt and tighten them to refit fixing trim cover front seat squab remove remove the front seat front seat remove release the interior trim panel fixing screw remove
