corrosion protection door and luggage compartment lid spray cavity wax from the process hole inside the door and luggage compartment lid to cover the internal sheet metal and frame with layer of protective film most of the cavity wax deposit is at the bottom of the sheet metal cavity and it will be finally drained from the hole at the bottom corrosion protection body repair body repair corrosion protection chassis adhesive function of chassis adhesive improve the stone chipping resistance of the underbody improve the corrosion resistance of the underbody sheet metal improve the seam sealing performance of the underbody sheet metal improve the sound insulation performance of the underbody caution ensure that suspension unit wheel tyre power unit exhaust and brake including all mounting point are shielded prior to application of fresh underbody sealer operation procedure for chassis adhesive cleaning to ensure the maximum viscosity of the product the underbody surface shall be clean and free from any dirt oil stain grease or wax residue inspection before operation thoroughly inspect the underbody for any potential damage or rust key location wheel arch lower side seam and overlap joint dust elimination eliminate the rust spot on the underbody with shovel or wire brush pay particular attention to the inaccessible location for example wheel frame overlap or seam check the type of the underbody coating check the type of the underbody coating and select suitable product read use instruction before operation please carefully read the instruction on the package of the product and view the technical data sheet read the safety instruction in the material safety data sheet carefully so that you can minimize some potential risk and hazard and achieve the optimum effect use cross spray technique spray the rust prevention product at distance of around spray in the horizontal direction first then spray the stiffener of the base plate from proper angle cross spray technique to achieve an optimum thickness of the wet film at least three spray shall be done drying time specific drying time shall be defined the coating material shall adhere to the underbody firmly and permanently different solution and different coating thickness may require different drying time product with high volatile solution require drying time at least clean spray gun use spray gun detergent to clean the spray gun immediately after use welding process body repair welding process welding process overview characteristic of welding process it is applicable to any shape and can be used to connect the integral body and the vehicle body integrity can be maintained after welding mass reduction can be achieved due to lack of the connecting part good sealing performance for air and water high production efficiency the welding connector strength is affected by the skill level of the operator excessive heating may cause surrounding panel deformation for modern vehicle the body welding repair usually adopt mag shielded arc welding resistance spot welding and soldering for the body panel welding repair an optimum welding method that will not reduce the original strength and durability of the body shall be applied depending on the specific situation spot welding or inert gas welding is preferred mag shielded arc welding mag shielded arc welding is mainly used for welding high strength low alloy steel body and aluminum casting etc advantage of the mag shielded arc welding include easy operation this method can melt the base metal completely low current can be used to weld thin metal which can diminish damage to the adjacent area strength stable and controllable arc suitable for welding location with gap and misalignment temperature and duration of the welding is controllable short heating time of the welding zone reduce fatigue and deformation of the base metal typical welding location and method butt welding fit two adjacent metal edge together fit mutually along the two metal panel or weld the butted edge overlap welding weld two overlapping metal panel plug welding punch hole on one or several exterior workpiece arc pass through the hole before entering into the interior workpiece and the hole is filled with the melting metal spot welding when the wire feed timing pulse is triggered current will be introduced into the two metal panel to be welded resistance spot welding the resistance spot welding is the most common method for welding the integral body this method can be used for the non deformable thin steel sheet on the body which require high welding strength suitable for roof window doorsill and external component etc the quality of the resistance spot welding depend on the voltage current pressure time and thickness of the metal panel to be welded etc the detail are as follow material and cleanness of the weldment the location between the weldment or the contact point between the weldment and the electrode shall be clean electrode pressing force insufficient force can cause spatter on the contact point excessive force can generate large current but it will lead to small welding spot diameter and penetration due to increase of the heat distribution zone welding current the welding spot diameter and welding strength increase with the current and large current and small pressure may also lead to spatter between the panel the spatter will be otherwise minimized welding time long welding time will cause much heat large diameter and deep penetration of the welding spot however prolonged welding time will lead to electrode impress and thermal deformation rather than increase of the welding spot arrangement of the welding spot the gap and margin of the welding spot distance between the spot and the panel edge is also crucial for determining the welding spot strength welding spot gap decrease can improve the weldment connection strength but the effect is actually limited excessive gap may cause the welding current to transfer through the last welding spot or leak then the added welding spot will not strengthen the connection strength of the weldment any more and it will have an adverse impact therefore the gap of the welding spot shall be beyond the area of current leak correct welding sequence shall be applied for the resistance spot welding continuous welding in one direction is generally not allowed when the electrode tip is hot and discolour stop welding and allow it to cool down to avoid crack due to stress concentration do not weld the boundary of the corner body repair welding process welding protection measure electronic control unit before welding repair is operated in consideration of the electronic control unit ecu refitted on the vehicle the following protection measure must be followed the plentiful heat and sharp vibration produced during these operation may damage the electronic control unit when disconnect or remove the airbag control module sdm pay special attention to the following proper protection measure protection measure for assistant protection system implement and remove or replace any item related to the assistant protection system srs be especially careful and comply with proper protection measure equipment when starting any test on the vehicle ensure that the related test equipment work normally and all harness and connector are in good state this is especially important to the electronic control unit seat belt rivet joint seat belt rivet joint is crucial for safety when repairing these area it is important to follow the following design specification note high strength low alloy steel hsla can be used at the installation position for seat belt try best to use the original production assembly including rivet joint of seat belt or the layout of cutting line should not interfere with the original rivet joint of seat belt carefully check the welding quality of the jointing for seat belt rivet joint manual wire brush for the spot welding is not obvious caution underbody coating using hot air gun prior to exposing spot weld the drill or clamping platoon welder for spot welding can be used to information vehicle access safety and restraint vehicle access description and operation system layout horn body control module right front door lock assembly right rear door lock assembly tail gate system control diagram vehicle access and safety control diagram hs can bus rf signal hard wire vehicle access safety and restraint description security system the function of the security system is to prevent illegal start of the vehicle when the ignition switch is turned to acc position the anti theft system can be disabled by the transmitter on the remote key this transmitter can be identified by the reader coil around the ignition switch and the coil transmit electric power and data to the remote key in frequency within certain distance away from the signal reader coil the security system include door switch luggage compartment switch bonnet switch security warning lamp hazard warning lamp button remote key ignition switch body control module bcm door switch each door lock assembly contain microswitch to notify the bcm of the door status when the door is open the microswitch contact will close to connect the ground circuit of the bcm when the door is closed the microswitch contact will disconnect and convert into an open circuit door switch bonnet and luggage compartment open switch are the main component of the alarm system when the alarm system work these switch will monitor the external intrusion into the vehicle the door open signal is also used by the bcm to indicate the door status information in the instrument pack information centre and control the interior lighting luggage compartment switch the luggage compartment lock motor contain microswitch used to notify the bcm of the luggage compartment lid status when the luggage compartment lid is closed the microswitch contact disconnect and the circuit of the bcm become an open circuit when the luggage compartment lid is open the switch will be closed and the ground circuit of the bcm will be connected canwhen the bus detect that the ground circuit is connected it will trigger the alarm the luggage compartment open microswitch is also used by the bcm to indicate the luggage compartment status information in the instrument pack information centre and automatically control the interior lighting safety and restraint vehicle access operation overview operation of vehicle access system the vehicle security system include the following three part peripheral system bonnet alarm if fitted door and luggage compartment lid open switch ignition switch central lock lock unlock switch acoustic alarm vehicle horn visible alarm hazard warning lamp the security system is divided into three independent zone and each zone include many discrete input signal see the table below when the security system is working any intrusion into these zone can trigger the alarm safety zone discrete signal front bonnet open switch middle inside driver door open switch front passenger door open switch left rear door open switch right rear door open switch driver door key unlock timed for wrong key is inserted into and placed in acc on position central lock lock unlock switch rear luggage compartment lid release switch complete anti theft status when the three zone of the security system are closed if lock request from the remote control or mechanical key is received the security system formally enter the complete anti theft status once in the complete anti theft status all activated safety zone will be monitored at all time when the switch of zone or is enabled if you attempt to lock the system from outside the vehicle will enter the partial lock status and give an error lock alarm bcmit will monitor the following outside lock request signal valid lock request from the remote key is received valid lock request from the driver door lock switch is received when the security system enter the complete anti theft status the bcm will allow the direction indicator lamp to give three flash complete anti theft status to partial anti theft status in the complete anti theft system if valid unlock request of the luggage compartment lid is received the bcm open the luggage compartment lid and the security system enter the partial anti theft status partial anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone only for example the bonnet is open zone and will enter the complete anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone only expect driver door for example door or more door are open zone and will enter the complete anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone only for example the luggage compartment lid is open zone and will enter the complete anti theft status partial anti theft status to complete anti theft status when each safety zone is in the security system the vehicle will enter the complete anti theft status for partial lock caused by error lock due to open door when the security system enter the anti theft status it can be disabled after being enabled within second and the alarm will not be triggered if the partial anti theft is caused by several zone when the relevant door cover of these zone are closed these zone will enter the complete anti theft status the entire security system enter the complete anti theft status only when all the zone enter the status error lock alarm with the door bonnet luggage compartment lid open and the ignition switch in the acc gear on gear or start gear or the engine running when using the mechanical key or remote key to send the lock request outside the vehicle the bcm will activate the horn to send an acoustic alarm appr to indicate error lock disable security system any of the following operation will cause the bcm to disable the security system valid unlock request from the remote key is received valid unlock request from the driver door switch is received valid emergency key is inserted into the ignition switch and switched from the acc gear to the on gear vehicle access safety and restraint caution ififif the driver driver driver door door door unlock unlock unlock switch switch switch isisis stuck stuck stuck ororor the switch switch switch circuit circuit circuit isisis short remote remote remote key will will will not bebebe affected affected affected when the security system is disabled the bcm will allow the direction indicator lamp to flash once unlock triggered by impact when the airbag control module sense an impact it will send an unlock request and then the door will unlock automatically except in the case of external locking auto relock after being unlocked with the remote key the vehicle will automatically return to the previous locked status if there is no operation within second in the auto relock status if the ignition switch is in acc position or valid key exist the current timing of the relock timer is cancelled and the auto relock function is cancelled auto lock if the driver door is closed and the vehicle is unlocked when the vehicle speed reach certain value preset the door will lock automatically auto unlock with the vehicle internally locked turn the ignition switch to the off position or pull out the key the vehicle will unlock automatically door lock controlled by internal central lock switch with the driver door closed and the impact signal disabled press the lock button of the central lock switch to lock all door with the vehicle door internally locked press the unlock button of the central lock switch to unlock all door when an impact occur and the impact signal is triggered the door unlock automatically the door cannot be relocked within current ignition cycle even if the lock button of the central lock switch is pressed when the vehicle door are externally locked the lock unlock button of the central lock switch do not work safety and restraint alarm system alarm system description and operation system layout lf anti theft coil alarm system safety and restraint system control diagram hs can bus rf signal hard wire safety and restraint alarm system description the vehicle is configured with an anti theft system connected with the ecm the characteristic value from the rf transceiver will be verified if the value match the corresponding data the engine can be started when the power is connected the key send the characteristic value to the lf anti theft coil and the serial data circuit to the bcm through the hard wire mounting position lf anti theft coil on the ign lock cylinder end view and pin information of lf anti theft coil harness connector pin no description immo transmission line immo transmission line alarm system safety and restraint operation the function of the anti theft system is provided by the bcm ecm and module which store and report environment identifier lf anti theft coil when the ignition key is inserted into the ign lock cylinder and the ignition switch is in on position by the lf anti theft coil of the ign lock cylinder then the rf transceiver transmit signal which contain its characteristic value bcm receive the signal and compare the value with that stored in the memory if the two value match the bcm send pre cancellation password to the ecm through the serial data circuit if the characteristic value of the rf transceiver is wrong the bcm send fuel disable password to the ecm when ecm receive the pre cancellation password from bcm the ecm will verify the password ecmthe ecm return the password verified to the bcm through the serial data circuit ecmthe ecm and bcm calculate the password if the result calculated by the bcm and ecm is consistent the ecm will allow the vehicle to start safety and restraint alarm system service procedure anti theft coil remove disconnect the battery negative remove the steering column trim cover fit the anti theft coil fit the steering column trim cover steering column trim cover refit connect the battery negative seat belt safety and restraint seat belt specification torque description value hs can bus hard wire safety and restraint seat belt description front seat belt pretensioner in case of collision the front seat belt pretensioner tighten this ensure the occupant are securely kept in their seat the seat belt pretensioner unit is fixed by bolt to the seat belt retractor assembly in the pillar the seat belt pretensioner connector is pin connector connected with the body harness in case of collision the seat belt pretensioner is controlled by the signal from the srs ecu the retractor of the two pretensioner are positioned and work in the same way each pretensioner unit is provided with an initiator and propulsion generator which act on the rotor of the seat belt retractor warning once the pre tensioner has been operated it cannot be reset the gas generator will be exhausted the unit must be replaced seat belt the seat belt provide the most basic protection for all occupant the restraint system also provide further protection for the front occupant through the airbag module and the pretensioning seat belt the front seat are all fitted with appropriate retractor and three point belt the vehicle retractor is type of emergency locking retractor elr the retractor is provided with an additional lock mechanism which include webbing sensor and an sensor if the webbing is suddenly pulled the webbing sensor will immediately start the lock mechanism if the vehicle is suddenly slowed down or tilt at large angle the sensor will start the lock mechanism the retractor will lock in case of impact so the front seat belt retractor is equipped with load limiter if the load limit has exceeded the critical level the load limiter make the seat belt webbing loose so that the load acting on the occupant are reduced front seat belt the retractor of the front seat belt is fitted on the body in the cavity at the bottom of the pillar lower trim panel pull the belt webbing from the retractor and fix it to the mounting point under the pillar rear seat belt the retractor of the rear seat belt at both side is fixed to the reinforcement panel of the rear wheelhouse inner panel with bolt one end of the webbing is fixed to the upper end of the pillar the other end is fixed to the mounting point of the body floor the retractor of the central seat belt is fixed to the seat frame with bolt and the other end is fixed to the lower part of the rear seat the right rear seat belt buckle and the central seat belt buckle form an assembly which is fixed by one bolt between the right rear seat and the central rear seat the left rear seat belt buckle is located at the occupant right hip seat belt safety and restraint seat belt warning the front seat belt buckle are integrated with the seat belt not tiedwarningswitch through srs ecu the seat belt warning will be triggered if the seat belt is not tied firmly during driving the warning message comprise an acoustic alarm and warning lamp on the instrument panel to prevent unnecessary warning some vehicle specification specify that pressure sensor shall be fitted within the foam of the passenger seat when there is no passenger on the seat if there is any passenger on the seat but the pressure sensor is not activated then the seat belt warning will not be triggered even if the occupant doe not wear the seat belt safety and restraint seat belt operation overview when the remote key is inserted the restraint system is activated with the ignition switch in on position the srs warning lamp illuminate for second then goe out this indicate that the system is in normal condition side impact normal operation of the side seat airbag and head curtain airbag depend on whether the severity level of the side impact detected by the side impact sensor exceed the threshold for triggering the side impact sensor when the srs ecu receive trigger signal from the side impact sensor it will activate the side seat airbag and curtain airbag module the triggered gas generator can produce large quantity of gas to expand the airbag the expanded airbag will eject from the seat upholstery similarly if the head curtain airbag module is triggered above the door to protect the passenger from head injury the fully expanded airbag will release excess gas to reduce the risk of passenger injury warning lamp check when the ignition switch is turned on the srs warning lamp shall illuminate then check the lamp if no failure occur in the srs the warning lamp shall go out after about second to monitor the subsequent ignition cycle system check include the state of srs ecu and srs harness system continuity fault with the ignition switch on if any of the following fault occur in the system the srs warning lamp will illuminate and remain on during subsequent ignition cycle ecuand srs failure harness failure ground connection failure open circuit airbag module failure seat belt pretensioner failure if the system detect any failure during driving the warning lamp will also illuminate to indicate failure of the srs system when the warning lamp illuminate the srs will not work when any impact occur withtheignitionswitchon thediagnosisfunctionofthesr ecu will monitor the srs if any fault is detected the srs ecu will store related dtc in nonvolatile memory and output signal to illuminate the srs warning lamp the failure information in the memory can be read with the scan tool low voltage failure when the voltage supply is not within the specified range the malfunction indicator lamp will illuminate the dtc is stored in the memory intermittent failure the warning lamp will illuminate at the moment and remain on in subsequent driving cycle the warning lamp will not illuminate in the next ignition unless the failure occur again but the dtc is still stored in the srs ecu memory permanent failure for permanent failure the srs lamp will illuminate when the test is started and it will remain on in every subsequent ignition cycle until the cause of the failure is solved in addition the diagnosis system will also record the information of the failure occurred when any failure is detected the system will retain certain operating function if it is detected that the circuit of the srs outside the srs ecu is faulty srs ecu will still activate the airbag and seat belt pretensioner module when the srs ecu failure or power supply failure is detected the whole system will not work if the circuit of the srs warning lamp is faulty the warning lamp will not illuminate when the ignition switch is turned on for initial check of the warning lamp if there is no other fault in the system all function of the system are available in case of crash additional information provided by the scan tool for dlc include srs ecudtc version level of hardware and software impact mode state vehicle identification number vin data srs ecuthe srs ecu record fault information in the memory and the information can be read by connecting the scan tool to the dlc below the closure plate of the driver side instrument panel seat belt safety and restraint service procedure front seat belt assembly remove remove key from ignition switch disconnect battery lead earth first and wait minute for thesr back up power gateway safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint system control diagram airbag control diagram hs can bus hard wire supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint description overview the srs can provide passive protection for the passenger when severe impact occur this system is usually based on application of the standard protection system seat belt the srs consist of the following component supplementary restraint system ecu srs ecu driver airbag module front passenger airbag module seat side airbag module if equipped seat belt pretensioner if equipped side impact sensor if equipped rotary coupler srs warning lamp on the ipk seat belt not tied warning lamp on the ipk srs ecuthe srs ecu connect the vehicle dlc through the hs can bus supplementary restraint system ecu srs ecu srs ecuit determine the affected range of the front impact side impact or rear impact through the internal and external impact sensor if equipped so as to control the srs srs ecuit is fitted on the central passage beneath the centre console which is secured with three screw srs ecuit is connected to the body harness through two connector srs ecuit monitor the vehicle impact through the internal accelerometer and the external sensor srs ecuit can identify the type and severity level of the impact according to these input data and ensure normal operation of the airbag if the severity of the front impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send signal to activate the front airbag module and seat belt pretensioner when fitting the side airbag two side impact sensor shall be fitted to monitor the side impact if the severity of the side impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send signal to activate the corresponding side airbag module and seat belt pretensioner if the severity of the rear impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send signal to activate the seat belt pretensioner caution it is important that the srs ecu is correctly mounted and is fitted in the designated location and orientation to ensure correct operation ecumonitoring after the vehicle is powered on the srs ecu monitor the preparation of the srs module after start it monitor the system continuously srs ecuthe srs ecu monitor the status of the following module srs ecuinternal accelerometer srs ecumicro processor side impact sensor if equipped driver side airbag module passenger side airbag module side airbag module if equipped seat belt pretensioner if equipped diagnosis count if any system failure is detected the srs warning lamp will illuminate to warn the driver the recommended diagnostic device can be used to check the srs malfunction the diagnostic information can be read include current malfunction frequency of malfunction and duration of each malfunction power supply and backup situation the power supply provide voltage for the srs ecu through special system fuse in the passenger compartment fuse box in case of power failure check the connection status of the passenger compartment fuse box and the fuse and harness of the srs ecu when the ignition switch is in on position the srs will be activated srs ecuthe srs ecu contain capacitor which can save electricity and ensure the corresponding trigger device and power supply is cut off by an impact side impact sensor for vehicle with side airbag module the side impact sensor is besidethebodysillframe the sensor is designed with mounting position for correct refit each sensor include an electronic circuit and sensing unit the side impact sensor send an acceleration signal to the safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint ecu and the ecu determine whether to trigger it through calculation caution the side impact crash sensor must be fitted in the correct orientation ensure the fixing screw do not damage the sensor or sensor harness when refitting the front seat driver airbag module airbag cover housing gas generator airbag connector the driver airbag module is fitted on the steering wheel by three bulged pin and one airbag module connector when fitting the driver airbag module push the airbag module toward the steering wheel firmly to embed the wire spring around the airbag module into the opening of the pin the electrical connection between the driver airbag module and the srs ecu is realized by the rotary coupler which is connected to the steering wheel by connector the driver airbag module is triggered by the control signal from the srs ecu folding airbag and gas generator are mounted in the module when severe front impact is detected by the srs ecu it will trigger the gas generator and expand the nylon bag rapidly front passenger airbag module warning after replacing the vehicle the passenger restraint system may not be capable of normal operation for hour warning if new airbag module show any sign of damage do not use airbag housing airbag cover airbag connector gas generator the front passenger airbag module is on the glove box of the instrument panel right before the front passenger seat the airbag module is fixed on the instrument panel with bolt the front passenger airbag module is triggered by the control signal from the srs ecu folding airbag and gas generator are mounted in the module when severe front impact is detected by the srs ecu it will trigger the gas generator and expand the nylon bag rapidly side airbag if equipped warning if new airbag module show any sign of damage do not use airbag housing airbag connector bracket the side airbag is outside the seat bracket below the seat upholstery it can protect the rib pelvis and upper visceral organ in case of side impact if the side impact is strong enough to deploy the side airbag the srs ecu will send control signal to trigger the side airbag the side airbag module connector is pin connector which is below the seat cushion and connected with the srs ecu through the body harness supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint caution do not try to remove the connector at the module end it is permanent connection safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint operation overview when the ignition switch is in on position the srs will be activated with the ignition switch in on position the srs warning lamp illuminate for several second then goe out this indicate that the system is in normal condition front impact if the front impact is severe and exceed the threshold set in thesrsecu seat belt will work when the accelerometer and front impact sensor in the srs ecu detect any impact the ecu will produce current to trigger the front airbag module the airbag the passenger side front airbag module is activated to expand the airbag when the front airbag is fully expanded it will release the gas immediately from the passage port and provide the passenger thisreducestherisk level of injury to the occupant srs ecuthe srs ecu can determine the type and severity level of the impact if the front impact detected by the sensor is severe the srs ecu will send an ignition signal to the front airbag module and seat belt pretensioner side impact normal operation of the side seat airbag and head curtain airbag depend on whether the severity level of the side impact detected by the side impact sensor and acceleration sensor in the srs ecu exceed the threshold for triggering the side impact sensor when the srs ecu detect severe side impact it will activate the side airbag curtain airbag module and seat belt pretensioner the triggered gas generator can produce large quantity of gas to expand the airbag the expanded airbag will eject from the seat upholstery similarly if the head curtain airbag module is triggered the airbag will eject from the interior trim panel above the door to protect the passenger from head injury the fully expanded airbag will release excess gas to reduce the risk of passenger injury front impact with angle when front impact with angle happen whether the airbag and seat belt pretensioner work depend on the speed and angle of the impact there are several condition the impact is below the threshold of the impact sensor in the srs ecu and no airbag or seat belt pretensioner is activated the speed and angle of impact exceed the triggering threshold of the front impact sensor and the front driver and passenger airbag and seat belt pretensioner are activated side airbag may also be triggered warning lamp check when the ignition switch is turned on the srs warning lamp shall illuminate then check the lamp if no failure occur in the srs the warning lamp shall go out after several second to monitor the subsequent ignition cycle system continuity fault with the ignition switch on if any of the following fault occur in the system the srs warning lamp will illuminate srs harness failure ground connection failure open circuit airbag module failure seat belt pretensioner failure if the system detect any failure during driving the warning lamp will also illuminate to indicate failure of the srs system when the warning lamp illuminate the srs may not work when any impact occur withtheignitionswitchon thediagnosisfunctionofthesr ecu will monitor the srs if any fault is detected the srs ecu will store related dtc in nonvolatile memory and output signal to illuminate the srs warning lamp the failure information in the memory can be read with the scan tool low voltage failure when the voltage supply is not within the specified range the malfunction indicator lamp will illuminate the dtc is stored in the memory intermittent failure when intermittent failure occur the warning lamp illuminate and it will be disabled after the failure is eliminated the warning lamp will not illuminate in the next ignition unless the failure occur again but the dtc is still stored in the srs ecu memory permanent failure for permanent failure the srs lamp will illuminate when the test is started and it will remain on in every subsequent ignition cycle until the cause of the failure is solved in addition the diagnosis system will also record the information of the failure occurred additional information provided by the scan tool for dlc include srs ecudtc version level of hardware and software vehicle identification number vin data supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint srs ecuthe srs ecu record fault information in the memory and the information can be read by connecting the scan tool to the dlc below the closure plate of the driver side instrument panel safety and restraint can bus hard wire park assistant system safety and restraint description rear obstacle detection if equipped during reversing if the vehicle has to go through obstruction on the road the pdc will send warning to the driver the system consist of the ultrasonic sensor fitted on the rear bumper and the instrument pack when the vehicle is in reverse gear the ultrasonic sensor is used to monitor the area around the rear bumper if any object is detected within the monitoring area the acoustic alarm device in the instrument pack will send an acoustic alarm the system is able to detect relatively hard solid object and object like wire fence if the rear camera is fitted the image of the rear vehicle will be displayed on the entertainment mainframe display during reversing rear ultrasonic sensor if equipped the ultrasonic sensor are fixed on the rear bumper all the sensor share the same structure with the color matching the vehicle the detection range of the rear ultrasonic sensor involve the area away from the back of the bumper the alarm frequency increase with the shortened distance between the object and the vehicle when the distance range from to when the object is away from the vehicle the alarm become consecutive alarm acoustic alarm the acoustic alarm in the instrument pack can inform the driver of the system status by acoustic alarm rear camera if equipped the rear camera is between the two rear license plate lamp under the tail gate which can provide the driver with real time image around the rear of the vehicle and help the reverse operation end view and pin information of harness connector end view and pin information of rear camera connector pin no description signal ground ground video signal camera power supply end view and pin information of right pdc sensor connector pin no description ground power supply safety and restraint park assistant system end view and pin information of middle pdc sensor connector pin no description ground power supply end view and pin information of left pdc sensor connector pin no description ground ground power supply park assistant system safety and restraint operation overview the system start to run when the ignition switch is in on position after the ultrasonic sensor receive the reverse gear signal it output signal to the acoustic warning device then the acoustic warning device will give an acoustic alarm beep this indicate that the system is activated then it receive the distance information from the ultrasonic sensor and determine whether there is any object in the detection range if there is no object it will not send an acoustic alarm if any object is detected it output signal to the acoustic warning device and control the device to provide repetitive acoustic alarm from the edge of the detection zone about from the rear vehicle the alarm frequency increase with the shortened distance between the object and the vehicle when the object is away from the vehicle the alarm become consecutive alarm detection zone the table below show the relationship between the alarm level obstacle distance and the acoustic alarm sn alarm level distance mm alarm cycle msec no obstacle zone zone zone zone zone zone zone caution blind spot area where the sensor cannot detect obstruction may occur if obstruction are present at distance of around mm from the sensor safety and restraint the tachometer is used to warn the driver high engine speed can damage the engine when the ignition switch is in on position the instrument pack use the hs can bus to receive the engine speed signal view foreword foreword about this document outline this document is intended to help you diagnose electrical malfunction this document is divided into the following part foreword contain the following content about this document electrical protection abbreviation wire color code wire harness abbreviation wire harness configuration code chart power supply line function connector list malfunction diagnosis and how to use the wiring diagram locator of connector and ground point show the location of each connector on the vehicle through the three dimensional space diagram in addition many connector will be shown in an illustration for convenience fuse description contain detailed description for location rated current and the protection circuit power distribution circuit contain engine bay power distribution and passenger compartment power distribution it is intended to understand the condition of power distribution service circuit contain the repair electrical wiring diagram detailed connector list instruction and connector end view of the section for each system on the vehicle note ensure that the relative mechanical function operate normally before performing the electrical test reference reference to the lh or rh side given in this document is viewed from the rear of the vehicle the operation instruction in this document do not contain the test reference after the vehicle is repaired after all the operation finished it is necessary to perform the inspection test it is especially important while performing the operation related to the safety part caution please refer to the electrical protection before performing the operation related to the electrical equipment battery voltageopen circuit voltage test before starting the electrical malfunction diagnosis check if the battery is available with the open circuit voltage test turn off all the electrical equipment on the vehicle adjust the digital multimeter to the appropriate scale to read the dc voltage try to contact the two pole of the battery with the probe to ensure the two pole are connected correctly and record the voltage displayed when the displayed voltage is or more it mean that the battery is available or the battery need to be charged caution if the vehicle has been used within hour before testing the static on the surface of the battery must be removed by turning on the headlight for about second wait for second before checking the open circuit voltage the battery voltage can be used as known reference value to determine whether the voltage in the circuit is enough high to operate each component normally this reference value can only be used as reference because many circuit can operate in large range of voltage in addition the change of the reading caused by the voltage drop and the voltage fluctuation caused by the length of the wire should be taken into consideration when the voltage passing through certain component foreword electrical protection outline the following instruction are intended to ensure the personal safety of the operator when installing the electrical equipment and electronic component equipment ensure that the relative test equipment operate normally and the wire harness and the connector are well connected before performing vehicle test procedure pay more attention to the condition of the wire and the connector used to operate the equipment caution before starting any work on ignition system make sure the high voltage terminal adapter and diagnostic equipment for testing have been insulated or shielded absolutely to minimize the risk of electric shock in order to prevent personal accident the medicinal pacemaker carrier should be away from the ignition circuit and the diagnostic equipment polarity never connect the two pole of the battery reversely ensure that both pole are connected correctly when connecting them to the test equipment high voltage circuit please use the insulated plier to disconnect the live circuit to prohibit the bare conductor contacting other component especially various ecus pay attention to the ignition coil terminal as it may produce high voltage pulse when the engine is running connector and wire harness it is difficult to install the electronic part and the connector in the engine bay therefore care should be taken ensure that the electronic part and the connector are dry without any oil before disconnecting and connecting the test equipment never use tool or drag the wire harness to disconnect the connector forcibly before disconnecting be sure to release the connector locking tab and reconnect them in the correct direction be sure to replace any protective cover and insulating material immediately once they are damaged refer to the service guide of the repair manual before replacing the defective part be sure to turn the ignition switch off disconnect the battery refer to battery disconnection to avoid unnecessary terminal damage support any wire harness disconnected when replacing the part keep the oily hand away from the electronic connection area and ensure that the connector locking tab are engaged completely battery disconnection turn all the electrical equipment off before disconnecting the battery some data time of the clock will be lost when the battery is disconnected caution to prevent damage the electrical equipment alway disconnect the battery when repairing the electrical system on the vehicle when the battery is disconnected the ground terminal must be disconnected first and connected last be sure to connect the wire harness of the battery correctly locate it in place and keep it away from any potential hazard area all the time battery charging ensure good ventilation of the battery top when charging the battery outside the vehicle after charging or discharging the battery for approximately minute the battery will release the hydrogen the gas is combustible foreword alway keep the charging area of the battery in good ventilation and prevent the creation of flame and spark rule turn off the ignition system before connecting or disconnecting any part in the system or the electrical component may be damaged by the electromagnetic excitation produced due to disconnection of the live circuit ensure your hand and the working surface have no oil or metal particle etc for example short connection or high resistance may be caused by the oil when handling the printed circuit board pay attention to the electronic component sensitive to the body static do not remove or install the connector forcibly especially the connector in the board the damaged connection will cause short or open circuit perform the ground protection periodically before or during the test for example cigar lighter doing so can release the static of the vehicle body as the static may cause some electronic component damaged foreword abbreviation outline acc accessory ampere air conditioning there are stand for the battery power with the ignition switch in on or st ign stand for the battery power with the ignition switch in on ign stand for the battery power with the ignition switch in on or acc acc stand for connecting the battery power directly bat stand for ground gnd stand for the battery power with the headlight switch in and ill illumination circuit stand for the battery power information is attached behind the wiring diagram of each system in the form of standard table and the connector are instructed in detail connector information include the following content connector no pin color attribute connector no the number is distributed by the system with prefix as wire harness id such as bd which stand for body wire harness refer to the wire harness abbreviation list for wire harness id pin stand for the total number of the connector pin caution not all the hole have lead connected color stand for the color of the connector housing attribute stand for the attribute of the connector include male connector female connector and eye connector the connector are usually inserted into the element directly and they commonly have convex pin connector function description usually derive from the connected element connector location usually determine the location of the connector by referring to the connector locator inline connector no optional item fit for the connector location inline connector no white male connector instrument pack panel to transmission case wire harness dash panel lh foreword malfunction diagnosis outline when diagnosing the electrical malfunction observe the following step study the wiring diagram carefully to make sure that you are familiar with the wiring diagram related to the malfunction study the power distribution and the fuse and check the relative circuit connected to the fuse or ground check if the circuit are operating normally use the connector layout to confirm the connection point in the circuit perhap between the power supply and the ground point and these place are damaged easily check if the lead out line information and the terminal signal information of the connector are correct check the next connector in the circuit with the above method using the above method check until the malfunction is found and troubleshoot to make sure that the circuit operate normally caution do not contact the face of the connector with probe as it may damage the terminal and cause an incorrect inspection in general the inverse side of the terminal should be detected note do not damage the terminal or seal caution do not detect the insulation part of the wire otherwise the small diameter wire will be disconnected and cause the humidity to enter into the wire and corroded it foreword how to use the wiring diagram wire jump page it show that the lead is connected to connector no and pin no see page is shown that you can refer to page of the repairing circuit for specific loop screened wire the screened wire is shown as above in the wiring diagram all brake lamp lh see page bare foreword twisted pair wire the twisted pair wire are shown as above in the wiring diagram component the name and description of the component are shown as above if the pin of the connector connected with the component are all shown in this page the component will be indicated by solid frame otherwise it will be indicated by dashed frame lead attribute lead attribute is shown above the lead in the wiring diagram and the additional information separated by is shown beside the lead color stand for the diameter of the lead with the unit of millimetre stand for the color of the lead if the lead has main color and color stripe the main color will be placed ahead for example stand for yellow with red stripe all stand for the configuration of the vehicle and it mean that any model is applicable caution the number of the screened wire mean the attribute of the lead bare stand for the screened wire dg body control module rear defogger rear window all foreword how to use the wiring diagram connector component end connector component end connector are shown as above in the wiring diagram this diagram is shown that the pin of the female connector is connected with component vanity mirror lamp lh cup symbol mean female connector inline connector inline connector are shown as above in the wiring diagram stand for the two inline connector number cup symbol mean female connector the connector on the right of the cup symbol is female and the connector inlined with it is male the number in the cup symbol mean the pin number for recognizing lead the example diagram mean that pin of male connector is inlined with pin of female connector vanity mirror lamp lh fuse description fuse description outline there are two fuse box in the vehicle one is installed on the left of the engine bay and the other is installed on the driver pillar instrument pack panel side the capacity and name of each fuse and relay are marked on the cover of each fusebox and relay block three different type of fuse are installed in the engine bay fusebox blade type fuse thinner plug in type the available current is case fuse square plug in type the available current is bolt type fuse generally also called fusible link the available current is only blade type fuse is installed in the passenger compartment fusebox fuse user manual seat belt driver seat passenger seat left sunscreen interior lighting right sunscreen inside view mu en user manual safety safe driving safety first before driving both for your own safety and your passenger we recommend that you consider the following aspect before you start driving make sure that the light and turn signal are in perfect condition check tyre pressure page check that all window provide good visibility factor bearing on safety as driver you are responsible for your own safety and your passenger alway remain attentive to traffic and do not become distracted by your passenger or telephone call never drive when your faculty are impaired under medication drug alcohol follow traffic rule and speed limit alway adapt your speed to the characteristic of the road as well as weather condition and traffic circumstance on long trip stop regularly to rest at least every two hour whenever possible avoid driving when tired or tense make sure that luggage is properly secured page make sure that no object prevent pedal operation page adjust mirror page adjust the front seat page protect child by using an appropriate child seat and correctly adjusting the seat page sit correctly also advise your passenger to sit correctly adjust your seat belt correctly page tell your passenger to properly buckle their seat belt page warning this chapter contain information of interest on handling the vehicle both for the driver and the passenger in other chapter on on board documentation there is other important information of which the driver and passenger must also be aware of their safety safety mu en user manual correct vehicle occupant position correct seat position safety equipment do not put your or your passenger safety at risk safety equipment can reduce the risk of injury in the event of an accident the following section include part of your safety equipment three point seat belt the aforementioned safety equipment purpose is to protect you and your passenger the best way possible in the event of an accident these safety system will be of no use to you or your passenger if you sit in an incorrect position or use these system inappropriately safety affect all of us warning driving under the effect of alcohol drug medication and narcotic can cause serious accident that can even be lethal alcohol drug medication and narcotic can considerably alter your perception reaction time and safety while driving which could lead to loss of control over the vehicle fig the correct distance between the driver and the wheel must be at least cm inch fig correct seat belt adjustment safety mu en user manual as follow we show correct seat position for you and your passenger people who cannot reach correct seat position due to their physical constitution must ask about possible special device at specialised shop optimum protection is only granted by the seat belt in correct position silence recommend that you see technical service for your own safety and to avoid injury in the event of sharp movement or braking or of accident silence recommend the following position valid for all vehicle occupant keep the back of your neck as close as possible to the headrest fig alway keep your foot in the footrest area while driving properly adjust and buckle your seat belt fig the driver must also have their back completely resting against the seat adjust the seat so that the wheel is at least cm inch from the sternum fig and you can hold it with both hand on both side from the outside with your arm bent adjust the seat so that you can press the pedal down all the way with your knee bent with distance between the knee zone and the instrument dashboard of at least cm inch fig alway keep your foot in the footrest area to keep control over the vehicle at all time number of seat the vehicle has seat in the front each seat is equipped with seat belt warning sitting in an incorrect position in the vehicle can increase the risk of serious or lethal injury in the event of abrupt movement or braking collision or accident before driving all occupant must alway be seated in correct position and remain in this position during the entire trip the same is true for seat belt placement only transport as many people as seat with seat belt that the vehicle has while driving alway keep your foot in the footrest area never place them for example on top of the seat or instrument dashboard and never put them out the window otherwise not only will the seat belt be unable to offer you any protection but it may also increase the risk of injury in the event of an accident safety mu en user manual risk of sitting in incorrect position if you are not wearing your seat belt or are wearing it improperly this increase the risk of serious or lethal injury seat belt only guarantee optimum protection when worn properly sitting in an incorrect position considerably reduce the protective function of the seat belt consequently you can suffer serious or even lethal injury the risk of serious or lethal injury especially increase if the occupant of the vehicle knock into each other because they are not seated correctly as driver you are responsible for every individual especially child that you transport in your vehicle as follow is an illustrative list of incorrect position that could prove dangerous to vehicle occupant when the vehicle is in movement never stand inside the vehicle never stand on the seat never kneel on the seat never lean on the instrument dashboard never sit just on the front of the seat never sit to the side never lean outside the window never put your foot outside the window never put your foot on the instrument dashboard never put your foot on the seat padding or on the back of the seat never travel in the footrest area never sit on top of the armrest never travel without being seated in the seat with your seat belt on never stay in the boot warning sitting in an incorrect position in the vehicle can increase the risk of serious or lethal injury in the event of accident abrupt movement or braking all occupant must alway be correctly seated while driving and be wearing their seat belt correctly vehicle occupant who are not seated correctly or are not wearing their seat belt run the risk of very serious or lethal injury safety mu en user manual warning if you cannot activate the pedal freely critical situation may arise while driving never place rug or any other carpeting over the rug already installed since this reduce the pedal area and can prevent you from using them with the consequent risk of accident never place object in the driver footrest area they could move into the pedal area and prevent the driver from activating them pedal make sure that you can alway press the brake and accelerator pedal without any problem make sure that the pedal can return to their original position without any problem make sure that the rug are entirely attached so that they do not move while driving or impede pedal operation you may only use rug that leave the pedal area unobstructed and that can be attached to keep them from moving you can purchase suitable rug at specialised establishment element to attach rug have been placed in the footrest area when one of the brake circuit fail you have to press the brake pedal farther down to stop the vehicle wear suitable footwear wear shoe that properly adjust to your foot and do not impede sensitivity when activating the pedal the control light activate to remind you to buckle your seat belt before driving the vehicle adjust your seat belt correctly tell your passenger to correctly buckle their seat belt before beginning to drive if you exceed certain speed when you begin driving without buckling your seat belt regardless of the passenger or if the passenger unbuckle while driving you will hear an acoustic signal for few second the warning light will also blink the light will turn off when with the on button activated you buckle your seatbelt seat belt why wear seat belt control light the driver has not buckled their seat belt light up in red fig pedal location seat belt mu en user manual the protective role of seat belt properly worn seat belt keep occupant in the correct position they also help to avoid uncontrolled movement that can cause serious injury and reduce the danger of flying out of the vehicle in the event of accident vehicle occupant who properly wear their seat belt will largely benefit from the fact that kinetic energy is absorbed by the seat belt the structure of the front part also guarantee absorption of the released kinetic energy this decrease the kinetic energy being released and at the same time the risk of injury this is why you must put your seat belt on before you begin driving the vehicle even for just short trip also ensure that your passenger has buckled their belt statistic on traffic accident have shown that correctly wearing your seat belt considerably reduce the risk of serious injury and increase the likelihood of surviving in the event of an accident fig driver who wear their seat belt properly will not be thrown from the vehicle in the event of abrupt braking important safety instruction to use seat belt alway wear your seat belt as described in this section ensure that it is possible to wear your seat belt at all time and that the seat belt are not damaged warning if you are not wearing your seat belt or are wearing it improperly this increase the risk of serious or lethal injury seat belt provide maximum protection only if used correctly one same seat belt should never be used by two people at the same time including child never remove your seat belt while the vehicle is in movement otherwise there is risk of death the seat belt should not slide over hard or fragile object glass pen etc since this could produce injury in the event of an accident the seat belt must never be caught damaged or rub against sharp edge never wear your seat belt under your arm or in any other incorrect position seat belt mu en user manual frontal accident and law of physic it is easy to explain how the law of physic work in the event of frontal accident as soon as the vehicle begin to move this create both in the vehicle and in its occupant energy called kinetic energy the magnitude of the kinetic energy mainly depend on the speed weight of the vehicle and weight of the occupant the greater the weight the greater the energy that must be absorbed in the event of an accident however the most important factor is the vehicle speed for example if speed double from km mph to km mph the kinetic energy quadruple given that the vehicle occupant in our example do not have their seat belt buckled in the event of collision all of the occupant kinetic energy will only be absorbed by this impact even driving at speed between km mph and km mph the force on the body in an accident can easily exceed one tonne kg the force on your body thick unbuttoned garment coat over sweater make it difficult to correctly adjust seat belt so they reduce their protective capacity avoid obstructing the fastener with paper or similar object since the buckle cannot click into place never modify the position of the seat belt with pin for the belt fastener ring or similar object seat belt with damage to the fabric joint automatic roller or fastener can cause serious injury in the event of accident for this reason regularly check the condition of the seat belt after an accident go to specialised workshop to replace seat belt that have been excessively loosened due to the strain they underwent it may be necessary to replace them even if there is no visible damage moreover you must check the belt point of attachment never try to repair seat belt on your own seat belt must not be disassembled or modified in any way the belt must be kept clean since if it is very dirty its automatic roller operation may be jeopardised fig driver not wearing their seat belt will be thrown forward seat belt mu en user manual increase along with the speed at which you drive occupant of the vehicle who have not buckled their seat belt are not united with the vehicle in the event of frontal collision these individual will move at the same speed as the vehicle before the collision this example is valid not only for frontal collision but also for all kind of accident even when driving at low speed in the event of collision there are force on the body that cannot be counteracted just by leaning against one hand in the event of frontal collision vehicle occupant who are not wearing their seat belt will be launched uncontrollably forward and will crash for example against the wheel the instrument dashboard or the proper seat belt adjustment buckle and unbuckle your seat belt fig insert the seat belt buckle in the fastener fig release the buckle from the belt fastener when properly placed seat belt keep occupant in the most protected position in the event of abrupt braking or accident buckle your seat belt before each trip fig properly adjust the front seat page pull the belt buckle and place the belt uniformly over your chest and pelvic region do not fold the belt when doing so buckle the belt into the fastener on its seat pull the belt to ensure that the buckle is properly inserted into the fastener unbuckle the seat belt fig only unbuckle the seat belt when the vehicle is stopped press the red button on the fastener the buckle will be released from the fastener accompany the belt with your hand so that the belt roll more easily it seat belt mu en user manual proper belt placement fig correctly adjusted seat belt seen from the front and side fig seat belt placement for pregnant woman doe not fold and the covering is not damaged and reduce the risk of serious or lethal injury when properly worn for this reason the seat belt must alway be buckled and you must ensure that the belt is in proper position an improper position in the seat can lead to serious or even lethal injury page the shoulder belt must alway go over the middle of the shoulder never over the neck arm or under the arm or behind the back the lap seat belt must alway go over the pelvic region and never over the abdomen alway keep the belt flat and tight against the body if necessary pull on the seat belt bit for pregnant woman the seat belt must be uniformly placed against the chest and as low as possible on the pelvic region and kept flat so it doe not press against the stomach it must also be used during the entire pregnancy fig warning the seat belt can only guarantee optimum protection when properly worn according to the occupant height removing the seat belt while driving can cause serious or lethal injury in the event of accident or abrupt braking the seat belt or loose seat belt can cause serious injury if the belt move from rigid area of the body to soft area to the abdomen seat belt only offer optimum protection in the event of an accident seat belt mu en user manual adapt the belt placement to the occupant height warning if the seat belt is improperly placed this can lead to serious or lethal injury in the event of an accident the shoulder belt should go over the middle of the shoulder and never under the arm or over the neck the seat belt must be flat and tight against the upper part of the body the lap seat belt must go over the pelvic region and never over the abdomen the seat belt must be flat and tight against the pelvis if necessary pull on the seat belt bit for pregnant woman the lap belt must go as low as possible over the pelvic region flat and surrounding the stomach fig do not fold the belt while wearing it once the belt has been properly placed do not pull it away from your body observation if your vehicle has new tyre drive with extreme prudence for the first km if you must drive on kerb or similar do so slowly and if possible with your wheel perpendicular to the obstacle occasionally check whether the tyre are damaged hole cut crack or dent remove all foreign object from the tyre profile damaged wheel or tyre must be immediately replaced keep the tyre from being soiled by oil grease or fuel if you have lost one of the valve cap replace it as soon as possible mark wheel before dismounting them to keep the same rotation direction when you reassemble them wheel mu en user manual keep dismounted wheel or tyre in cool dry and if possible dark location invisible damage damage to tyre and wheel is often not visible to the naked eye if the vehicle is vibrating unusually or tend to veer to the side this may be sign of tyre deterioration reduce speed as soon as possible if you suspect that has wheel has been perforated check whether the tyre are damaged if the outside of the tyre bear no sign of damage keep driving slowly and prudently to the nearest specialised shop so they can check your vehicle foreign object inserted in the tyre do not remove foreign object if they have penetrated the inside of the tyre use the tyre repair kit page to seal the damaged wheel go to specialised shop for repair or replacement silence recommend going to silence dealer to do this the tyre tread patching compound envelop the inserted foreign object and provisionally seal the tyre unidirectional tread tyre the side of unidirectional tyre are marked with arrow it is essential that you follow this mandatory rotation direction when mounting the wheel this guarantee best use of the tyre property in term of aquaplaning adherence noise and wear later accessory assembly if you wish to replace or refit the wheel tyre or hub cap we recommend that you ask an official silence service to consult on existing technical possibility new tyre do not reach optimum adherence until after the first km as such we recommend that you drive with caution to avoid possible accident never drive with damaged tyre there is risk of having an accident if the vehicle vibrate unusually or tend to veer to the side while driving stop immediately and check the condition of the tyre do not use used tyre when you do not know the circumstance under which they were used before warning new wheel and tyre we recommend that you bring your vehicle to specialised shop to conduct all work on wheel or tyre they have the necessary knowledge and special tool and appropriate spare part even snow tyre lose adherence with ice if you have put on new tyre drive carefully and moderate your speed during the first km you must alway use the same type wheel mu en user manual only use combination of tyre and wheel as well as wheel bolt which are suitable and approved by silence otherwise the vehicle may be damaged and this can cause an accident for technical reason it is not possible to use wheel from other vehicle in some case not even wheel from the same vehicle make do not exclusively select spare tyre based on nominal size because although they have the same nominal size they can greatly vary depending on the manufacturer lack of clearance can deteriorate the tyre or the vehicle and this can jeopardise road safety risk of accident only use tyre older than year in the event of an emergency and only when driving prudently it is forbidden to mount run flat tyre on your vehicle this forbidden use may cause damage to your vehicle or cause accident warning of tyre of the same size wheel perimeter and if possible tread for all wheel when you replace tyre do not just replace one change at least the two tyre on the same axle if you wish to equip your vehicle with combination of tyre and wheel other than the one assembled at the factor ask at your specialised shop before purchasing them the dimension of combination of wheel and tyre to use on your vehicle are specified in the vehicle additional documentation eec certificate of conformity the additional documentation in the vehicle is specific to each country date of manufacture the date of manufacture is on the side of the tyre or on the inner side of the wheel dot mean that the tyre was manufactured in week of the year information used tyre must be disposed of according to applicable regulation wheel mu en user manual fig pressure label you must ask at silence assistance centre about the possibility of mounting wheel or tyre of size other than the one originally mounted at silence do not use used tyre whose history you do not know warning tyre life cycle to lengthen the life cycle of the tyre they must alway be inflated to the proper pressure and you must drive with moderation check your tyre pressure at least once per month and also before each long trip you must alway check pressure when the tyre are cold do not reduce tyre pressure if they are hot adapt tyre pressure to the load the vehicle is carrying avoid driving too fast on curve and abrupt acceleration occasionally check the tyre for irregular wear tyre pressure inflation pressure is indicated on the adhesive label located on the back of the front left hand door frame fig insufficient or excessive pressure greatly reduce the life cycle of tyre and has negative influence on how the vehicle behave while driving tyre pressure is extremely important especially when driving at high speed tyre pressure must be adapted to the vehicle current load if the vehicle is going to be completely loaded you must increase the inflation pressure to the top load number indicated on the adhesive label driving style fast driving on curve and abrupt acceleration and braking squeaking brake increase wheel wear wheel balance the wheel on new vehicle are balanced however different circumstance during use create imbalance eccentricity which manifest as vibration through the wheel since imbalance also mean greater wear on steering suspension and tyre you must balance the wheel again wheel must also be balanced again after installing new tyre and after each repair wheel mu en user manual failure in wheel alignment if the undercarriage is improperly adjusted this not only increase wear on tyre but also reduce safety while driving if you observe excessive tyre wear an official silence service shop must check wheel alignment inappropriate handling of tyre and wheel can lead to sudden pressure loss in tyre loss of tread or even exploding tyre the driver is responsible for inflating all the vehicle tyre to the correct pressure the recommended inflation pressure is indicated on sticker fig regularly check tyre pressure and alway keep it at the recommended inflation pressure if the tyre pressure is too low the tyre may overheat causing the tread to come off and even explosion when the tyre are cold they should alway match the pressure on the sticker fig regularly check tyre pressure when tyre are cold if necessary adjust mounted tyre pressure when the tyre are cold regularly check that the tyre show no sign of wear or damage never exceed the speed and maximum load permitted for your vehicle tyre type warning information if tyre pressure is insufficient it increase energy consumption wear indicator at regular interval and crosswise from the direction the vehicle drive there are wear indicator at the bottom of the original tyre tread that are mm high fig the minimum profile depth permitted is reached when the tyre are worn down to the wear indicator replace the tyre with new one fig wear indicator tyre must be replaced at the latest when the wear indicator are worn down otherwise you run the risk of an accident especially when driving under adverse weather condition such as rain and ice it is important that the tread on the tyre be as deep as possible and that it be approximately the same on the tyre on the rear and front axle poor driving safety due to insufficient tread depth is clear especially when driving with the risk of aquaplaning when driving through deep puddle when driving on curve and in braking behaviour if you do not adapt your speed you can lose control of the vehicle warning snow chain after driving for few metre check that they are properly in place correct their position if necessary by following manufacturer installation instruction remember the maximum speed of km mph wheel mu en user manual using unsuitable chain or placing them incorrectly can cause accident and considerable damage alway use suitable snow chain follow manufacturer installation instruction for chain if driving with snow chain never exceed the maximum permitted speed warning if there is snow on the road snow chain will not only improve traction but also braking behaviour if you use snow chain you must dismount all hubcap and decorative ring for tyre information dismount chain on trip without snow otherwise they will worsen vehicle behaviour damage the tyre and quickly deteriorate if the chain are in direct contact with the tyre they can damage or scratch it silence recommend alway using covered snow chain wheel mu en user manual emergency troubleshooting emergency consumed power use mu en user manual if you drive with too low of charge in either of the traction battery the vehicle could stop in the middle of traffic and could also cause accident and serious injury make sure that both traction battery are alway sufficiently charged if either of the traction battery is not sufficiently charged gear property may change the vehicle speed or acceleration behaviour if the charge is too low power supply may even abruptly be reduced and the vehicle speed may be limited to km alway adapt your speed and driving style to visibility condition the weather the road and traffic as well as the state of charge of the traction battery warning warning if you leave the vehicle parked for long period of time with the traction battery uncharged this could cause irreversible damage to them see good battery charging practise page information driving mode fig driving mode indicator with the driving mode you can adapt different characteristic of the vehicle property vary because the centre of gravity has moved alway distribute the load in the vehicle in uniform fashion and as horizontally as possible unsecured object in the boot can suddenly move and change the vehicle driving behaviour alway adapt your speed and driving style to visibility condition the weather the road and traffic instrument dashboard remove hard sharp or heavy object from
