make has unit with an innovative removable battery pack with strap and wheel to be transported like trolley called be this mean that you can charge both in the vehicle itself and wherever else you want by bringing it to an electrical outlet this battery pack can also be exchanged between vehicle and even be used to give life to many other appliance safety instruction the battery can be very dangerous product risk of death when outside the vehicle it must alway be transported on its wheel at slow speed max km slower than the average walking speed of human you must avoid all mistreatment of the battery such as blow jump cobblestone stair hole and fall for example down stair as this can lead it to catch fire you must also avoid contact with the water if you see any sign or suspect that the battery pack was used improperly or fell you must not plug it in call silence official technical service because there is risk of death if the battery catch fire you can put it out with water extinguisher or class extinguisher unless the battery is plugged into the electrical grid or near other battery if this happen you must use extinguisher or class extinguisher to mitigate the fire and take the battery to secure isolated area at before inserting or removing the battery pack make sure that the vehicle is not charging or turned on the charger is built into the battery pack itself so all you need to charge is an electrical outlet connected to the grid and the power cable it is cooled by convection to connect you must first plug in the iec connector vehicle or the battery itself and then into the grid you must charge completely at least after partially charging or time in the same way you must also do complete slow charge after or quick charge to disconnect you must first unplug from the electrical grid and then the iec connector you may interrupt the charging process at any time additionally least of any exposure other vehicle other battery etc once in secure zone call emergency service to take charge of the situation warning fig removable battery caution use mu en user manual the control system automatically stop charging when it reach if you connect at low temperature the internal heater will turn on until it reach suitable temperature for charging with battery unit that have this element activated this heater operate while connected to the electrical grid and make it so that current come in under suitable temperature in this case the charging time will be affected and will take longer than under normal temperature condition we recommend keeping the battery connected to the electrical grid in cold place and during cold season of the year so that the heater heat the cell and they are at suitable temperature for the vehicle to be used normally warning to keep your battery warranty you must completely charge it up to at least once per month if you foresee that you will not be using it for long period of time it is very important that you charge it enough that it doe not go down to critical level battery that exceed certain low voltage limit lose autonomy to operate which mean it cannot be charged and need to be brought to official technical service information temperature this vehicle has system to control and stabilise voltage and cell temperature to avoid critical situation safety system limit battery use if the cell temperature exceed safety limit in the event of overheating or supercooling the battery operational range is between and depending on the temperature the lithium cell performance may vary the charger doe not charge the battery if the cell temperature is lower than or higher than the battery current temperature can be seen on the vehicle display if the limit are exceeded upper or lower alert or error the following led will warn you if necessary the action you must take are low temperature the battery doe not operate under optimum condition store it in warmer location and charge it if the internal heater is activated high temperature due to intensive use the battery cannot provide any more power avoid sport mode temperature indicator it blink when component is nearing its admissible limit upper or lower the light remain steady when this limit is exceeded motor int solid light controller int solid light battery int solid light int solid light light up red use mu en user manual autonomy the definition of an electric vehicle autonomy is the distance it can travel with one single complete battery charge this is influenced by many factor such as driving style vehicle load kg and proper distribution tyre pressure and unfavourable weather condition such as wind which can reduce it the display provide an estimate of remaining kilometre or mile of autonomy this information is approximate and depend on the driving mode city eco sport and the current amount of power used battery charge indicator in soc in order to read the battery state of charge soc in with utmost precision you must finish the charging process up to at least after or partial charge good battery charging practice you must follow these recommendation when charging the battery when charging at home place the battery in isolated ventilated area like garage laundry room storage room or patio do not charge the battery in the rain when it is outside the vehicle do not charge in area with traffic or entrance to the home keep the battery far from hot element or that can cause heat such as radiator or stove keep the battery far from mobile element like vehicle or door avoiding all possible blow to the battery keep the battery at prudent distance from all other element like table cabinet chair and shelve periodically check on the battery while charging good practice for proper battery maintenance the battery life cycle can last cycle with of its capacity if you conduct maintenance as prescribed by the warranty and follow sery of good practise avoid excessive use of sport mode and attempt to use city mode as much as possible reason avoiding aggressive driving with constant abrupt acceleration avoid completely draining the battery charge the battery when it near of soc thereby avoiding complete cycle and limiting discharge depth keep the battery temperature between and limit time outside this range to the least amount possible when you exceed these temperature the battery deteriorate more easily and lose its property both with high and low temperature whenever possible use the internal charger to charge avoiding constant and stop the vehicle if necessary so it can cool down use mu en user manual battery safety alert never open the battery case authorised personnel this can be very dangerous and completely invalidate the warranty only authorised personnel can work on the battery case if smoke begin coming from the battery it is mandatory to place an emergency call firefighter risk of death and if it is in an enclosed location remove it the vehicle cannot explode so there is no issue transporting it to safe location to avoid injury burn and electrical shock never disassemble the battery unit or remove its cover only authorised personnel can do this keep child away from this part of the vehicle do not perforate or hit this area when using hoist or expose it to liquid or incinerate it or expose it to flame since excess heat can lead to fire and this can be very dangerous make sure you read the shop manual before repairing or replacing the battery use of quick charge do complete slow charge after or quick charge use mu en user manual first aid measure if the battery break smoke or catch fire evacuate personnel from the contaminated area and provide maximum ventilation to clean out the gase in any event seek medical attention contact with eye rinse with plenty of water eyelid open for at least minute contact with skin remove all contaminated clothing and rinse the affected area with plenty of soap and water for at least minute do not apply grease or pomade inhalation bring to open air and ventilate the contaminated area provide oxygen or cpr if necessary fire protection measure extinguishing method you may use class dry chemical extinguisher water in the event that the vehicle battery is not connected to the electrical grid or near other battery specific danger cell can reheat due to an external source or inappropriate use recycling the battery pack once your battery pack life cycle has reached an end you must dispose of it according to applicable regulation and in an environmentally friendly way the law prohibit disposing of the battery pack in regular household waste container you must bring it to an authorised silence service shop for proper recycling thereby reducing the environmental impact use mu en user manual identification of ring of light to know the battery state of charge there is led ring of light on the side that provide this information regardless of whether or not the battery is inside or outside the vehicle if the vehicle is turned on the light doe not light up under any circumstance moving red trail and another blue one going all the way around each in different direction it then doe the same thing with turquoise fixed portion in green or the entire ring if at moving green trail in the entire ring with fixed portion of it showing the total charged percentage the entire ring is red complete green ring with blue trail going around while still plugged in moving reddish trail and another white one fixed portion in red or the entire ring if at moving red trail in the entire ring with fixed portion of it showing the total charged percentage the entire ring is yellow complete red ring with white trail going around while still plugged in bps with green colour code bp not charging bp charging when you tap the middle of the ring it show next the remaining charge percentage is momentarily shown with while the battery is charging the following alternate if the battery any event the vehicle and battery have male iec jack to connect the power cable the cable as female iec terminal and another male schuko terminal normally used in europe to connect to the residential grid the charging cable is in the bag with the emergency kit in the boot space page the charger is built into the battery pack itself so all you need to charge is an electrical outlet connected to the grid and the power cable it is cooled by convection to connect you must first plug in the iec connector vehicle or the battery itself and then into the grid you must charge completely at least after partially charging or time in the same fashion you must fig charger cable terminal fig battery plugged into the residential grid also do complete slow charge after or quick charge to disconnect you must first unplug from the electrical grid and then the iec connector you may interrupt the charging process at any time additio nally the control system automatically stop charging when it reach if the battery is at less than or more than due to inappropriate use it will not charge if you connect at low temperature the internal heater will turn on until it reach suitable temperature for charging with battery unit that have this element activated this heater operate while connected to the elec trical grid and make it so that current come in under suitable temperature in this case the charging time will be affected and will take longer than un der normal temperature condition we recommend keeping the battery connected to the electrical grid in before inserting or removing the battery pack current output schuko connector plus the direct current usb output fig silence nomad fig silence bss battery management system bms this battery has monitoring system called bms battery management system that control parameter like temperature and voltage it was developed by silence team and play very important role it balance the charge in the different cell providing top battery performance this system is also responsible for communicating the state of charge and setting charge and discharge current level it also has an action plan in the event of anomalous battery status tripping protection in preventive fashion in the event of excess or defective current voltage temperature etc in short the bms is the battery brain which work to optimise battery performance and safety use mu washing the vehicle the longer you take to clean residue insect remain bird excrement tree sap or de icing salt stuck to your vehicle the greater the damage they can do to the surface high temperature for example due to sunlight reinforce this harmful effect before washing soften thick dirtiness with plenty of water to eliminated encrusted dirtiness like excrement insect or tree sap it is best to use plenty of water and microfiber cloth also wash underneath the vehicle after the de icing salt period in winter incorrectly applying maintenance product can be harmful to your health alway keep maintenance product in safe place and out of reach of child risk of intoxication warning high pressure cleaning device if you wash the vehicle with high pressure cleaning device you must closely follow the device instruction the aforementioned is especially valid for pressure and distance from the vehicle when spraying with water do not point the spray straight at joint inside window door or cover the same hold true for tyre flexible rubber tube soundproofing material sensor etc keep minimum distance of cm do not remove snow and ice with high pressure cleaning device never use circular sprayer or mill to remove dirtiness water should be no hotter than automatic carwash rinse the vehicle before you begin washing ensure that the window are closed and the wiper are deactivated follow the carwash operator instruction especially if there are removable part on your vehicle maintenance mu en user manual vehicle give preference to brush free carwash washing by hand clean the vehicle from the top down with gentle sponge or brush for washing use cleaning product that are solvent free wash vehicle with matte paint by hand to keep from damaging the vehicle while washing it first remove the bulkiest dust and dirtiness to remove insect remain grease stain and fingerprint it is best to use special cleaner for matte paint apply the product with microfiber cloth avoid using excessive pressure to keep from damaging the paint surface rinse with plenty of water next clean with neutral cleaner and gentle microfiber cloth rinse the vehicle with plenty of water again then allow to air dry remove any remaining water with leather cloth only wash the vehicle when turned off or according to carwash operator instruction risk of accident if you clean underneath the car or inside the wheel well protect yourself from sharp or cutting metal part risk of cutting yourself after cleaning the brake may take longer to brake due to moisture or in winter to ice on the disc or brake pad risk of accident in this case you must brake several time until the brake dry warning if you wash the vehicle in an automatic carwash you must retract the outside mirror to keep them from being harmed do not wash the vehicle if exposed to intense solar radiation risk of damaging the paint do not use sponge to clean insect remain or kitchen sponge with rough surface or anything similar risk of damaging the surface vehicle part with matte paint do not use polish or hard wax risk of damaging the surface never select wash programme that include waxing this could ruin the matte paint appearance do not put sticker or magnet on matte painted part since they can damage the paint when removed information vehicle maintenance mu en user manual only wash the vehicle in location intended for this purpose these location are designed to prevent wash water which may be contaminated from going to the drain system dirtiness dirtiness dirtiness dirtiness de icing salt flaw in the paint connector guide fection mark harness side control unit bare male terminal oy direction mark wy ts connection symbol general information general precaution follow the below precaution to ensure safe proper servicing for your vehicle srs supplemental restrain system air bag information necessary to service the system safely and correctly is included throughout this service manual make sure you read each section carefully before carrying out maintenance operation improper maintenance including incorrect removal and installation of the srs air bag can lead to personal injury caused by unintentional activation of the system do not use electrical test equipment on any circuit related to the srs air bag all srs electrical wiring harness and connector are covered with yellow outer insulation general precaution do not operate the engine for an extended period of time without proper exhaust ventilation keep the work area well ventilated and free of any flammable material care must be taken when handling any flammable or poisonous material before servicing the vehicle cover fender upholstery and carpeting with appropriate cover before jacking up the vehicle apply wheel stopper and only jack up the vehicle at jack up point after jacking up the vehicle support the vehicle weight with safety stand before working on the vehicle when removing heavy object such as the engine or transaxie transmission take care not to drop them also do not allow them to strike other part especially the brake tube and master cylinder gi before start repair that do not require battery power alway turn off the engine and disconnect the earth cable from the battery to prevent accidental short circuit loosen the screw nut completely when removing the earth cable from the baitery to prevent serious burn avoid contact with hot metal part such as the radiator exhaust manifold tail pipe and muffler carry out the operation when the part has cooled down do not remove the radiator cap when the coolant is hot an inspection may be difficult with normal test probe when connector pin is extremely small when this is the case wind small pin or wire around the test probe or sharpen the end of the probe to perform the inspection use measurement equipment such as the consult electronic system diagnosis tester and an oscilloscope to perform diagnosis operation check the vehicle damage carefully make careful diagnosis of the damage and perform the correct operation check the correct part assembly condition before removal or disassembly make alignment mark when necessary in location which will not interfere with the part operation when replacing part alway use correct specified part or tool replace oil seal gasket packing ring locking washer cotter pin self locking nut etc with new one these part are indicated can not be reused and must be replaced with new part alway replace taper roller bearing and the needle bearing for inner and outer race as set take care not to mix up the removed part when replacing part alway use genuine nissan replacement part use correct lubricant specified dispose waste oil and cleaning oil in way that is set by the law gi general information wind wire around probe test probe ios sharpen the end general information precaution for ecc engine before connecting or disconnecting ecc control module harness connector be sure to turn the ignition switch to the off position and disconnect the negative battery terminal release fuel pressure to eliminate danger before disconnecting pressurized fuel line frorn fuel pump to injector do not apply any shock to the electric part such as ecc control unit or airflow metre use measurement equipment to perform diagnosis operation precaution for catalyst converter if large amount of unburnt fuel flow into the converter the temperature within the converter will become excessively high to prevent this follow the procedure below only carry out the ignition spark or measuring engine compression check when necessary and carry out the test quickly do not run the engine when the fuel tank level is low otherwise the engine may misfire causing damage to the converter only use specified gasoline do not place burnable object below the vehicle keep flammable material off the exhaust pipe and the catalyst converter precaution for fuel only use unleaded gasoline for gasoline engine vehicle using fuel other than that specified could damage the vehicle gi general information vehicle unit identification plate location vehicle identification number supplement model only oe oo wise jodel number plate engine when using primary electrical detection timing light connect sensor to no cylinder primary ray when using or no cylinder primary ray is longer than other cylinder caution when using eg built in battery type face the sensor direction of the arrow to spark plug when connect ing the sensor to the primary ray inspection make sure the idle speed is at standard value check that ignition timing is at standard value when idling make sure the ignition timing advance to correct angle when engine is raced caution timing mark on the crank pulley is indicated in orange and other point are painted in white adjustment ignition timing can be adjusted by adjusting the distributor installation position make sure the idle speed is at standard value remove throttie sensor connector loosen the distributor installation belt and rotate the distributor turn the distributor clockwise to advance ignition timing connect the throttle sensor connector after checking the idle rev and making sure it is at set value seal the distributor installation bolt with sealing tape sealing tape part no the ignition timing adjustment is basically not necessary as the ignition timing will not change over time if adjust ment become necessary carry out the adjustment when installing the crank angle sensor adjust the ignition timing by adjusting the crank angle sensor installation position make sure the idle speed is at standard value remove throttle sensor connector en bolt sealing tape position to advance ignition timing sealing tape position to advance ignition timing cam shaft rotation direction maintenance engine loosen crank angle sensor installation bolt and rotate the crank angle sensor turn the sensor anti clockwise to advance ignition timing connect the throttle sensor connector after checking the idle rev and making sure it is at set value tighten the bolt and seal one of the crank angle sensor installation bolt with sealing tape sealing tape partno idle mixture ratio the air fuel ratio feedback system has self learning function co and hc density adjustment is not necessary as correction range is extensive inspection warm up the engine sufficiently and make sure the idle speed and the ignition timing are at standard value check co and hc density with co and hc meter if the value do not conform to standard value perform following procedure to inspect air fuel feedback condition in the data monitor mode select sensor monitor increase the engine rev to above and make sure rich and lean are displayed alternatively short circuit the self diagnosis connector on the vehicle side within fuse block or remove the ecc control unit and operate the adjustment volume on the controi unit side place the ignition switch to the on position and use lead line toe short circuit the chk and ign pin of the diagnosis connector for more than two second to re lease the setting or place the ignition switch to on position and turn the adjustment volume on ecc control unit side clockwise self diagnosis position for more than two second and then return to normal position after warming up the engine sufficiently raise the engine speed until the engine warning lamp or ecc control unit side red lamp flash the flashing will usually start above approx caution the accelerator pedai must be pressed during idling to perform the inspection as the air fuel ratio feed back control is clamped en monitoringy no fault cas rpm pos air flow mtr eng coolant temp sen sensor sensor monitor rich vehicle speed sen okm battery vol throttle sen record ort circuit these pin for lia bwon note when removing the spark plug make sure to blow around the spark plug with compressed air warm up the engine sufficiently release the fuel pressure place the ignition switch to off position remove all six spark plug using the spark plug wrench install engine rev counter or consult remove injection harness connector install adapter to allen compression gauge and then set to engine depress the accelerator pedai fully place the ignition switch to start position and crank the engine read compression pressure and engine speed once the gauge needle stop moving carry out the above operation for each cylinder when the engine speed is out of specification check the specific gravity of the battery and perform test again if the compression pressure is still not in standard value inspect the component around the combustion chamber valve valve seat hydraulic valve lifter piston piston ring cylinder bore cylinder head cylinder head gasket etc correct any malfunction part and repeat compression test en engine auxiliary belt tension maintenance ee tor inspection en pone owao adjustm the inspection should be performed when the engine is cold or more than thirty minute engine diagnostic system general the performance of the diagnostic system has improved greatly with warning indication to driver operation of the self diagnostic system and application of the newly developed electrical system diagnostic tester consult enhanced fault warning item in case when fault occur in the ecc system while driving the exhaust temperature warning lamp will flash in the instrument panel to warn driver at the same time the back up function will activate to ensure the safety of occupant and the vehicle simplified trouble shooting strategy the following feature have been incorporated for simplifying inspection and problem diagnosis of the ecc system improvement of self diagnostic function the diagnostic mode have been rearranged and the mode shift and display system have been simplified ia fault warning oz sensor monitor by diagnostic connector indicated by exhaust gas self diagnosis installed near the fuse box temperature switch on of diagnosis and real connector installed on the vehicle this tester can permit data display recording and printing function test ok or ng can be determined for each system as basic inspection self diagnosis self diagnosis purpose only alway use the wiring diagram supplied with the product tech sheet when servicing the dishwasher for specific operating and installation information on the model being serviced refer to the use and care guide or installation instruction provided with the dishwasher goal and objective the goal of this service manual is to provide information that will enable the in home service professional to properly diagnose malfunction and repair the maytag chopper dishwasher the objective of this service manual are to understand and follow proper safety precaution successfully important safety message in this manual and on your appliance alway read and obey all safety message this is the safety alert symbol this symbol alert you to potential hazard that can kill or hurt you what can happen if the instruction are not followed mi maytag chopper dishwasher general theory of operation maytag chopper wash system maintenance free chopper system blade stainless steel chopper pulveriz food preventing particle from plugging the wash system and spray jet providing optimal cleaning without the need to prerinse no more scraping plate the blade stainless steel chopper disintegrate the food particle that come off your dish so they don end up recirculating and sticking to your dish and glassware maytag dishwasher feature the most powerful motor on the market so you can be sure what you put in come out clean how chopper work see figure water and food particle flow through the coarse filter then are disintegrated by the chopper blade an impeller pull soiled water into the wash pump through the screen filtering soil particle too large to pass through and sending them back to the chopper blade the wash water is then cycled back through the spray arm during the drain phase secondary pump flush out used wash water figure chopper wash system maytag chopper dishwasher mf general information new component chopper sump assembly top bottom view chopper motor diagnostic troubleshooting for service technician use only danger electrical shock hazard electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform disconnect power before servicing diagnostic voltage measurement replace all part and panel before ting after performing voltage measurement in death disconnect power before servicing vps aga resulting on failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock voltage measurement safety information when performing live voltage measurement you must do the following verify the control are in the off position so that the appliance doe not start when energized allow enough space to perform the voltage measurement without obstruction keep other people safe distance away from the appliance to prevent potential injury alway use the proper testing equipment after voltage measurement alway disconnect power before servicing important electrostatic discharge esd sensitive electronic esd problem are present everywhere most people begin to feel an esd discharge at approximately it take as little as to destroy damage or weaken the main control assembly the new main control assembly may appear to work well after repair is finished but malfunction may occur at later date due to esd stress use an anti static wrist strap connect wrist strap to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance or touch your finger repeatedly to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance before removing the part from its package touch the anti static bag to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance avoid touching electronic part or terminal contact handle electronic control assembly by edge only when repackaging main control assembly in anti static bag observe above instruction important safety notice for technician only this service data sheet is intended for use by person having electrical electronic and mechanical experience and knowledge at level generally considered acceptable in the appliance repair trade any attempt to repair major appliance may result in personal injury and property damage the manufacturer or seller cannot be responsible troubleshooting for service technician use only service diagnostic cycle note to invoke the diagnostic cycle perform the following while in standby hm press start resume to wake up the control panel press any key in the sequence with no more than second between key press the service diagnostic cycle will start when the door is closed torapid advance interval at time press start resume rapid advancing may skip sensor check as some check require complete interval note while in the diagnostic cycle the start resume feature is turned off for example auto resume after door interrupt and the start resume key become an interval advance key invoking service diagnostic clear all status and last run information from memory and restore default drain and wash motor will pulsate on and off last run cycle and option are returned to default reset owi calibration value to the default value force owi optical water indicator calibration cycle on next customer cycle note calibration cycle may add additional rinse prior to the final rinse to assure clear water and then calibrate the owi during the fill at the beginning of the final rinse mh operating state return to standby upon completing or terminating the service diagnostic cycle reference service diagnostic with error code section for detail turn on all led immediately upon receiving the entry sequence even if the door is open for second as display test turn off all led for second prior to reporting customer error history press hi temp in this interval to clear customer error history if the hi temp key doe not respond the control panel is in sleep mode open and close the door to wake up the control panel and then press hi temp to clear error history owi optical soil sensor check hm check owi sensor for the presence of water during the interval and turn on the clean led in interval if water detected hm check owi for the presence of air during drain interval and turn on the clean led in interval if air detected check owi sense for the presence of bulk soil during pause interval execute apf and turn on clean led in interval if bulk soil detected hi maytag chopper dishwasher thermistor temperature sensor check turn clean led on if thermistor is in its normal temperature range to to turn sanitized led on if fill temperature is above turn on sanitized led in this interval to indicate that the salt level reed switch is closed turn on clean led in this interval to indicate that vent current is detected customer cycle operation to quickly advance through customer cycle invoke the rapid advance mode by pressing high temp heated dry high temp heated dry after starting the cycle then press start resume to advance through cycle interval note rapid advance mode is automatically enabled in the service diagnostic cycle but must be manually invoked in customer cycle diagnostic troubleshooting for service technician use only service diagnostic with error code entry sequence press start resume to wake only authorized technician should perform disconnect power before servicing diagnostic voltage measurement replace all part and panel before ting after performing voltage measurement failure to do so can result in death or disconnect power before servicing electrical shock failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock voltage measurement safety information when performing live voltage measurement you must do the following hm verify the control are in the off position so that the appliance doe not start when energized allow enough space to perform the voltage measurement without obstruction keep other people safe distance away from the appliance to prevent potential injury alway use the proper testing equipment after voltage measurement alway disconnect power before servicing important electrostatic discharge esd sensitive electronic esd problem are present everywhere most people begin to feel an esd discharge at approximately it take as little as to destroy damage or weaken the main control assembly the new main control assembly may appear to work well after repair is finished but malfunction may occur at later date due to esd stress use an anti static wrist strap connect wrist strap to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance or touch your finger repeatedly to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance before removing the part from its package touch the anti static bag to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance avoid touching electronic part or terminal contact handle electronic control assembly by edge only when repackaging main control assembly in anti static bag observe above instruction important safety notice for technician only this service data sheet is intended for use by person having electrical electronic and mechanical experience and knowledge at level generally considered acceptable in the appliance repair trade any attempt to repair major appliance may result in personal injury and property damage the manufacturer or seller cannot be responsible nor assume any liability for injury or damage of any kind arising from the use of this data to rpm hi maytag chopper dishwasher fuse service resistance check small triac load fuse check operation of load during the service diagnostic cycle if any of the triac load work fuse is ok if all triac load fail to work fuse could be open see fuse resistance check fuse resistance check unplug the dishwasher or disconnect power measure resistance of fuse note fuse are on the bottom of the control board but can be checked from the top side see control pinout diagram if resistance is then fuse is ok if resistance is then fuse is open if the fuse is open inspect and check resistance of all load on fuse if any load are open shorted or have evidence of overheating or pinched wire replace load and or repair wire component testing testing dishwasher component from the control before testing any of the component perform the following check the most common cause for misdiagnosed control failure is poor connection therefore disconnecting inspecting and reconnecting wire will be necessary throughout test procedure all test check should be made with vom or dvm having sensitivity of ohm per volt dc or greater check all connection before replacing component looking for broken or loose wire failed terminal or wire not pressed into connector far enough voltage check must be made with all connector attached to the board resistance check must be made with power cord unplugged or power disconnected and with wiring harness or connector disconnected from the control the testing procedure in this section may require the use of needle probe to measure voltage failure to use needle probe will damage the connector testing for service technician use only electronic control board control assembly pinch to release rotate control panel snap lock control in place connector brace to remove pinch arm and rotate ds gix button on bottom of control housing slide into keyhole slot on control panel to support the control meter check of load supply important load must be connected for triac to operate correctly meter check best made at the control of triac fuse ore aos hiore inc oo oem pg omevene pe be au he boe ro aboo rar oon rip bio obanar nln na nian orare alist titi wl tl at fp pto wf utp po cc owl ras wide out ps br bu rd vv br rd yl neutral dc gnd maytag chopper dishwasher hf testing for service technician use only danger electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform diagnostic voltage measurement after performing voltage measurement disconnect power before servicing failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock general theory of operation refer to wiring diagram on page neutral and ac voltage enter the control board at pin respectively ac is converted to dc voltage at the low volt power supply lvp supply include and vdc and dc gnd ref these low voltage supply are used to provide power to the microprocessor and board component control the triac power the sensor buzzer fan motor and energize the ac relay the vdc is vital to the operation of the dishwasher this supply is necessary to operate all vac load in the dishwasher whether they are connected to relay or controlled by triac vdc is generated by the power supply and flow through the door switch when closed to be available to the heater relay wash motor relay and the pilot relay relay coil become energized when the control close the lv drive circuit for specific relay completing pathway which in turn close the relay switch providing ac to the load the pilot relay provide to the remainder of the component that are controlled by the neutral sensed triac in the chopper dishwasher there is one fuse on the control board small triac load fuse if the triac fuse is open all load controlled by triac will not operate note refer to fuse service and diagnostic check on page hi maytag chopper dishwasher power check this test check for incoming and outgoing power to and from the control board this test assume that proper voltage is present at the outlet or direct connect cable test procedure unplug dishwasher or disconnect power remove access panel remove terminal box cover with voltmeter set to ac insert black probe inside white wire screw nut and insert red probe inside black wire screw nut plug in dishwasher or reconnect power if vac is present unplug dishwasher or disconnect power and proceed to step if vac is not present have customer correct power problem at outlet or breaker remove outer door panel remove cover from control board and locate connector with voltmeter set to ac connect black probe to pin and red probe to pin plug in dishwasher or reconnect power if vac is present go to step if vac is not present check for open connection between terminal block and control repair as needed verify dc supply vdc is used to power ic and micro processor on the circuit board as well as provide power to the sensor and fan motor vdc were missing the fan motor and owi optical water indicator would not function to verify with voltmeter set to dc connect the black lead to dc gnd and the red lead to vdc is used to actuate the vac relay and triac on the control if vdc was missing the heater motor and all other load would not turn on to verify witha voltmeter set to dc connect the black lead to dc gnd and the red lead to troubleshooting missing dc supply refer to the wiring diagram on page when troubleshooting the dc supply if vdc or vdc is missing on the control unplug dishwasher or disconnect power and then disconnect all component load from the control relying on the missing or loaded supply plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and check if the dc supply has returned if not replace the control ifit has turn off dishwasher and reconnect one connector at time until the component loading down that supply has been identified unplug dishwasher or disconnect power reassemble all part and panel testing for service technician use only danger electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform diagnostic voltage measurement after performing voltage measurement disconnect power before servicing failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical retest door latch switch disconnect connector from the control board using an ohmmeter measure across pin and with the door closed strike completely in latch mechanism switch closed if ohm or less is measured proceed to step if high resistance is measured when door is closed check for loose connection and repair as needed using an ohmmeter measure across pin and with the door open strike removed from latch mechanism switch open if reading is infinite go to step if reading show continuity or door switch is damaged replace door switch and retest set voltmeter to dc and connect red lead to test pad and black lead to dc gnd on the control board plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and with door open verify that vdc is present across and if vdcis not present replace the control and retest if sprocket to to decrease tension move spring end left to next groove replace assembly draw take up spring so that it rest on regulator stop fig and recheck tension tighten stud set screw fig to adjust needle thread tension this machine is equipped with one cycle dial tension which when set correctly offer barely perceptible tension at to maximum at the end of one complete turn of thumb nut at there should be barely perceptible amount of tension as otherwise difficulty may be experienced when sewing satin stitching and other form of closed ornamental stitching adjustment pull thread through tension disc to test amount of tension on thread at position at this point there should be slight pull on the thread to indicate there is minimum tension which gradually increase with the turning of thumb nut to the right providing full range of tension with one revolution of the thumb nut if the pull is too strong for minimum tension press in dial ji to disen gage pin on nut from dial and reset pin in next hole to left of previous setting repeat this adjust ment until point of no tension is reached then advance one hole to right to give minimum tension at zero position to set needle bar at laser radiation is emitted from the optical pickup len when the unit is turned on do not look directly into the pickup len wy do not use optical instrument to look at the pickup len do not adjust the preset variable resistor on the optical pickup do not disassemble the optical pickup unit if the optical pickup is replaced use the manufacture specified replacement pickup only use of control or adjustment or performance of procedure other than those specified herin may result in hazardous radiation exposure pap wn hi safety precaution this safety precaution is applied only in before servicing unplug the power cord to prevent an electric shock wy when replacing part use only manufacturer recommended component for safety check the condition of the power cord replace if wear or damage is evident after servicing be sure to restore the lead dress insulation barrier insulation paper shield etc before returning the serviced equipment to the customer be sure to make the following insulation resistance test to prevent the customer from being exposed to shock hazard gue gon sf insulation resistance test unplug the power cord and short the two prong of the plug with jumper wire turn on the power switch measure the resistance value with ohmmeter between the jumpered ac plug and each exposed metal cabinet ry part such as screwhead antenna control shaft handle bracket etc equipment with antenna terminal should read between and to all exposed part fig equipment without antenna terminal should read approximately infinity to all exposed part fig note some exposed part may be isolated from the chassis by design these will read infinity antenna terminal exposed exposed metal metal part part ohmmeter ohmmeter fig fig resistance resistance approx oo if the measurement is outside the specified limit there is possibilty of shock hazard the equipment should be repaired and rechecked before it is returned to the customer li accessory ac power supply cord polarized pe for for canada stereo connection cable pe miconnection before making connection be sure that the power of this unit and all other system component is first turned off see the operating instruction of the level repeat button repeat sl li concerning the remote control battery installation ww use of battery operation note open cover xe ne open the cover press gently and slide the cover out insert the battery included with this unit when you need to replace these battery use two um iec or equivalent battery do not mix old and new battery or battery of different type carbon and alkaline etc the average battery life is about one year although the battery life vary depending on how often the device is used you should gener ally replace the battery about once year replace the battery if command from the remote control transmit ter do not operate the unit even when the transmitter is held close to the front panel never subject battery to excessive heat or flame do not attempt to disassemble them and be sure they are not short circuited if you will not be using the remote control for long period of time remove the battery and store in cool dark place aim the remote control transmission window toward the cd player sensor avoid any obstacle pie saale aay cd player cre meter ft use the remote control with degree angle of the cd player maximum distance is within meter ft directly facing toward the cd player insert battery included close cover be sure to insert battery so that their positive and negative polarity match the indication in the compartment batter ies installed incorrectly may leak and cause damage refer to use of battery below for additional information close the cover if battery leak remove all battery and dispose of them properly thoroughly clean the battery compartment before inserting new bat tery if the leaking electrolyte come in contact with skin or clothe flush with water immediately keep battery out of reach of child do not use rechargeable type nickel cadmium battery do not attempt to recharge alkaline or carbon battery alway remove old weak or worn out battery and dispose of them properly do not dispose of used battery in household rubbish avoid dust be sure the transmission window and the cd player sensor are free from dust excessive dust might prevent reception if direct sunlight or another strong light source strike the remote control signal sensor part of the cd player it may impair the remote control operation if there is problem place the cd player away from the light source if this unit is installed in rack with glass door the glass door thickness or color might make it necessary to use the remote control transmitter shorter distance from the unit sl li basic operation procedure open close press power to switch on the power if there is disc in the disc tray play will start from the first track press open close to open the disc tray and insert disc also available from the remote control label must face upward before attempting to use the remote control transmitter to open the disc tray make sure that there are no obstruction in front of the unit press open close again to close the disc tray also available from the remote control total number of track total playing time light when the total number of track exceed the total playing time displayed include the silent section between track for this reason it may be few second longer than the playing time indicated on the disc you can skip this step and press play the disc tray will be automatically closed and then play will start from the first track open close auto phone phowe level press play also available from the remote control index number for disc without index number will be continuously displayed track number in play elapsed time play indicator the unit stop automatically when the last track finish playing li handling precaution for traverse deck the laser diode in the traverse deck optical pickup may break down due to potential difference caused by static electricity of clothe or human body so be careful of electrostatic breakdown during repair of the traverse deck optical pickup handling of traverse deck optical pickup do not subject the traverse deck optical pickup to static electricity as it is extremely sensitive to electrical shock variable resistor do not turn optical pickup sl to prevent the breakdown of the laser diode an antistatic shorting pin is inserted into the flexible board fpc board when removing or connecting the short pin finish the job in as short time as possible take care not to apply excessive stress to the flexible board fpc board do not turn the variable resistor laser power adjustment it has already been adjusted len do not touch fpc board handle it carefully be sure to short this position clip shorting pin use the shorting pin or clip grounding for electrostatic breakdown prevention human body grounding use the anti static wrist strap to discharge the static electricity from your body work table grounding put conductive material sheet or steel sheet on the area where the optical pickup is placed and wrist strap anti static bracelet ground the sheet caution the static electricity of your clothe will not be grounded through the wrist strap so take care not to let your clothe touch the traverse deck optical cr sk pickup iron plate or some metal to conduct electricity fingertip start key lectric start safety instruction this mower meet or exceed cpsc blade safety requirement fo walk behind rotary mower and the specification of the american national standard institute however im proper use or maintenance by the operator or owner can result in injury to reduce the potential for injury comply with these safety instruction and alway pay attention to the safety alert symbol failure to comply with the instruction may result in personal injury before operating read this manual carefully before operating the mower become fam iliar with the control and proper use of the mower never allow child to operate the mower or adult to operate it ithout proper instruction keep everyone especially child and pet away fro the area of ra tio thoroughly inspect area where mower will be used and remove stick stone wire and debris wear long pant and substantial shoe do not operate mower while wearing open sandal or when barefoot check fuel level before starting engine do not fill fuel tank indoor when the engine is running or until engine cool for several minute after running wipe up spilled gaso line before starting engine keep all guard shield and safety device tn place repair or replace defective or dam aged part traction drive and blade are designed to stop when control bar is released assure control and brake function property before each use of mower while operating do not run engine indoor alway maintain secure footing keep firm grip on the handle and walk never run never operate mower in wet grass mow only in daylight or in mow across the face of slope never up and down use extreme caution when changing direction on slope not mow excessively steep slope alway wear safety glass or eye shield during operation to protect eye from foreign ob ject that may be thrown from the machine keep face hand and foot away from the mower hous ing and cutter blade when the engine is running stay behind the handle until the engine stop and keep clear of discharge opening at all time when mulching keep grass bag in place and assure chute door is closed when bag ging grass stop engine and assure chute door is closed before the bag is removed and emptied shut engine ff and wait for all moving part to stop before unclogging chute since the blade rotate for few second after the blade control is released stay behind the handle until all moving part stop after striking foreign object or if mower vibrate abnormally stop engine and remove ire from spark plug check ow er fo damage and make all repair before using mower again stop the engine before adjusting the height of cut stop the blade when crossing gravel driveway road or sidewalk stop engine before leaving the operator position behind the handle disconnect wire from spark plug if mower ill be tte do not touch engine while it is running or shortly after it is stopped because engine will be hot enough to cause burn maintenance perform only those maintenance instruc tion described in this manual if ajor repair the toro company rev price safety instruction are ever needed or if assistance is desired contact an authorized toro service dealer before mower is cleaned inspected ser viced or adjusted stop engine and disconnect high tension wire from spark plug keep wire away from plug to prevent accidental starting to assure the mower is in safe operating condition frequently check and keep all nut bolt and screw tight assure blade nut are tightened to ft lb to reduce fire hazard keep engine free of excessive grease grass leaf and accumula tion of dirt check grass catcher bag frequently for wear or deterioration replace with new bag for your protection allow engine to cool before storing mower in any enclosure do not store mower near any open flame or where gasoline fume may be ignited by spark do not overspeed the engine by changing governor setting at the time of manufacture the mower conformed to the safety standard in effect for rotary mower to assure optimum perform ance and continued safety certification of the mower use genuine toro replacement part and accessory replacement part and ac cessory made by other manufacturer may result in non conformance with the safety standard and that could be dangerous safety decal safety decal and instruction are easily visible to the operator and are located near any area of potential danger replace any decal that is damaged caution not operate unlessbag is in place or rear door is closed door powtiow io io swing door back and forth to close on top of rear discharge warning xu vj gimumt oaolib when ready to start engine figure before operating fill crankcase with oil initially crankcase must be filled with ounce of sae oil use any high quality detergent oil having the american petroleum in stitute api service classification ms sc sd seorsf before each use assure oil level is in safe range add oil if level is low position mower on level surface and clean around oil dipstick remove dipstick by rotating cap counter clockwise turn wipe dipstick and insert it into filler neck rotate cap clockwise turn then remove dipstick and check level of oil fig if level is low add only enough oil to raise level to full mark on dipstick do not fill above full mark because engine could be dam aged when started insert dipstick into filler neck and rotate cap clockwise turn to lock note check oil level each time mower is used or after every operating hour initially change oil after the first hour of operation thereafter change oil after every hour of operation more frequent oil change are required in dusty or dirty condition fill fuel tank with gasoline note the toro company strongly recommend the use of fresh clean unleaded regular grade gasoline in toro gasoline powered prod uct unleaded gasoline burn cleaner extend engine life and promote good starting by re ducing the build up of combustion chamber de posit leaded gasoline can be used if un leaded is not available clean around fuel tank cap and remove cap from tank fig using unleaded gasoline fill fuel tank to within inch fro to tank install fuel tank cap and wipe up any spilled gasoline important do not mix oil with the gasoline never use thanol asoline ta in in methanol gasohol containing more than ethanol gasoline additive premium gaso line or white gas because engine fuel system damage could result danger gasoline is extremely flammable and explosive under certain condition do not smoke when handling fuel and keep fuel far away from open flame and spark never buy more than day supply of gasoline store gasoline in an approved container and keep it out of the reach of child refuel outdoor only when engine is cold fill tank to within mm from its top this space is for expansion of fuel use funnel or spout to prevent spilling wipe up any spilled gas operating instruction ln mowing versatility rear bag bag lush grass in the spring to help keep your lawn free of thatch forming clipping mulch in summer when grass grow slower and is drier mulch by closing chute door fig for convenience and protection keep grass bag on mower side discharge disperse clipping with optional rear mounted side discharge chute which install in second between the handle design let you trim on both side fig figure shred leaf in fall optional leaf shredder fig reduce leaf to fraction of their original size while you bag minimiz emptying stop and number of trash bag used for disposal remove thatch as spring tine on optional toro dethatcher scratch and vibrate through the grass thatch is loosened and conveniently vacuumed into the bag fig operating tip check oil level maintain oil level in safe range as shown on dipstick before each mowing assureengine brake self propelled drive and control bar function properly when control bar is re leased blade and self propelled drive are de signed to stop cutting and bagging best result are achieved when engine is runn in at maximum speed and only about of the grass blade is cut if long grass must be cut use highest eight of cut setting fo first mowing then recut the grass at more normal setting if too long of grass is cut mower may plug and cause engine to stall sharp blade begin each cutting season with sharp blade periodically file down nick operating instruction starting stopping and self propelling control key switch model throttle ground speed control and blade self propelled control bar are on upper handle fig fingertip starter is on top of engine push high tension wire onto spark plug and move ground speed control to fig ic starting bag keep bag installed when bagging or mulching stop engine and wait for all part to stop assure chute door handle is fully forward and pin is engaged in catch installing bag slide hole in bag frame onto retaining collar on housing fig and set rear of bag frame onto lower handle pull chute door handle forward until pin clear catch and move handle rearward until pin lock in bag notch fig chute door in mower housing is now open it may be necessary to push down on top right of bag door to release handle locking pin from catch caution except when side discharge attachment is used never open chute door on mower when engine is running unless grass bag is properly installed because grass clip ping and other debris discharge with enough force to cause injury the grass bag material ill catch and contain the majority of foreign object such as small stone and other similar debris the bag material however is subject to normal wear and deteriora tion so check the bag frequently and if it is defective install new genuine toro replacement bag that has this or similar warning emptying bag release control bar and wait for blade to stop move chute door handle forward to engage the locking pin with catch fig grasp handle at front and rear of bag and lift bag off mower for protection alway keep hand and foot away from mower housing because engine is running gradually tip bag forward to empty clipping caution if chute door cannot be closed because grass clipping clog die charge area gently move chute door handle back and forth until door can be closed completely do not force chute door closed because door or handle may be damaged if door still cannot be lo to in and rem ove obstruction to reinstall bag repeat step and adjusting height of cut five letter cast into the housing represent each graduated increment fig stop the engine for easier adjustment lift housing up so wheel is off ground squeeze adjusting lever toward wheel and move them to desired setting fig assure pin on adjusting lever engage notch in housing adjust all wheel to the same setting note front wheel can be adjusted to inch move adjusting lever past and release pin into hole in housing maintenance caution pull ire ff spark plug before per forming adjustment or maintenance servicing air cleaner normally clean air cleaner after every operating hour more frequent cleaning is required when mower is operated in dusty or dirty condition stop engine and remove key from switch or pull wire off spark plug push in locking tab and lift off air cleaner cover clean cover thoroughly fig if top of foam element is dirty remove it from air cleaner body fig clean thoroughly wash foam element in solution of liquid soap and warm water squeeze to remove dirt but do not twist because foam may tear dry by wrapping in clean rag squeeze rag and foam element to dry saturate element with teaspoon ml of sae engine oil squeeze element to remove excess oil and to distribute oil thoroughly damp element is desirable reinstall foam element and air cleaner cover important do not operate engine without air cleaner element because extreme engine wear and damage will likely result replacing spark plug use an ngk spark plug or equivalent correct air gap is mm remove plug after every operating hour and check its condition stop engine and pull wire off spark plug clean around spark plug and remove plug from cylinder head important replace cracked fouled or dirty spark plug do not sand blast scrape or clean electrode because engine damage could result from grit entering cylinder set air gap at mm fig install correctly gapped spark plug and gasket seal tighten plug firmly to ft lb draining gasoline stop engine remove key from switch or pull wire off spark plug remove cap from fuel tank and use pump type syphon to drain fuel into clean gas can note this is the only procedure recommended for draining fuel adjusting throttle throttle control adjustment may be required if engine doe not start or stop whenever new throttle control cable is installed throttle must be adjusted stop engine and remove key from switch or pull wire off spark plug close door on mower housing and remove grass bag move throttle control to fast loosen cable clamp screw until throttle ca ble slide fig align hole in throttle lever and throttle bracket pull throttle cable to remove any slack and tighten cable clamp screw to lock adjustment in place maintenance changing crankcase oil change oil after the first operating hour and after every hour thereafter since warm oil drain better and carry more contaminant than cold oil run engine for minute or so before draining oil stop engine and remove key from switch or pull wire off spark plug remove grass bag drain gasoline from fuel tank refer to draining carefully examine blade for sharpness and wear especially caution if blade is allowed to wear slot may form near the sail eventually piece of blade may break off resulting in serious injury to yourself or bystander where flat and curved part meet fig since sand and abrasive material can wear away the metal that connect the flat and curved part of the blade check blade before using the mower jf slot or wear is noticed fig replace blade refer to step removing blade grasp end of blade using rag or thickly padded glove remove blade nut anti scalp cup and blade fig note for optimum performance install new blade before cutting season begin during the year file down small nick to maintain the cutting edge sharpening blade using file sharpen top side of blade and maintain original cutting angle fig the blade will remain balanced if same amount of material is re moved from both cutting edge important check balance of blade by putting it on blade balancer an inexpensive balancer can be purchased at hardware store balanced blade ill stay in horizontal posi tion and an unbalanced blade will settle to the heavy side if blade is not balanced file more metal off cutting edge on heavy end of blade install sharp balanced blade and anti scalp cup with blade nut sail part of blade must point toward top of mower housing to assure correct installation tighten the blade nut to ft lb lubrication after every operating hour or when season end front and rear wheel must be lubricated apply or drop of light oil on inside of front wheel bolt spin wheel to distribute oil into bushing wipe up excess oil move rear wheel height of cut lever to setting wipe grease fitting with clean rag fig install grease gun onto fitting and gently apply pump of multi purpose lithium base grease excess pressure may damage seal maintenance adjusting blade brake henever new blade brake cable assembly is installed an adjustm ent is required stop engine and remove key from switch or pull wire off spark plug loosen cable clam screw un til brakecable co it slide fig pull cable to remove slack but do not put tension on spring tighten screw to lock adjustm ent in place check control bar operation control bar should be checked periodically at the beginning of each mowing season as minimum to assure the blade brake clutch bbc system is operating properly start engine refer to starting page push the control bar down to the lowest posi tion fig this cock the mechanism prior to engaging the blade lift the control bar to the normal blade en gaged position about below the handle fig the fan noise and bag inflation indicate that the mower blade is engaged and rotating release the control bar bang should be heard and the blade should stop within second if the blade doe not stop within second when the control bar is released stop using it until it has been serviced by an authorized toro service dealer if your unit stop with the required second the bbc system is operating properly however to detect the bbc system deterioration before it affect normal operation proceed to step start engine if not already running push the control bar down to its lowest posi tion cocking the mechanism fig slowly lift the control bar to point where the bag just begin to inflate about below the han dle this indicate that the mower blade is en gaged and has begun to rotate note this intermediate position is not the nat ural operating position of the control bar how ever it has proven to be useful in early detection of latent bbc sticking which could create an un safe condition later in the life of the mower release the control bar note if bang is heard and the bag immedi ately deflate the control bar had been raised too far repeat step but do not raise the han dle as high in step maintenance if the control bar hang up and the blade doe not quickly stop it indicate possible deterioration has set in which could result in an unsafe oper ating condition have mower inspected by an authorized toro service dealer caution if the control bar hang up and the blade doe not stop quickly you should stop the blade before shutting the engine off this can be done by raising the control bar to the normal operating position and releasing it adjusting ground speed control for neutral whenever new ground speed control cable is installed or if mower drive when ground speed control is in an adjustment is repair manual please contact the toro company service department lyndale avenue south minneapolis mn general service safety instruction use extreme care when handling any gaso line and oil that may be in the engine gasoline and oil are flammable and should not be exposed to flame or spark do not smoke while handling any fuel or oil drain and or store gasoline and oil only in approved container wipe up any spilled gasoline or oil do not run an engine in confined area ith adequate ve tila tio exhaust fume are hazardous and could possibly be deadly disconnect and ground the spark plug high tension wire before perform ing any engine service this will prevent accidental starting of the engine if the engine must be kept running to perform maintenance or making adjust ment keep clear of the pto shaft cutting blade and other moving part do not overspeed the engine by changing the governor setting maximum engine speed with no load is listed in the engine specification on page the engine must be stopped before check ing the oil or adding oil to the crankcase do not touch the engine uffler or guard while the engine is running or soon after it has stopped these area could be hot enough to cause burn model and serial number each toro ohv engine is equipped with two number four dig it model num ber and six digit serial number the number are located on the uffler side of the blower the consumer air cleaner should be serviced every hour of engine use the commercial air cleaner is of two part design the foam precleaner should be serviced every hour and the paper element every hour more frequent service for both air cleaner may be required in dusty or dirty condition consumer ir cleaner maintenance stop the engine and pull the high tension wire off the spark plug clean loose debris from the air cleaner figure body push in the locking tab and lift off the air cleaner cover see fig clean the cover figure remove the foam element see fig wash the element in soap and water squeeze to remove dirt do not twist as the element may tear dry the element and saturate it with teaspoon of sae engine oil squeeze the element to remove excess oil reinstall the air cleaner element and cover commercial ir cleaner maintenance follow step and in consumer air cleaner maintenance loosen and remove the two cover knob see fig lift the cover away from the body remove the air cleaner element paper and foam see fig clean the foam element with soap and water do not oil this element clean the paper element with ir filter element cleaner toro part number caution this com pound contain sodium metasilicate which may cause burn and is harmful if swallowed keep this chem ical out of the reach of child follow the manufacturer instruction light accum ulation of dust may be re moved by blowing compressed air from the inside to the outside of the dry element caution alway wear safety glass when working with compressed air this proce dure should be used if the element must be im ediately put back in service because washed element must be dried before use make sure the compressed air is at kpa psi or less hold the air nozzle at least inch away from the element fter cleaning examine the paper element for hole or leak by holding light behind the element slowly turn the element and look through the paper for defect fc vlt tdo mqt figure changing oil change oil after the first hour of use and every hour thereafter run the engine to warm the oil before draining stop the engine and pull the high tension wire off the spark plug drain gasoline from the fuel tank refer to fuel system on page raise the left side of the mower at least inch and remove the drain plug use mm socket or wrench insert an oil drain tube part number over the drain opening and lower the mower raise the right side of the mower until all the oil has flowed into drain pan see fig figure install the drain plug and fill the crankcase with liter oz of sae weight oil sae is an acceptable substitute make sure the oil has service classification of ms sc sd se or sf do not overfill the engine with oil check th ip tic to ke sureth eo il level is between the full and add mark see fig figure spark plug remove the spark plug after every operating hour and check its condition use an ngk spark plug or its equivalent the air gap is mm in stop the engine and remove the spark plug wire clean the area around the spark plug and remove the plug replace cracked fouled or dirty spark plug do not sandblast scrape or clean electrode because engine damage could result from grit entering the cylinder set the air gap to mm in see fig install the plug and tighten it to kg ft lbs valve lash valve lash is preset at the factory to mm in in there is no regular maintenance or adjustment of the valve lash required unless the head has been removed or th service in the valve tra in has been performed see valve lash adjustm ent on page for complete valve setting instruction governor and throttle linkage the governor is set to operate the engine at rpm to adjust the governor loosen the governor arm locknut turn the governor shaft fully clockwise move the governor arm fully to the right tighten the governor arm locknut see fig it is not necessary to remove the oil fill tube to make this adjustment the engine should operate at rpm when set on fast to adjust the throttle stop the engine and remove the spark plug wire loosen throttle cable clamp fl align the hole in the throttle bracket and throttle lever insert tight fitting pin in drill bit move the throttle control on the handle to fast and tighten the cable clamp screw back out the upper screw until only few thread remain in the throttle lever install the plug wire and start the engine using only the lower screw adjust the engine speed to rpm now turn the upper screw until it just barely contact the throttle levertang maximum gap of in is acceptable remove the alignm ent pin on bbc engine set the throttle control on the handle in the stop position bend the grounding tang until it just contact the throttle lever see fig governor and throttle adjustment should be made on an as needed basis only figure ignition the ohv engine use solid state ignition system with tim ing fixed at degree btdc degree at rpm ignition tim ing is not adjustable however ig itio tim in may change if blade impact has occurred and the key has sheared the engine may continue to run with degradation in per formance under these condition the tim ing will advance making starting and running ifficult the coil to irgap isset at mm in fuel system the fuel tank has capacity of oz and should be drained of fuel before changing oil and at the end of each season to drain the fuel pull off the spark plug wire use pump or syphon to transfer the gasoline into clean gasoline container caution gasoline is highly flammable handle it carefully do not smoke or allow any open flame or spark while handling gasoline caution gasoline is highly flammable han dle it carefully do not smoke or allow any open flame or spark while handling gasoline the fuel system is equipped with an micron inline fuel filter see fig if poor running occur this filte should be exam ined for contam ination there is no in tank filte on this engine the fuel tank cap should be rio ic lly examined for proper venting the cap is fitted with asealing disc but cannot be disassembled see fig il figure figure carburetor the ohv engine is equipped with mikuni bv float bowl type carburetor the standard main jet is number this number is stam ped in the base of the jet the only adjustment on the carburetor is the pilot system pilot screw the pilot screw should be set one turn open see fig this will have the most effect at idle or low speed with no load on the engine figure the carburetor on the model usesa brass fuel inlet seat not replaceable and an inlet needle with viton tip the needle is replace able the carburetor on the model usesa viton fuel inlet seat not replaceable and an figure storage to insure long engine life and easy engine starting it is im portant to follow these storage procedure at the end of each season drain system can be checked for spark intensity using spark tester part number pull off the plug connector and remove the spark plug inspect the plug for wear carbon deposit and damage replace the plug if damaged burnt or fouled check for the correct plug gap and adjust if necessary to mm in in attach the spark tester and set the tester gap to mm in turn counter clockwise from the closed position pull the starter and observe for spark if the spark is evident the ignition system is operating properly if no spark is evident proceed to testing kill switch operation testing ill switch operation there are two ignition kill system one for zone start and one for bbc and commercial appli cation both system depend on lever contacting grounding tang to ground the ignition and stop the engine bbc and commercial kill system make sure the kill wire is properly con nected to the grounding tang on the throttle control see fig figure when the throttle control is in the run position the throttle lever should move away from the ground tang see fig figure in this position the ground tang should not have continuity to ground this can be tested by disconnecting the kill wire and testing the tang with volt ohm meter place the volt ohm eter on with one probe on the tang and one probe on ground there sh ould be reading of in fin ity continuity in the ff position the throttle lever should contact the ground tang this ill ground the ignition and stop the engine see fig figure zone start application make sure the engine kill wire is connected to the ground tang see fig removal ignition drain the
