for the ohv electric start iring schematic testing electric starter the starter otor fail to turn check the battery make sure it is connected and fu lly charged if the motor still doe not turn go to step two remove the spark plug wire and disconnect the starter otor plug apply volt direct to the starter otor terminal see fig figure if the otor turn check the starter switch use volt ohm meter there should be continuity through the switch in the start mode check the battery ground to the engine there should be continuity between the negative battery terminal and the body of the engine check the wire connector at the battery and starter motor if the otor doe not turn verify that the engine is not seized if the engine is free go to step replace the otor as the internal com ponent are not replaceable testing alternator atte ry doe not charge rin ow er operation check battery connection and alternator connection to make sure they are complete and secure check the connection point at the battery alternator wire key switch and all ground connection measure the alternator output with the engine at rpm with the battery connected alternator output should be volt dc the voltage can be measured between the red wire on the key switch and the black ground wire if the measured voltage is volt or less it indicate the alternator may not be charg ing go to step four complete resistance test on the alternator coil this test may be completed ithout removing the coil from the engine all measurement are taken between the out put wire and ground disconnect the wire for these test for clarity the coil has been removed in the follow ing figure lte rn to rco il isequipped ith io assembly that ill rectify the alternator output see fig reverse the lead and complete the test the measurement is ohm the meter was on the ohm scale and the reading is or ohm see fig figure check the diode and coil assembly with volt ohm eter the meter in fig indicate resistance of infinity when numeral one appear to the left of the decimal point the measurement should be infinity when the positive ro be fth meter is placed on the output wire see fig the negative probe is connected to the accomplished ithout disassembly of the starter first take the starter off the engine if the pulley spring has not be detensioned ithdraw about one foot of rope and while holding the pulley in place unwind the remaining rope on the pulley slow ly release the rope until the pulley spring fu lly relax important do not turn the pulley any further in the clockwise direction or the spring will unhook from the center hub if the spring un hook the pulley ill turn freely in the counter clockw ise ire ctio ith an increase in spring tension to correct the pulley must be removed and the spring re hooked pulley extend about two foot of rope hold the pulley in place and take two additional rap of rope aro un the lle in counter clockw ise direction this will pre load the spring re install the recoil starter spring replacement llo step one th ro nine unde recoil starter removal and disassembly on page re fu lly remove the broken spring to prevent rapid expansion of the spring caution wear safety glass and use extreme care to prevent injury the replacement spring is provided with wire retainer to hold it together place the spring in the pulley and release it from its retainer the spring should be installed in the pulley as shown in fig if the spring has expanded outside the pulley it can be reinstalled by first placing the hook end into the pulley see fig wind the spring into the pulley in counter clockwise direction from the outside until the spring is fully retained place the pulley in the recoil housing and turn it slowly in counter clockwise direc tion with slight down pressure if bang is heard and the bag im medi ately deflate the control bar has been raised too high repeat step and but do not raise the handle as high as in step if the control bar return to the lowest position and the bag quickly deflate when the control bar is released the unit is operating properly if the control bar doe not fall or the blade doe not uickly stop it indicate the system could be experi encing ear deterioration hich could result in an unsafe operating condition use care in operating the ow er and bbc system and contact an utho rized toro service dealer for an inspection of
